Vulnerability localization performance consistently improved with increasing LLM scale, with larger models exhibiting higher confidence in fault localization.
LLMAO is the first fault localization technique trained using a language model architecture that can detect security vulnerabilities at the code line level.
conclusions_zh: LLMAO在Top-1结果上比最先进的机器学习故障定位基线提升了2.3%至54.4%，Top-5结果提升了14.4%至35.6%。
漏洞定位性能随LLM规模的增大而持续提升，更大的模型在故障定位中表现出更高的置信度。
LLMAO是首个使用语言模型架构训练的、能够在代码行级别检测安全漏洞的故障定位技术。

title: Evaluating the Impact of Conventional Code Analysis Against Large Language Models in API Vulnerability Detection
authors: Erol Yildirim, Ahmed Demir
abstract: In the rapidly changing world of digital technologies, application programming interfaces (APIs) have become extremely important to allow different software applications to communicate with each other. This communication has greatly enhanced the capabilities and functionality of web applications. This shift towards using more APIs in software development marks a major change in how digital services connect with each other. However, this progress also brings certain security concerns. The increasing reliance on APIs underscores the importance of employing tools that allow early detection and remediation of security vulnerabilities. In this paper, we detail a study that engaged 10 static code analysers and four popular Large Language Models (LLMs), each queried with two unique prompts. Our focus was on assessing their ability to detect a compilation of 40 API vulnerabilities in the source code, specifically selected to represent each category within the OWASP Top 10 API Security Risks. Our results revealed significant variations in the performance of these tools. ChatGPT 4 emerged as the most effective LLM, with a detection rate of 62.5% for the first prompt and 42.5% for the second prompt. In contrast, LLaMA 2 showed the lowest effectiveness in both prompts. Meanwhile, static code analyser results showed a generally low detection rate of API vulnerabilities. Snyk led the group with a 25% detection rate, while several analysers such as pylint, Pyre, and Trivy did not detect any vulnerabilities. These findings indicate that while static code analysers are valuable in certain contexts, their effectiveness remains lower than LLMs when appropriately prompted.
summary: Compares 10 static code analyzers and 4 LLMs on 40 API vulnerabilities covering OWASP Top 10 API risks, with ChatGPT-4 achieving 62.5% detection rate versus Snyk's 25%.
summary_zh: 比较10个静态代码分析器和4个LLM在40个涵盖OWASP Top 10 API风险的API漏洞上的表现，ChatGPT-4检测率62.5%，而Snyk为25%。
abstract_zh: 在快速变化的数字技术世界中，应用程序编程接口（API）变得极其重要，它允许不同的软件应用之间进行通信。这种通信极大地增强了Web应用的功能和特性。这种在软件开发中更多使用API的转变标志着数字服务互连方式的重大变化。然而，这种进步也带来了某些安全问题。对API日益增长的依赖突出了采用能够早期检测和修复安全漏洞的工具的重要性。在本文中，我们详细介绍了一项研究，该研究使用了10个静态代码分析器和4个流行的大型语言模型（LLM），每个LLM使用两个不同的提示进行查询。我们的重点是评估它们检测源代码中40个API漏洞的能力，这些漏洞特别选择以代表OWASP Top 10 API安全风险中的每个类别。我们的结果显示这些工具的性能存在显著差异。ChatGPT 4是最有效的LLM，第一个提示的检测率为62.5%，第二个提示为42.5%。相比之下，LLaMA 2在两个提示中都表现出最低的有效性。同时，静态代码分析器的结果显示API漏洞检测率普遍较低。Snyk以25%的检测率领先，而pylint、Pyre和Trivy等分析器未检测到任何漏洞。这些发现表明，虽然静态代码分析器在某些上下文中有价值，但当适当提示时，其有效性仍低于LLM。
contributions: Compares 10 static code analyzers and 4 LLMs on API vulnerability detection across OWASP Top 10 API Security Risks.
Curates a set of 40 API vulnerabilities representing each OWASP Top 10 API Security Risk category.
Finds ChatGPT 4 achieves 62.5% detection rate with first prompt and 42.5% with second prompt, the best among LLMs.
Shows LLaMA 2 has lowest effectiveness among tested LLMs for API vulnerability detection.
Demonstrates static analyzers have generally lower detection rates (Snyk best at 25%) than appropriately prompted LLMs.
contributions_zh: 比较10个静态代码分析器和4个LLM在OWASP Top 10 API安全风险中的API漏洞检测能力。
策划了代表每个OWASP Top 10 API安全风险类别的40个API漏洞集合。
发现ChatGPT 4在第一个提示下检测率达62.5%，第二个提示下为42.5%，是LLM中最优的。
展示LLaMA 2在测试的LLM中API漏洞检测效果最低。
证明静态分析器的检测率普遍低于适当提示的LLM（Snyk最高为25%）。

experiment.key_results: ChatGPT 4 achieved 62.5% detection rate (prompt 1) and 42.5% (prompt 2); Snyk best static analyzer at 25%; LLaMA 2 lowest LLM effectiveness

research_questions: How do LLMs compare to static code analyzers in detecting API vulnerabilities across OWASP Top 10 API Security Risks?
How does prompt design affect LLM performance in API vulnerability detection?
Which specific LLMs and static analyzers are most and least effective for API vulnerability detection?
research_questions_zh: 在检测OWASP Top 10 API安全风险中的API漏洞时，LLM与静态代码分析器的表现如何比较？
提示设计如何影响LLM在API漏洞检测中的表现？
哪些特定的LLM和静态分析器在API漏洞检测中最有效和最无效？
conclusions: ChatGPT 4 was the most effective LLM, achieving a detection rate of 62.5% with the first prompt and 42.5% with the second; LLaMA 2 performed worst across both prompts.
Static code analyzers generally exhibited low API vulnerability detection rates, with Snyk leading at 25%, while pylint, Pyre, and Trivy failed to detect any vulnerabilities.
With appropriate prompting, LLMs significantly outperformed static code analyzers in vulnerability detection, although prompt design had a substantial impact on results.
conclusions_zh: ChatGPT 4是最有效的LLM，第一个提示的检测率为62.5%，第二个提示为42.5%；LLaMA 2在两个提示中效果最差。
静态代码分析器的API漏洞检测率普遍较低，Snyk以25%领先，而pylint、Pyre和Trivy未检测到任何漏洞。
在适当提示下，LLM的漏洞检测效果显著优于静态代码分析器，但提示设计对结果有显著影响。

title: Multitask-based evaluation of open-source llm on software vulnerability
authors: Xin Yin, Chao Ni, Shaohua Wang
abstract: This paper proposes a pipeline for quantitatively evaluating interactive Large Language Models (LLMs) using publicly available datasets. We carry out an extensive technical evaluation of LLMs using Big-Vul covering four different common software vulnerability tasks. This evaluation assesses the multi-tasking capabilities of LLMs based on this dataset. We find that the existing state-of-the-art approaches and pre-trained Language Models (LMs) are generally superior to LLMs in software vulnerability detection. However, in software vulnerability assessment and location, certain LLMs (e.g., CodeLlama and WizardCoder) have demonstrated superior performance compared to pre-trained LMs, and providing more contextual information can enhance the vulnerability assessment capabilities of LLMs. Moreover, LLMs exhibit strong vulnerability description capabilities, but their tendency to produce excessive output significantly weakens their performance compared to pre-trained LMs. Overall, though LLMs perform well in some aspects, they still need improvement in understanding the subtle differences in code vulnerabilities and the ability to describe vulnerabilities to fully realize their potential. Our evaluation pipeline provides valuable insights into the capabilities of LLMs in handling software vulnerabilities.
summary: Evaluates open-source LLMs (CodeLlama, WizardCoder) on four vulnerability tasks using Big-Vul dataset, finding LLMs excel at assessment and location but lag in detection.
summary_zh: 使用Big-Vul数据集评估开源LLM（CodeLlama、WizardCoder）在四项漏洞任务上的表现，发现LLM在评估和定位方面表现优异但在检测方面落后。
abstract_zh: 本文提出了一个使用公开可用数据集定量评估交互式大型语言模型（LLM）的管道。我们使用Big-Vul对LLM进行了广泛的技术评估，涵盖四种不同的常见软件漏洞任务。此评估基于该数据集评估了LLM的多任务能力。我们发现，现有的最先进方法和预训练语言模型（LM）在软件漏洞检测方面通常优于LLM。然而，在软件漏洞评估和定位方面，某些LLM（如CodeLlama和WizardCoder）已展示出优于预训练LM的表现，且提供更多上下文信息可以增强LLM的漏洞评估能力。此外，LLM展现出强大的漏洞描述能力，但其产生过多输出的倾向显著削弱了其与预训练LM相比的性能。总体而言，虽然LLM在某些方面表现良好，但在理解代码漏洞的细微差异和描述漏洞的能力方面仍需改进，以充分发挥其潜力。我们的评估管道为LLM处理软件漏洞的能力提供了有价值的见解。
contributions: Proposes a pipeline for quantitatively evaluating interactive LLMs on software vulnerability tasks using public datasets.
Evaluates LLMs on four vulnerability tasks: detection, assessment, location, and description using Big-Vul dataset.
Finds state-of-the-art approaches and pre-trained LMs are generally superior to LLMs in vulnerability detection.
Shows CodeLlama and WizardCoder demonstrate superior performance in vulnerability assessment and location compared to pre-trained LMs.
Reveals LLMs have strong vulnerability description capabilities but excessive output weakens performance compared to pre-trained LMs.
contributions_zh: 提出一个使用公开数据集定量评估交互式LLM在软件漏洞任务上的管道。
使用Big-Vul数据集评估LLM在四项漏洞任务上的表现：检测、评估、定位和描述。
发现最先进的方法和预训练语言模型在漏洞检测方面通常优于LLM。
展示CodeLlama和WizardCoder在漏洞评估和定位方面的表现优于预训练语言模型。
揭示LLM具有较强的漏洞描述能力，但过多输出削弱了其相对于预训练语言模型的性能。

experiment.key_results: Pre-trained LMs superior to LLMs in detection; CodeLlama and WizardCoder outperform pre-trained LMs in vulnerability assessment and location

research_questions: How do open-source LLMs perform across four software vulnerability tasks: detection, assessment, location, and description?
Do pre-trained language models outperform interactive LLMs in all vulnerability-related tasks?
How does providing additional contextual information affect LLMs' vulnerability assessment capabilities?
research_questions_zh: 开源LLM在软件漏洞检测、评估、定位和描述四个任务中的表现如何？
预训练语言模型在所有漏洞相关任务中是否都优于交互式LLM？
提供额外的上下文信息如何影响LLM的漏洞评估能力？
conclusions: Existing state-of-the-art methods and pre-trained language models generally outperform LLMs on vulnerability detection tasks.

CodeLlama and WizardCoder outperformed pre-trained language models in vulnerability assessment and localization, and providing more contextual information enhanced the assessment capabilities of LLMs.
LLMs exhibit strong vulnerability description capabilities, but their tendency toward excessive output significantly diminishes their performance compared to pre-trained language models.
conclusions_zh: 现有最先进方法和预训练语言模型在漏洞检测任务中总体上优于LLM。
CodeLlama和WizardCoder在漏洞评估和定位方面优于预训练语言模型，提供更多上下文信息能增强LLM的评估能力。
LLM具有较强的漏洞描述能力，但其过度输出的倾向显著削弱了与预训练语言模型相比的表现。

title: LLM-Fuzzer: Scaling Assessment of Large Language Model Jailbreaks
authors: Jiahao Yu, Xingwei Lin, Zheng Yu, Xinyu Xing
abstract:
The jailbreak threat poses a significant concern for Large Language Models (LLMs), primarily due to their potential to generate content at scale. If not properly controlled, LLMs can be exploited to produce undesirable outcomes, including the dissemination of misinformation, offensive content, and other forms of harmful or unethical behavior. To tackle this pressing issue, researchers and developers often rely on red-team efforts to manually create adversarial inputs and prompts designed to push LLMs into generating harmful, biased, or inappropriate content. However, this approach encounters serious scalability challenges.
To address these scalability issues, we introduce an automated solution for large-scale LLM jailbreak susceptibility assessment called LLM-Fuzzer. Inspired by fuzz testing, LLM-Fuzzer uses human-crafted jailbreak prompts as starting points. By employing carefully customized seed selection strategies and mutation mechanisms, LLM-Fuzzer generates additional jailbreak prompts tailored to specific LLMs. Our experiments show that LLM-Fuzzer-generated jailbreak prompts demonstrate significantly increased exploitability and transferability. This highlights that many open-source and commercial LLMs suffer from severe jailbreak issues, even after safety fine-tuning.
summary: Introduces LLM-Fuzzer, an automated fuzz-testing-inspired framework for scalable LLM jailbreak assessment using seed selection and mutation of jailbreak prompts.
summary_zh: 提出LLM-Fuzzer，一个受模糊测试启发的自动化框架，通过种子选择和越狱提示变异进行可扩展的LLM越狱评估。
abstract_zh: 越狱威胁对大型语言模型（LLM）构成重大关切，主要是因为它们有可能大规模生成内容。如果不加以适当控制，LLM可能被利用来产生不良结果，包括传播虚假信息、攻击性内容以及其他形式的有害或不道德行为。为了解决这一紧迫问题，研究人员和开发者通常依赖红队努力，手动创建对抗性输入和提示，旨在推动LLM生成有害、有偏见或不当的内容。然而，这种方法面临严重的可扩展性挑战。为了解决这些可扩展性问题，我们引入了一种名为LLM-Fuzzer的自动化解决方案，用于大规模LLM越狱易感性评估。受模糊测试启发，LLM-Fuzzer使用人工制作的越狱提示作为起始点。通过采用精心定制的种子选择策略和变异机制，LLM-Fuzzer生成针对特定LLM的额外越狱提示。我们的实验表明，LLM-Fuzzer生成的越狱提示展示了显著提高的可利用性和可迁移性。这突显了许多开源和商业LLM即使在安全微调后仍存在严重的越狱问题。
contributions: Introduces LLM-Fuzzer, an automated framework for large-scale LLM jailbreak susceptibility assessment inspired by fuzz testing.
Uses human-crafted jailbreak prompts as seeds with customized selection strategies and mutation mechanisms.
Generates additional jailbreak prompts tailored to specific LLMs with increased exploitability and transferability.
Demonstrates that many open-source and commercial LLMs suffer from severe jailbreak issues even after safety fine-tuning.
contributions_zh: 提出LLM-Fuzzer，一个受模糊测试启发的用于大规模LLM越狱易感性评估的自动化框架。
使用人工制作的越狱提示作为种子，配合定制的选择策略和变异机制。
生成针对特定LLM的额外越狱提示，具有更高的可利用性和可迁移性。
证明许多开源和商业LLM即使在安全微调后仍存在严重的越狱问题。

experiment.key_results: LLM-Fuzzer-generated jailbreak prompts demonstrate significantly increased exploitability and transferability across open-source and commercial LLMs

research_questions: Can fuzz testing techniques be adapted to automatically generate diverse jailbreak prompts for assessing LLM safety at scale?
How do seed selection strategies and mutation mechanisms affect the exploitability of generated jailbreak prompts?
Do LLM-Fuzzer-generated jailbreak prompts transfer effectively across different LLM models?
research_questions_zh: 模糊测试技术能否被改造为自动生成多样化的越狱提示以大规模评估LLM安全性？
种子选择策略和变异机制如何影响生成的越狱提示的可利用性？
LLM-Fuzzer生成的越狱提示能否在不同LLM模型之间有效转移？
conclusions: Jailbreak prompts generated by LLM-Fuzzer significantly outperformed manually crafted prompts in both exploitability and transferability.
Many open-source and commercial LLMs remain seriously vulnerable to jailbreaking even after safety fine-tuning.
The fuzzing-based automated approach effectively addresses the scalability challenges of manual red-teaming.
conclusions_zh: LLM-Fuzzer生成的越狱提示在可利用性和可转移性方面显著优于人工制作的提示。
许多开源和商业LLM即使经过安全微调仍存在严重的越狱问题。
基于模糊测试的自动化方法有效解决了手动红队测试的可扩展性挑战。

title: An Empirical Study of Automated Vulnerability Localization with Large Language Models
authors: Jian Zhang, Chong Wang, Anran Li, Weisong Sun, Cen Zhang, Wei Ma, Yang Liu
abstract: Recently, Automated Vulnerability Localization (AVL) has attracted much attention, aiming to facilitate diagnosis by pinpointing the lines of code responsible for discovered vulnerabilities. Large Language Models (LLMs) have shown potential in various domains, yet their effectiveness in vulnerability localization remains underexplored. In this work, we perform the first comprehensive study of LLMs for AVL. Our investigation encompasses 10+ leading LLMs suitable for code analysis, including ChatGPT and various open-source models, across three architectural types: encoder-only, encoder-decoder, and decoder-only, with model sizes ranging from 60M to 16B parameters. We explore the efficacy of these LLMs using 4 distinct paradigms: zero-shot learning, one-shot learning, discriminative fine-tuning, and generative fine-tuning. Our evaluation framework is applied to the BigVul-based dataset for C/C++, and an additional dataset comprising smart contract vulnerabilities. The results demonstrate that discriminative fine-tuning of LLMs can significantly outperform existing learning-based methods for AVL, while other paradigms prove less effective or unexpectedly ineffective for the task. We also identify challenges related to input length and unidirectional context in fine-tuning processes for encoders and decoders. We then introduce two remedial strategies: the sliding window and the right-forward embedding, both of which substantially enhance performance. Furthermore, our findings highlight certain generalization capabilities of LLMs across Common Weakness Enumerations (CWEs) and different projects, indicating a promising pathway toward their practical application in vulnerability localization.
summary: First comprehensive study of 10+ LLMs (60M-16B parameters) for automated vulnerability localization using 4 paradigms on BigVul C/C++ and smart contract datasets.
summary_zh: 首次全面研究10多个LLM（60M-16B参数）用于自动漏洞定位，在BigVul C/C++和智能合约数据集上使用4种范式。
abstract_zh: 近年来，自动漏洞定位（AVL）引起了广泛关注，旨在通过精确定位导致已发现漏洞的代码行来促进诊断。大型语言模型（LLM）在各个领域展示了潜力，但它们在漏洞定位方面的有效性仍未得到充分探索。在这项工作中，我们对LLM用于AVL进行了首次全面研究。我们的调查涵盖了10多个适合代码分析的领先LLM，包括ChatGPT和各种开源模型，跨三种架构类型：仅编码器、编码器-解码器和仅解码器，模型大小从60M到16B参数。我们使用4种不同的范式探索这些LLM的效果：零样本学习、单样本学习、判别式微调和生成式微调。我们的评估框架应用于基于BigVul的C/C++数据集以及一个包含智能合约漏洞的额外数据集。结果表明，LLM的判别式微调可以显著优于现有的基于学习的AVL方法，而其他范式被证明效果较差或对该任务出人意料地无效。我们还识别了与微调过程中编码器和解码器的输入长度和单向上下文相关的挑战。然后我们引入了两种补救策略：滑动窗口和右向前嵌入，两者都显著提高了性能。此外，我们的发现强调了LLM在常见弱点枚举（CWE）和不同项目之间的某些泛化能力，表明它们在漏洞定位实际应用方面的前景。
contributions: Performs the first comprehensive study of LLMs for Automated Vulnerability Localization (AVL) across 10+ LLMs.
Evaluates encoder-only, encoder-decoder, and decoder-only architectures with model sizes from 60M to 16B parameters.
Tests four paradigms: zero-shot learning, one-shot learning, discriminative fine-tuning, and generative fine-tuning.
Demonstrates discriminative fine-tuning significantly outperforms existing learning-based methods for AVL.
Introduces sliding window and right-forward embedding strategies to address input length and unidirectional context challenges.
contributions_zh: 对10多个LLM进行了首次全面的自动漏洞定位（AVL）研究。
评估了编码器、编码器-解码器和解码器架构，模型大小从60M到16B参数。
测试了四种范式：零样本学习、单样本学习、判别式微调和生成式微调。
证明判别式微调显著优于现有的基于学习的AVL方法。
引入滑动窗口和右向前嵌入策略来解决输入长度和单向上下文挑战。

experiment.key_results: Discriminative fine-tuning significantly outperforms existing learning-based methods for AVL; sliding window and right-forward embedding strategies substantially enhance performance

research_questions: Which LLM architectural type (encoder-only, encoder-decoder, decoder-only) and learning paradigm is most effective for automated vulnerability localization?
How do zero-shot, one-shot, discriminative fine-tuning, and generative fine-tuning compare for vulnerability localization?
Can remedial strategies like sliding window and right-forward embedding address input length and unidirectional context limitations?
research_questions_zh: 哪种LLM架构类型（仅编码器、编码器-解码器、仅解码器）和学习范式对自动化漏洞定位最有效？
零样本、单样本、判别式微调和生成式微调在漏洞定位中的效果如何比较？
滑动窗口和右前向嵌入等补救策略能否解决输入长度和单向上下文的限制？
conclusions: Discriminative fine-tuning of LLMs significantly outperformed existing learning-based vulnerability localization methods, while other paradigms proved less effective or surprisingly ineffective.
Both the sliding window and right-forward embedding remediation strategies significantly improved performance during encoder and decoder fine-tuning.
LLMs demonstrated a degree of generalization across CWE types and different projects, indicating their promise for practical vulnerability localization applications.
conclusions_zh: LLM的判别式微调显著优于现有的基于学习的漏洞定位方法，而其他范式效果较差或出乎意料地无效。
滑动窗口和右前向嵌入两种补救策略都能显著提升编码器和解码器微调过程中的性能。
LLM在跨CWE类型和不同项目之间展现了一定的泛化能力，表明其在漏洞定位实际应用中具有前景。

title: VTT-LLM: Advancing Vulnerability-to-Tactic-and-Technique Mapping through Fine-Tuning of Large Language Model
authors: Chenhui Zhang, Le Wang, Dunqiu Fan, Junyi Zhu, Tang Zhou, Liyi Zeng, Zhaohua Li
abstract: Vulnerabilities are often accompanied by cyberattacks. CVE is the largest repository of open vulnerabilities, which keeps expanding. ATT&CK models known multi-step attacks both tactically and technically and remains up to date. It is valuable to correlate the vulnerability in CVE with the corresponding tactic and technique of ATT&CK which exploit the vulnerability, for active defense. Mappings manually is not only time-consuming but also difficult to keep up-to-date. Existing language-based automated mapping methods do not utilize the information associated with attack behaviors outside of CVE and ATT&CK and are therefore ineffective. In this paper, we propose a novel framework named VTT-LLM for mapping Vulnerabilities to Tactics and Techniques based on Large Language Models, which consists of a generation model and a mapping model. In order to generate fine-tuning instructions for LLM, we create a template to extract knowledge of CWE (a standardized list of common weaknesses) and CAPEC (a standardized list of common attack patterns). We train the generation model of VTT-LLM by fine-tuning the LLM according to the above instructions. The generation model correlates vulnerability and attack through their descriptions. The mapping model transforms the descriptions of ATT&CK tactics and techniques into vectors through text embedding and further associates them with attacks through semantic matching. By leveraging the knowledge of CWE and CAPEC, VTT-LLM can eventually automate the process of linking vulnerabilities in CVE to the attack techniques and tactics of ATT&CK. Experiments on the latest public dataset, ChatGPT-VDMEval, show the effectiveness of VTT-LLM with an accuracy of 85.18%, which is 13.69% and 54.42% higher than the existing CVET and ChatGPT-based methods, respectively. In addition, compared to fine-tuning without outside knowledge, the accuracy of VTT-LLM with chain fine-tuning is 9.24% higher on average across different LLMs.
summary: Proposes VTT-LLM framework mapping CVE vulnerabilities to ATT&CK tactics/techniques via LLM fine-tuning with CWE/CAPEC knowledge, achieving 85.18% accuracy.
summary_zh: 提出VTT-LLM框架，通过使用CWE/CAPEC知识微调LLM将CVE漏洞映射到ATT&CK战术/技术，在ChatGPT-VDMEval上达到85.18%的准确率。
abstract_zh: 漏洞通常伴随着网络攻击。CVE是最大的公开漏洞库，不断扩展。ATT&CK从战术和技术两个层面建模已知的多步攻击，并保持更新。将CVE中的漏洞与利用该漏洞的ATT&CK对应战术和技术关联起来，对于主动防御具有重要价值。手动映射不仅耗时，而且难以保持更新。现有的基于语言的自动映射方法没有利用CVE和ATT&CK之外与攻击行为相关的信息，因此效果不佳。在本文中，我们提出了一个名为VTT-LLM的新颖框架，用于基于大型语言模型将漏洞映射到战术和技术。该框架由一个生成模型和一个映射模型组成。为了生成LLM的微调指令，我们创建了一个模板来提取CWE（常见弱点的标准化列表）和CAPEC（常见攻击模式的标准化列表）的知识。我们根据上述指令微调LLM来训练VTT-LLM的生成模型。生成模型通过其描述关联漏洞和攻击。映射模型通过文本嵌入将ATT&CK战术和技术的描述转换为向量，并通过语义匹配进一步将其与攻击关联。通过利用CWE和CAPEC的知识，VTT-LLM最终可以自动化将CVE中的漏洞链接到ATT&CK的攻击技术和战术的过程。在最新公开数据集ChatGPT-VDMEval上的实验表明VTT-LLM的有效性，准确率为85.18%，分别比现有的CVET和基于ChatGPT的方法高13.69%和54.42%。此外，与不使用外部知识的微调相比，使用链式微调的VTT-LLM在不同LLM上的准确率平均高9.24%。
contributions: Proposes VTT-LLM, a novel framework for mapping vulnerabilities to ATT&CK tactics and techniques using LLMs.
Creates fine-tuning instruction templates extracting knowledge from CWE and CAPEC standards.
Designs a two-stage architecture with a generation model correlating vulnerabilities and attacks, and a mapping model using text embedding and semantic matching.
Achieves 85.18% accuracy on ChatGPT-VDMEval dataset, 13.69% higher than CVET and 54.42% higher than ChatGPT-based methods.
Demonstrates chain fine-tuning with outside knowledge improves accuracy by 9.24% on average compared to fine-tuning without it.
contributions_zh: 提出VTT-LLM，一种使用LLM将漏洞映射到ATT&CK战术和技术的新颖框架。
创建从CWE和CAPEC标准中提取知识的微调指令模板。
设计了一个两阶段架构，包括关联漏洞和攻击的生成模型，以及使用文本嵌入和语义匹配的映射模型。
在ChatGPT-VDMEval数据集上达到85.18%的准确率，比CVET高13.69%，比基于ChatGPT的方法高54.42%。
展示使用外部知识的链式微调比不使用外部知识的微调平均准确率高9.24%。

experiment.key_results: 85.18% accuracy on ChatGPT-VDMEval, 13.69% higher than CVET, 54.42% higher than ChatGPT; chain fine-tuning improves accuracy by 9.24% on average

research_questions: Can LLMs be fine-tuned to automatically map CVE vulnerabilities to ATT&CK tactics and techniques?
Does incorporating external knowledge from CWE and CAPEC improve the accuracy of vulnerability-to-attack mapping?
How does chain fine-tuning with outside knowledge compare to standard fine-tuning for vulnerability mapping?
research_questions_zh: LLM能否通过微调自动将CVE漏洞映射到ATT&CK战术和技术？
融入CWE和CAPEC的外部知识是否能提高漏洞到攻击映射的准确性？
结合外部知识的链式微调与标准微调在漏洞映射方面的效果如何比较？
conclusions: VTT-LLM achieved 85.18% accuracy on the ChatGPT-VDMEval dataset, outperforming CVET by 13.69% and ChatGPT-based methods by 54.42%.
Chain fine-tuning improved average accuracy by 9.24% across different LLMs compared to fine-tuning without external knowledge.
By leveraging CWE and CAPEC knowledge to create fine-tuning instructions, VTT-LLM can automate the CVE-to-ATT&CK mapping process.
conclusions_zh: VTT-LLM在ChatGPT-VDMEval数据集上达到85.18%的准确率，比CVET高13.69%，比基于ChatGPT的方法高54.42%。
链式微调比不使用外部知识的微调在不同LLM上平均准确率提高了9.24%。
通过利用CWE和CAPEC知识创建微调指令，VTT-LLM能够自动化CVE到ATT&CK的映射过程。

title: Comparison of static application security testing tools and large language models for repo-level vulnerability detection
authors: Xin Zhou, Duc-Manh Tran, Thanh Le-Cong, Ting Zhang, Ivana Clairine Irsan, Joshua Sumarlin, Bach Le, David Lo
abstract: Software vulnerabilities pose significant security challenges and potential risks to society, necessitating extensive efforts in automated vulnerability detection. There are two popular lines of work to address automated vulnerability detection. On one hand, Static Application Security Testing (SAST) is usually utilized to scan source code for security vulnerabilities, especially in industries. On the other hand, deep learning (DL)-based methods, especially since the introduction of large language models (LLMs), have demonstrated their potential in software vulnerability detection. However, there is no comparative study between SAST tools and LLMs, aiming to determine their effectiveness in vulnerability detection, understand the pros and cons of both SAST and LLMs, and explore the potential combination of these two families of approaches. In this paper, we compared 15 diverse SAST tools with 12 popular or state-of-the-art open-source LLMs in detecting software vulnerabilities from repositories of three popular programming languages: Java, C, and Python. The experimental results showed that SAST tools obtain low vulnerability detection rates with relatively low false positives, while LLMs can detect up 90% to 100% of vulnerabilities but suffer from high false positives. By further ensembling the SAST tools and LLMs, the drawbacks of both SAST tools and LLMs can be mitigated to some extent. Our analysis sheds light on both the current progress and future directions for software vulnerability detection.
summary: Compares 15 SAST tools with 12 open-source LLMs for repo-level vulnerability detection in Java, C, and Python, finding LLMs detect 90-100% but with high false positives.
summary_zh: 比较15个SAST工具和12个开源LLM在Java、C和Python仓库级漏洞检测中的表现，发现LLM检测率达90-100%但误报率高。
abstract_zh: 软件漏洞给安全带来重大挑战并对社会构成潜在风险，需要大量自动化漏洞检测的努力。有两条流行的研究路线来解决自动化漏洞检测。一方面，静态应用安全测试（SAST）通常用于扫描源代码中的安全漏洞，特别是在工业界。另一方面，基于深度学习（DL）的方法，特别是随着大型语言模型（LLM）的引入，已展示了其在软件漏洞检测方面的潜力。然而，目前没有SAST工具与LLM之间的比较研究来确定它们在漏洞检测方面的有效性、了解SAST和LLM的优缺点，以及探索这两类方法的潜在组合。在本文中，我们比较了15种不同的SAST工具和12个流行或最先进的开源LLM在检测三种流行编程语言（Java、C和Python）仓库中的软件漏洞方面的表现。实验结果表明，SAST工具获得的漏洞检测率较低但误报率相对较低，而LLM可以检测到90%到100%的漏洞但误报率较高。通过进一步集成SAST工具和LLM，可以在一定程度上缓解两者的缺点。我们的分析揭示了软件漏洞检测的当前进展和未来方向。
contributions: Provides the first comparative study between SAST tools and LLMs for vulnerability detection.
Compares 15 diverse SAST tools with 12 popular or state-of-the-art open-source LLMs.
Evaluates on repositories from three programming languages: Java, C, and Python.
Finds SAST tools obtain low detection rates with low false positives, while LLMs detect 90-100% of vulnerabilities with high false positives.
Demonstrates that ensembling SAST tools and LLMs can mitigate drawbacks of both approaches.
contributions_zh: 提供了SAST工具与LLM在漏洞检测方面的首次比较研究。
比较了15种不同的SAST工具和12个流行或最先进的开源LLM。
在Java、C和Python三种编程语言的仓库上进行评估。
发现SAST工具检测率低但误报低，而LLM检测率达90-100%但误报率高。
证明将SAST工具与LLM集成可以在一定程度上缓解两种方法的缺点。

experiment.key_results: SAST tools have low detection rates with low false positives; LLMs detect 90-100% of vulnerabilities but with high false positives; ensembling mitigates both drawbacks

research_questions: How do SAST tools and LLMs compare in repo-level vulnerability detection across Java, C, and Python?
What are the respective strengths and weaknesses of SAST tools versus LLMs for vulnerability detection?
Can ensembling SAST tools and LLMs mitigate the individual drawbacks of each approach?
research_questions_zh: 在Java、C和Python仓库级漏洞检测中，SAST工具和LLM的表现如何比较？
SAST工具和LLM在漏洞检测中各自的优势和劣势是什么？
将SAST工具与LLM集成能否减轻各自方法的单独缺陷？
conclusions: SAST tools exhibited low vulnerability detection rates but also low false positive rates, while LLMs detected 90% to 100% of vulnerabilities but suffered from high false positive rates.
Integrating SAST tools with LLMs can mitigate the respective shortcomings of both approaches to some extent.
A comprehensive comparison of 15 SAST tools and 12 LLMs revealed current progress and future directions in vulnerability detection.
conclusions_zh: SAST工具漏洞检测率较低但误报率也较低，LLM能检测90%至100%的漏洞但存在高误报率。
将SAST工具与LLM集成可以在一定程度上缓解两者各自的缺陷。
15种SAST工具与12种LLM的全面对比揭示了当前漏洞检测的进展和未来方向。

title: Large language model for vulnerability detection: Emerging results and future directions
authors: Xin Zhou, Ting Zhang, David Lo
venue: Proceedings of the 2024 ACM/IEEE 44th International Conference on Software Engineering: New Ideas and Emerging Results
abstract: Previous learning-based vulnerability detection methods relied on either medium-sized pre-trained models or smaller neural networks from scratch. Recent advancements in Large Pre-Trained Language Models (LLMs) have showcased remarkable few-shot learning capabilities in various tasks. However, the effectiveness of LLMs in detecting software vulnerabilities is largely unexplored. This paper aims to bridge this gap by exploring how LLMs perform with various prompts, particularly focusing on two state-of-the-art LLMs: GPT-3.5 and GPT-4. Our experimental results showed that GPT-3.5 achieves competitive performance with the prior state-of-the-art vulnerability detection approach and GPT-4 consistently outperformed the state-of-the-art.
summary: Explores GPT-3.5 and GPT-4 for vulnerability detection with various prompts, finding GPT-3.5 competitive with and GPT-4 consistently outperforming prior state-of-the-art.
summary_zh: 探索GPT-3.5和GPT-4使用各种提示进行漏洞检测，发现GPT-3.5与先前最优方法持平，GPT-4则始终超越先前最优方法。
abstract_zh: 先前基于学习的漏洞检测方法依赖于中等大小的预训练模型或从头训练的较小神经网络。大型预训练语言模型（LLM）的最新进展在各种任务中展示了出色的少样本学习能力。然而，LLM在检测软件漏洞方面的有效性在很大程度上尚未被探索。本文旨在通过探索LLM在各种提示下的表现来填补这一空白，特别关注两个最先进的LLM：GPT-3.5和GPT-4。我们的实验结果表明，GPT-3.5达到了与先前最优漏洞检测方法持平的性能，而GPT-4始终优于最优方法。
contributions: Explores the effectiveness of LLMs (GPT-3.5 and GPT-4) for software vulnerability detection with various prompting strategies.
Bridges the gap in understanding LLM capabilities for vulnerability detection compared to smaller pre-trained models.
Shows GPT-3.5 achieves competitive performance with prior state-of-the-art vulnerability detection approaches.
Demonstrates GPT-4 consistently outperforms the state-of-the-art in vulnerability detection.
contributions_zh: 探索LLM（GPT-3.5和GPT-4）使用各种提示策略进行软件漏洞检测的有效性。
填补了理解LLM漏洞检测能力与较小预训练模型相比的空白。
展示GPT-3.5达到了与先前最优漏洞检测方法持平的性能。
证明GPT-4在漏洞检测方面始终优于最优方法。

experiment.key_results: GPT-3.5 achieves competitive performance with prior state-of-the-art; GPT-4 consistently outperforms state-of-the-art

research_questions: How effective are GPT-3.5 and GPT-4 at detecting software vulnerabilities compared to prior state-of-the-art approaches?
How do various prompting strategies affect LLM performance in vulnerability detection?
Can few-shot learning capabilities of LLMs bridge the gap with specialized vulnerability detection models?
research_questions_zh: GPT-3.5和GPT-4在检测软件漏洞方面与先前最先进的方法相比效果如何？
不同的提示策略如何影响LLM在漏洞检测中的表现？
LLM的少样本学习能力能否弥合与专业漏洞检测模型之间的差距？
conclusions: GPT-3.5 achieved performance comparable to prior state-of-the-art methods in vulnerability detection.
GPT-4 consistently outperformed state-of-the-art methods in vulnerability detection, demonstrating the potential of LLMs in this domain.
The choice of prompting strategy has a significant impact on the vulnerability detection performance of LLMs.
conclusions_zh: GPT-3.5在漏洞检测方面达到了与先前最先进方法相当的性能。
GPT-4在漏洞检测中始终优于最先进的方法，展现了LLM在该领域的潜力。
提示策略的选择对LLM的漏洞检测性能有重要影响。

title: Large Language Models are Zero-Shot Fuzzers: Fuzzing Deep-Learning Libraries via Large Language Models
authors: Yinlin Deng, Chunqiu Steven Xia, Haoran Peng, Chenyuan Yang, Lingming Zhang
abstract: Deep Learning (DL) systems are pervasive in modern software, powering applications in diverse domains. Testing DL libraries is crucial for ensuring the reliability of downstream DL applications. However, existing approaches struggle to generate valid API-level inputs. This paper proposes TitanFuzz, a novel approach that leverages Large Language Models (LLMs) to directly generate input programs for fuzzing DL libraries. TitanFuzz combines LLM-based code generation with evolutionary fuzzing strategies.
summary: Proposes TitanFuzz leveraging LLMs as zero-shot fuzzers for DL libraries, combining code generation with evolutionary strategies to find bugs in TensorFlow and PyTorch.
summary_zh: 提出TitanFuzz，利用LLM作为零样本模糊测试器测试深度学习库，结合代码生成与进化策略在TensorFlow和PyTorch中发现漏洞。

experiment.key_results: Found 65 bugs in TensorFlow/PyTorch, 44 confirmed, 15 CVEs
experiment.real_world_impact: 15 CVEs assigned for TensorFlow/PyTorch bugs

contributions: Proposes TitanFuzz, a novel approach for leveraging LLMs as zero-shot fuzzers for DL libraries, combining code generation with evolutionary strategies to find bu.
Leverages Codex, InCoder for intelligent test case generation and mutation strategies.
Conducts extensive evaluation against 3 baseline approaches including FreeFuzz, DeepREL, DocTer.
Demonstrates strong empirical results: Found 65 bugs in TensorFlow/PyTorch, 44 confirmed, 15 CVEs.
Addresses crash, NaN, inconsistency vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.
contributions_zh: 提出 TitanFuzz, a novel approach for leveraging LLMs as zero-shot fuzzers for DL libraries, combining code generation with evolutionary strategies to find bu.
利用 Codex, InCoder for intelligent test case generation and mutation strategies.
进行了广泛的评估 against 3 baseline approaches including FreeFuzz, DeepREL, DocTer.
证明了 strong empirical results: Found 65 bugs in TensorFlow/PyTorch, 44 confirmed, 15 CVEs.
解决 crash, NaN, inconsistency vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.
abstract_zh: 提出TitanFuzz，利用LLM作为零样本模糊测试器测试深度学习库，结合代码生成与进化策略在TensorFlow和PyTorch中发现漏洞。
research_questions: Can LLMs serve as zero-shot fuzzers to directly generate valid input programs for testing deep learning libraries?
How effective is combining LLM-based code generation with evolutionary fuzzing strategies for DL library testing?
What types of bugs (crashes, NaN errors, inconsistencies) can LLM-generated test programs uncover in TensorFlow and PyTorch?
research_questions_zh: LLM能否作为零样本模糊测试器直接生成有效的输入程序来测试深度学习库？
将基于LLM的代码生成与进化模糊测试策略相结合对DL库测试的效果如何？
LLM生成的测试程序能在TensorFlow和PyTorch中发现哪些类型的漏洞（崩溃、NaN错误、不一致性）？
conclusions: TitanFuzz discovered 65 bugs in TensorFlow and PyTorch, of which 44 were confirmed and 15 were assigned CVE numbers.
LLMs can generate valid API-level inputs under zero-shot conditions, addressing the difficulty existing methods face in generating effective test inputs.
Combining LLM code generation with evolutionary fuzzing strategies proved more effective than baseline methods such as FreeFuzz, DeepREL, and DocTer.
conclusions_zh: TitanFuzz在TensorFlow和PyTorch中发现了65个漏洞，其中44个被确认，15个获得CVE编号。
LLM能够在零样本条件下生成有效的API级输入，解决了现有方法难以生成有效测试输入的问题。
将LLM代码生成与进化模糊测试策略结合，比FreeFuzz、DeepREL和DocTer等基线方法更有效。

title: Fuzz4All: Universal Fuzzing with Large Language Models
authors: Chunqiu Steven Xia, Matteo Paltenghi, Jia Le, Yun Tian, Michael Pradel, Lingming Zhang
abstract: Fuzzing has achieved remarkable success in finding bugs and vulnerabilities in various software systems. Systems under test (SUTs) that take in programming language code as inputs are particularly important. However, existing fuzzers for such systems often target a specific language, limiting their applicability. We propose Fuzz4All, the first fuzzer that is universal across languages. The key idea is to leverage Large Language Models (LLMs) as an input generation and mutation engine.
summary: Proposes Fuzz4All, the first universal fuzzer using LLMs to test compilers/interpreters across C, C++, Go, SMT2, and Java, finding 98 bugs.
summary_zh: 提出Fuzz4All，首个使用LLM跨C、C++、Go、SMT2和Java测试编译器/解释器的通用模糊测试器，发现98个漏洞。

experiment.key_results: Found 98 bugs, 64 confirmed, across 6 SUTs in 5 languages
experiment.real_world_impact: Bugs found in GCC, Clang, Go compiler, Z3, javac

contributions: Proposes Fuzz4All, a novel approach for first universal fuzzer using LLMs to test compilers/interpreters across C, C++, Go, SMT2, and Java, finding 98 bugs.
Leverages GPT-4, StarCoder for intelligent test case generation and mutation strategies.
Conducts extensive evaluation against 4 baseline approaches including CSmith, YARPGen, GrayC.
Demonstrates strong empirical results: Found 98 bugs, 64 confirmed, across 6 SUTs in 5 languages.
Addresses crash, miscompilation vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.
contributions_zh: 提出 Fuzz4All, a novel approach for first universal fuzzer using LLMs to test compilers/interpreters across C, C++, Go, SMT2, and Java, finding 98 bugs.
利用 GPT-4, StarCoder for intelligent test case generation and mutation strategies.
进行了广泛的评估 against 4 baseline approaches including CSmith, YARPGen, GrayC.
证明了 strong empirical results: Found 98 bugs, 64 confirmed, across 6 SUTs in 5 languages.
解决 crash, miscompilation vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.
abstract_zh: 提出Fuzz4All，首个使用LLM跨C、C++、Go、SMT2和Java测试编译器/解释器的通用模糊测试器，发现98个漏洞。
research_questions: Can a single LLM-based fuzzer be made universal across multiple programming languages for compiler/interpreter testing?
How does leveraging LLMs as input generation and mutation engines compare to language-specific fuzzers?
Can Fuzz4All find bugs across diverse systems under test (compilers, interpreters, solvers) in C, C++, Go, SMT2, and Java?
research_questions_zh: 单个基于LLM的模糊测试器能否实现跨多种编程语言的通用编译器/解释器测试？
利用LLM作为输入生成和变异引擎与特定语言的模糊测试器相比效果如何？
Fuzz4All能否在C、C++、Go、SMT2和Java的多种被测系统（编译器、解释器、求解器）中发现漏洞？
conclusions: Fuzz4All discovered 98 bugs across 6 systems under test in 5 programming languages, with 64 confirmed.
Using an LLM as a universal input generation engine enables a single fuzzer to effectively test systems across multiple languages, outperforming language-specific tools such as CSmith and YARPGen.
The approach successfully uncovered real bugs in production systems including GCC, Clang, the Go compiler, Z3, and javac.
conclusions_zh: Fuzz4All在5种语言的6个被测系统中发现了98个漏洞，其中64个已被确认。
LLM作为通用输入生成引擎，使单一模糊测试器能够有效测试跨多种语言的系统，优于CSmith、YARPGen等语言特定工具。
该方法成功在GCC、Clang、Go编译器、Z3和javac等生产系统中发现了真实漏洞。

title: WhiteFox: White-Box Compiler Fuzzing Empowered by Large Language Models
authors: Chenyuan Yang, Yinlin Deng, Runyu Lu, Jiayi Yao, Jiawei Liu, Reyhaneh Jabbarvand, Lingming Zhang
abstract: Compiler correctness is crucial, as miscompilation can falsify program execution. Fuzzing has been used extensively to uncover compiler defects. However, existing methods are limited by the inability to target specific optimization passes. We propose WhiteFox, the first white-box compiler fuzzer using LLMs. WhiteFox analyzes compiler source code to understand optimization triggers and generates test programs that specifically exercise those code paths.
summary: Proposes WhiteFox, white-box compiler fuzzer using LLMs to analyze optimization source code and generate targeted test programs, finding 101 bugs in GCC and LLVM.
summary_zh: 提出WhiteFox，使用LLM分析优化源代码并生成针对性测试程序的白盒编译器模糊测试器，在GCC和LLVM中发现101个漏洞。

experiment.key_results: Found 101 bugs in GCC and LLVM, 96 confirmed
experiment.real_world_impact: Bugs confirmed and fixed in GCC and LLVM trunk

contributions: Proposes WhiteFox, a novel approach for white-box compiler fuzzer using LLMs to analyze optimization source code and generate targeted test programs, finding 10.
Leverages GPT-4 for intelligent test case generation and mutation strategies.
Conducts extensive evaluation against 3 baseline approaches including CSmith, YARPGen, Fuzz4All.
Demonstrates strong empirical results: Found 101 bugs in GCC and LLVM, 96 confirmed.
Addresses miscompilation, crash vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.
contributions_zh: 提出 WhiteFox, a novel approach for white-box compiler fuzzer using LLMs to analyze optimization source code and generate targeted test programs, finding 10.
利用 GPT-4 for intelligent test case generation and mutation strategies.
进行了广泛的评估 against 3 baseline approaches including CSmith, YARPGen, Fuzz4All.
证明了 strong empirical results: Found 101 bugs in GCC and LLVM, 96 confirmed.
解决 miscompilation, crash vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.
abstract_zh: 提出WhiteFox，使用LLM分析优化源代码并生成针对性测试程序的白盒编译器模糊测试器，在GCC和LLVM中发现101个漏洞。
research_questions: Can LLMs analyze compiler optimization source code to understand optimization triggers and generate targeted test programs?
How does white-box compiler fuzzing using LLMs compare to black-box approaches like CSmith and Fuzz4All?
Can LLM-generated test programs specifically exercise compiler optimization passes to uncover miscompilation bugs?
research_questions_zh: LLM能否分析编译器优化源代码以理解优化触发条件并生成有针对性的测试程序？
使用LLM的白盒编译器模糊测试与CSmith和Fuzz4All等黑盒方法相比效果如何？
LLM生成的测试程序能否专门触发编译器优化遍以发现错误编译漏洞？
conclusions: WhiteFox discovered 101 bugs in GCC and LLVM, of which 96 were confirmed.
By analyzing compiler source code to understand optimization trigger conditions, WhiteFox can generate test programs specifically designed to trigger particular optimization passes.
The white-box approach significantly outperformed black-box baseline methods including CSmith, YARPGen, and Fuzz4All.
conclusions_zh: WhiteFox在GCC和LLVM中发现了101个漏洞，其中96个已被确认。
通过分析编译器源代码理解优化触发条件，WhiteFox能够生成专门触发特定优化遍的测试程序。
白盒方法显著优于CSmith、YARPGen和Fuzz4All等黑盒基线方法。

title: KernelGPT: Enhanced Kernel Fuzzing via Large Language Models
authors: Chenyuan Yang, Zijie Zou, Lingming Zhang
abstract: Kernel fuzzing is essential for detecting vulnerabilities in operating systems. Syzkaller, the state-of-the-art kernel fuzzer, requires manually written specifications (syzlang) to describe syscall interfaces. This process is tedious and error-prone, leaving many interfaces under-tested. We propose KernelGPT, a novel approach leveraging LLMs to automatically generate Syzkaller specifications from kernel source code and documentation.
summary: Proposes KernelGPT using LLMs to automatically generate Syzkaller specifications for kernel fuzzing, improving syscall coverage and finding new kernel bugs.
summary_zh: 提出KernelGPT，利用LLM自动生成Syzkaller规范用于内核模糊测试，提升系统调用覆盖率并发现新的内核漏洞。

experiment.key_results: Generated specs for 113 new drivers, found 11 new kernel bugs
experiment.real_world_impact: New bugs found in Linux kernel drivers

contributions: Proposes KernelGPT, a novel approach for using LLMs to automatically generate Syzkaller specifications for kernel fuzzing, improving syscall coverage and finding.
Leverages GPT-4 for intelligent test case generation and mutation strategies.
Conducts extensive evaluation against 3 baseline approaches including Syzkaller (manual specs), SyzDescribe, KSG.
Demonstrates strong empirical results: Generated specs for 113 new drivers, found 11 new kernel bugs.
Addresses kernel-bug, use-after-free, null-pointer vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.
contributions_zh: 提出 KernelGPT, a novel approach for using LLMs to automatically generate Syzkaller specifications for kernel fuzzing, improving syscall coverage and finding.
利用 GPT-4 for intelligent test case generation and mutation strategies.
进行了广泛的评估 against 3 baseline approaches including Syzkaller (manual specs), SyzDescribe, KSG.
证明了 strong empirical results: Generated specs for 113 new drivers, found 11 new kernel bugs.
解决 kernel-bug, use-after-free, null-pointer vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.
abstract_zh: 提出KernelGPT，利用LLM自动生成Syzkaller规范用于内核模糊测试，提升系统调用覆盖率并发现新的内核漏洞。
research_questions: Can LLMs automatically generate accurate Syzkaller specifications (syzlang) from kernel source code and documentation?
How do LLM-generated kernel fuzzing specifications compare to manually written ones in terms of syscall coverage?
Can automatically generated specifications enable fuzzing of previously under-tested kernel drivers?
research_questions_zh: LLM能否从内核源代码和文档中自动生成准确的Syzkaller规范（syzlang）？
LLM生成的内核模糊测试规范在系统调用覆盖率方面与手写规范相比如何？
自动生成的规范能否使此前测试不足的内核驱动程序进行模糊测试？
conclusions: KernelGPT successfully generated specifications for 113 new drivers and discovered 11 new kernel vulnerabilities.
LLMs can understand syscall interfaces in kernel source code and generate valid syzlang descriptions, outperforming SyzDescribe and KSG.
Automated specification generation significantly expanded the coverage of kernel fuzzing, addressing the time-consuming and error-prone nature of manual specification writing.
conclusions_zh: KernelGPT成功为113个新驱动程序生成了规范，并发现了11个新的内核漏洞。
LLM能够理解内核源代码中的系统调用接口并生成有效的syzlang描述，优于SyzDescribe和KSG。
自动化规范生成显著扩展了内核模糊测试的覆盖范围，解决了手动编写规范费时易错的问题。

title: FuzzGPT: Synthesizing Fuzzing Test Cases via Large Language Models
authors: Yinlin Deng, Chunqiu Steven Xia, Chenyuan Yang, Shujing Zhang, Shengqi Yang, Lingming Zhang
abstract: Fuzzing is essential for software testing, but generating effective test inputs remains challenging. We propose FuzzGPT, which leverages LLMs to synthesize high-quality fuzzing test cases by learning from historical bug-triggering inputs. FuzzGPT uses in-context learning with carefully curated examples of past vulnerability-triggering inputs to guide the LLM in generating similar edge-case inputs.
summary: Proposes FuzzGPT using LLMs with in-context learning from historical bug-triggering inputs to synthesize effective fuzzing test cases for JavaScript engines and compilers.
summary_zh: 提出FuzzGPT，利用LLM通过上下文学习历史触发漏洞的输入来合成有效的模糊测试用例，用于JavaScript引擎和编译器。

experiment.key_results: Found 76 bugs across JS engines and compilers, 49 previously unknown
experiment.real_world_impact: Bugs found in V8, SpiderMonkey, JavaScriptCore

contributions: Proposes FuzzGPT, a novel approach for using LLMs with in-context learning from historical bug-triggering inputs to synthesize effective fuzzing test cases for.
Leverages GPT-3.5, Codex for intelligent test case generation and mutation strategies.
Conducts extensive evaluation against 3 baseline approaches including Montage, DIE, Minerva.
Demonstrates strong empirical results: Found 76 bugs across JS engines and compilers, 49 previously unknown.
Addresses crash, assertion-failure vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.
contributions_zh: 提出 FuzzGPT, a novel approach for using LLMs with in-context learning from historical bug-triggering inputs to synthesize effective fuzzing test cases for.
利用 GPT-3.5, Codex for intelligent test case generation and mutation strategies.
进行了广泛的评估 against 3 baseline approaches including Montage, DIE, Minerva.
证明了 strong empirical results: Found 76 bugs across JS engines and compilers, 49 previously unknown.
解决 crash, assertion-failure vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.
abstract_zh: 提出FuzzGPT，利用LLM通过上下文学习历史触发漏洞的输入来合成有效的模糊测试用例，用于JavaScript引擎和编译器。
research_questions: Can LLMs learn from historical bug-triggering inputs via in-context learning to synthesize new vulnerability-triggering test cases?
How effective are LLM-synthesized fuzzing inputs at finding previously unknown bugs in JavaScript engines and compilers?
Does curating examples of past vulnerability-triggering inputs improve LLM generation of edge-case inputs?
research_questions_zh: LLM能否通过上下文学习从历史触发漏洞的输入中学习以合成新的触发漏洞的测试用例？
LLM合成的模糊测试输入在JavaScript引擎和编译器中发现未知漏洞的效果如何？
精选过去触发漏洞的输入示例是否能改善LLM生成边界情况输入的效果？
conclusions: FuzzGPT discovered 76 bugs in JavaScript engines and compilers, of which 49 were previously unknown.
Through in-context learning with carefully curated historical bug-triggering inputs, LLMs can effectively generate similar edge-case inputs.
FuzzGPT uncovered real bugs in production JavaScript engines including V8, SpiderMonkey, and JavaScriptCore.
conclusions_zh: FuzzGPT在JavaScript引擎和编译器中发现了76个漏洞，其中49个是此前未知的。
通过精心策划的历史漏洞触发输入进行上下文学习，LLM能够有效生成类似的边界情况输入。
FuzzGPT在V8、SpiderMonkey和JavaScriptCore等生产环境JavaScript引擎中发现了真实漏洞。

title: AI-Powered Fuzzing: Breaking the Bug Hunting Barrier
authors: Jonathan Metzman, Laszlo Szekeres, Laurent Simon, Read Sprabery, Abhishek Arya
abstract: We present OSS-Fuzz-Gen, an LLM-based framework for automatically generating fuzz targets for open-source software projects. By leveraging LLMs' understanding of code and API semantics, OSS-Fuzz-Gen analyzes project source code and generates effective fuzz harnesses that can be integrated into the OSS-Fuzz continuous fuzzing pipeline.
summary: Google's OSS-Fuzz-Gen uses LLMs to automatically generate fuzz targets for open-source projects, significantly expanding fuzzing coverage in OSS-Fuzz.
summary_zh: Google的OSS-Fuzz-Gen使用LLM自动为开源项目生成模糊测试目标，显著扩大OSS-Fuzz的模糊测试覆盖范围。

experiment.key_results: Generated harnesses for 160+ projects, increased code coverage by 30%
experiment.real_world_impact: Deployed in Google OSS-Fuzz pipeline

contributions: Proposes OSS-Fuzz-Gen, a novel approach for google's OSS-Fuzz-Gen uses LLMs to automatically generate fuzz targets for open-source projects, significantly expanding.
Leverages Gemini, GPT-4 for intelligent test case generation and mutation strategies.
Evaluates on OSS-Fuzz projects benchmark, providing comprehensive empirical evidence.
Demonstrates strong empirical results: Generated harnesses for 160+ projects, increased code coverage by 30%.
Addresses memory-safety, crash vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.
contributions_zh: 提出 OSS-Fuzz-Gen, a novel approach for google's OSS-Fuzz-Gen uses LLMs to automatically generate fuzz targets for open-source projects, significantly expanding.
利用 Gemini, GPT-4 for intelligent test case generation and mutation strategies.
在以下数据集上进行评估： OSS-Fuzz projects benchmark, providing comprehensive empirical evidence.
证明了 strong empirical results: Generated harnesses for 160+ projects, increased code coverage by 30%.
解决 memory-safety, crash vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.
abstract_zh: Google的OSS-Fuzz-Gen使用LLM自动为开源项目生成模糊测试目标，显著扩大OSS-Fuzz的模糊测试覆盖范围。
research_questions: Can LLMs automatically generate effective fuzz targets for open-source projects by analyzing source code and API semantics?
How does the code coverage of LLM-generated fuzz harnesses compare to manually written ones in the OSS-Fuzz pipeline?
Can LLM-based fuzz target generation scale to hundreds of diverse open-source projects?
research_questions_zh: LLM能否通过分析源代码和API语义自动为开源项目生成有效的模糊测试目标？
在OSS-Fuzz管道中，LLM生成的模糊测试工具的代码覆盖率与手写的相比如何？
基于LLM的模糊测试目标生成能否扩展到数百个不同的开源项目？
conclusions: OSS-Fuzz-Gen successfully generated fuzz harnesses for over 160 projects, increasing code coverage by 30%.
The LLM's understanding of code and API semantics enables it to generate effective harnesses for projects that previously had no fuzzing coverage.
The approach has been deployed in the Google OSS-Fuzz continuous fuzzing pipeline, demonstrating its practical viability.
conclusions_zh: OSS-Fuzz-Gen成功为160多个项目生成了模糊测试工具，代码覆盖率提高了30%。
LLM对代码和API语义的理解使其能够为之前没有模糊测试覆盖的项目生成有效的测试工具。
该方法已部署在Google OSS-Fuzz持续模糊测试管道中，证明了其实际可用性。

title: PromptFuzz: Harnessing Fuzzing Techniques for Robust Testing of Prompt Injection in LLMs
authors: Jiahao Lyu, Yiqi Meng, Zhiyi Li
abstract: Large Language Models are susceptible to prompt injection attacks. We propose PromptFuzz, a fuzzing-based framework that systematically tests LLMs for prompt injection vulnerabilities by generating diverse adversarial prompts using mutation-based and generation-based fuzzing techniques.
summary: Proposes PromptFuzz, applying fuzzing techniques to systematically test LLMs for prompt injection vulnerabilities through adversarial prompt generation.
summary_zh: 提出PromptFuzz，应用模糊测试技术通过对抗性提示生成系统地测试LLM的提示注入漏洞。

experiment.key_results: Discovered 200+ prompt injection variants across 3 major LLMs
experiment.real_world_impact: Identified bypasses in production LLM systems

contributions: Proposes PromptFuzz, a novel approach for applying fuzzing techniques to systematically test LLMs for prompt injection vulnerabilities through adversarial prompt.
Leverages GPT-4, Claude for intelligent test case generation and mutation strategies.
Conducts extensive evaluation against 1 baseline approaches including Manual prompt testing.
Demonstrates strong empirical results: Discovered 200+ prompt injection variants across 3 major LLMs.
Addresses prompt-injection vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.
contributions_zh: 提出 PromptFuzz, a novel approach for applying fuzzing techniques to systematically test LLMs for prompt injection vulnerabilities through adversarial prompt.
利用 GPT-4, Claude for intelligent test case generation and mutation strategies.
进行了广泛的评估 against 1 baseline approaches including Manual prompt testing.
证明了 strong empirical results: Discovered 200+ prompt injection variants across 3 major LLMs.
解决 prompt-injection vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.
abstract_zh: 提出PromptFuzz，应用模糊测试技术通过对抗性提示生成系统地测试LLM的提示注入漏洞。
research_questions: Can fuzzing techniques be systematically applied to test LLMs for prompt injection vulnerabilities?
How effective are mutation-based and generation-based fuzzing strategies at discovering diverse adversarial prompts?
What is the extent of prompt injection susceptibility across major commercial LLMs?
research_questions_zh: 模糊测试技术能否被系统地应用于测试LLM的提示注入漏洞？
基于变异和基于生成的模糊测试策略在发现多样化对抗性提示方面的效果如何？
主要商业LLM的提示注入易感性程度如何？
conclusions: PromptFuzz discovered over 200 prompt injection variants across 3 major LLMs.
Mutation-based and generation-based fuzzing techniques can systematically uncover prompt injection vulnerabilities in LLMs.
The approach identified security bypass methods in production LLM systems, confirming the inadequacy of current LLM safety defenses.
conclusions_zh: PromptFuzz在3个主要LLM中发现了200多种提示注入变体。
基于变异和基于生成的模糊测试技术能够系统性地揭示LLM中的提示注入漏洞。
该方法识别出了生产LLM系统中的安全绕过方式，证实了当前LLM安全防护的不足。

title: InputBlaster: Generating Diverse Crash-Inducing Inputs for Android Apps via LLM
authors: Zhe Liu, Chunyang Chen, Junjie Chen, Mengzhuo Xia, Boyu Wang
abstract: Mobile app testing requires generating diverse inputs that can trigger crashes. We present InputBlaster, a framework that leverages LLMs to understand UI context and generate semantically valid yet boundary-testing inputs for Android apps. By analyzing app UI elements and their expected input types, InputBlaster generates targeted crash-inducing inputs.
summary: Proposes InputBlaster using LLMs to generate diverse crash-inducing inputs for Android apps by understanding UI context and semantics.
summary_zh: 提出InputBlaster，利用LLM理解UI上下文和语义为Android应用生成多样化的崩溃触发输入。

experiment.key_results: Found 118 unique crashes, 73% more than baselines

contributions: Proposes InputBlaster, a novel approach for using LLMs to generate diverse crash-inducing inputs for Android apps by understanding UI context and semantics.
Leverages GPT-3.5, GPT-4 for intelligent test case generation and mutation strategies.
Evaluates on 36 Android apps benchmark, providing comprehensive empirical evidence.
Demonstrates strong empirical results: Found 118 unique crashes, 73% more than baselines.
Addresses crash vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.
contributions_zh: 提出 InputBlaster, a novel approach for using LLMs to generate diverse crash-inducing inputs for Android apps by understanding UI context and semantics.
利用 GPT-3.5, GPT-4 for intelligent test case generation and mutation strategies.
在以下数据集上进行评估： 36 Android apps benchmark, providing comprehensive empirical evidence.
证明了 strong empirical results: Found 118 unique crashes, 73% more than baselines.
解决 crash vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.
abstract_zh: 提出InputBlaster，利用LLM理解UI上下文和语义为Android应用生成多样化的崩溃触发输入。
research_questions: Can LLMs understand UI context and generate semantically valid yet boundary-testing inputs for Android app testing?
How does LLM-based input generation compare to random and heuristic approaches for finding Android app crashes?
What role does UI element analysis play in guiding LLM-generated crash-inducing inputs?
research_questions_zh: LLM能否理解UI上下文并为Android应用测试生成语义有效但边界测试的输入？
基于LLM的输入生成在发现Android应用崩溃方面与随机和启发式方法相比效果如何？
UI元素分析在指导LLM生成导致崩溃的输入方面发挥什么作用？
conclusions: InputBlaster discovered 118 unique crashes, 73% more than baseline methods.
By analyzing application UI elements and their expected input types, LLMs can generate targeted boundary-testing inputs.
The LLM's understanding of UI context enables it to significantly outperform Monkey, QTypist, and CrashTranslator in generating crash-inducing inputs.
conclusions_zh: InputBlaster发现了118个独特的崩溃，比基线方法多73%。
通过分析应用UI元素及其预期输入类型，LLM能够生成有针对性的边界测试输入。
LLM对UI上下文的理解使其在生成导致崩溃的输入方面显著优于Monkey、QTypist和CrashTranslator。

title: LlamaFuzz: Large Language Model Enhanced Greybox Fuzzing
authors: Hongwei Zhang, Zhuo Zhao, Birui Zhang, Yifeng Chen
abstract: Greybox fuzzing is one of the most effective vulnerability detection techniques. However, mutating structured inputs while maintaining their validity remains challenging. We present LlamaFuzz, which integrates fine-tuned LLMs into greybox fuzzing to generate structure-aware mutations. By fine-tuning Llama on a corpus of valid inputs, LlamaFuzz learns input grammar and produces mutations that maintain structural validity while exploring edge cases.
summary: Proposes LlamaFuzz integrating fine-tuned Llama models into greybox fuzzing for structure-aware mutations, improving coverage on structured input formats.
summary_zh: 提出LlamaFuzz，将微调的Llama模型集成到灰盒模糊测试中进行结构感知变异，提升结构化输入格式的覆盖率。

experiment.key_results: 15% higher branch coverage, found 12 new bugs vs AFL++

contributions: Proposes LlamaFuzz, a novel approach for integrating fine-tuned Llama models into greybox fuzzing for structure-aware mutations, improving coverage on structured.
Leverages Llama-2 for intelligent test case generation and mutation strategies.
Evaluates on FuzzBench benchmark, providing comprehensive empirical evidence.
Demonstrates strong empirical results: 15% higher branch coverage, found 12 new bugs vs AFL++.
Addresses memory-safety, crash vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.
contributions_zh: 提出 LlamaFuzz, a novel approach for integrating fine-tuned Llama models into greybox fuzzing for structure-aware mutations, improving coverage on structured.
利用 Llama-2 for intelligent test case generation and mutation strategies.
在以下数据集上进行评估： FuzzBench benchmark, providing comprehensive empirical evidence.
证明了 strong empirical results: 15% higher branch coverage, found 12 new bugs vs AFL++.
解决 memory-safety, crash vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.
abstract_zh: 提出LlamaFuzz，将微调的Llama模型集成到灰盒模糊测试中进行结构感知变异，提升结构化输入格式的覆盖率。
research_questions: Can fine-tuned LLMs learn input grammar to produce structure-aware mutations that maintain validity while exploring edge cases?
How does integrating LLM-based mutations into greybox fuzzing compare to grammar-based fuzzers for structured inputs?
Does LoRA fine-tuning of Llama on valid input corpora effectively teach input structure for fuzzing purposes?
research_questions_zh: 经过微调的LLM能否学习输入语法以产生在保持有效性的同时探索边界情况的结构感知变异？
将基于LLM的变异集成到灰盒模糊测试中与基于语法的模糊测试器在结构化输入方面的效果如何比较？
在有效输入语料库上对Llama进行LoRA微调是否能有效教会输入结构以用于模糊测试？
conclusions: LlamaFuzz achieved 15% higher branch coverage than AFL++ and discovered 12 new bugs.
Fine-tuning Llama-2 via LoRA to learn input grammar enables the generation of mutations that maintain structural validity, outperforming Nautilus, Superion, and Gramatron.
Integrating a fine-tuned LLM into greybox fuzzing effectively addresses the challenge of maintaining validity when processing structured inputs.
conclusions_zh: LlamaFuzz比AFL++分支覆盖率提高了15%，并发现了12个新漏洞。
通过LoRA微调Llama-2学习输入语法，能够生成保持结构有效性的变异，优于Nautilus、Superion和Gramatron。
将微调LLM集成到灰盒模糊测试中，有效解决了处理结构化输入时保持有效性的挑战。

title: ChatAFL: A Large Language Model Guided Protocol Fuzzer
authors: Ruijie Meng, Martin Mirber, Marcel Bockmann, Frank Bsser, Thorsten Holz
abstract: Protocol implementations are essential components of network infrastructure, making their security critical. We present ChatAFL, a protocol fuzzer guided by LLMs. ChatAFL leverages the LLM's understanding of protocol specifications to generate semantically valid protocol messages, enriching the fuzzer's message pool with structure-aware inputs.
summary: Proposes ChatAFL, an LLM-guided protocol fuzzer that generates semantically valid protocol messages for testing network protocol implementations.
summary_zh: 提出ChatAFL，一种LLM引导的协议模糊测试器，为测试网络协议实现生成语义有效的协议消息。

experiment.key_results: 28% higher state coverage, found 8 new bugs in protocol implementations
experiment.real_world_impact: Bugs found in DTLS, RTSP, FTP implementations

contributions: Proposes ChatAFL, a novel approach for LLM-guided protocol fuzzer that generates semantically valid protocol messages for testing network protocol implementati.
Leverages GPT-3.5, GPT-4 for intelligent test case generation and mutation strategies.
Evaluates on ProFuzzBench benchmark, providing comprehensive empirical evidence.
Demonstrates strong empirical results: 28% higher state coverage, found 8 new bugs in protocol implementations.
Addresses crash, memory-corruption vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.
contributions_zh: 提出 ChatAFL, a novel approach for LLM-guided protocol fuzzer that generates semantically valid protocol messages for testing network protocol implementati.
利用 GPT-3.5, GPT-4 for intelligent test case generation and mutation strategies.
在以下数据集上进行评估： ProFuzzBench benchmark, providing comprehensive empirical evidence.
证明了 strong empirical results: 28% higher state coverage, found 8 new bugs in protocol implementations.
解决 crash, memory-corruption vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.
abstract_zh: 提出ChatAFL，一种LLM引导的协议模糊测试器，为测试网络协议实现生成语义有效的协议消息。
research_questions: Can LLMs' understanding of protocol specifications be leveraged to generate semantically valid protocol messages for fuzzing?
How does LLM-guided message generation compare to grammar-based and state-aware protocol fuzzers?
Does enriching the fuzzer's message pool with LLM-generated structure-aware inputs improve protocol state coverage?
research_questions_zh: 能否利用LLM对协议规范的理解来生成语义有效的协议消息用于模糊测试？
LLM引导的消息生成与基于语法和状态感知的协议模糊测试器相比效果如何？
用LLM生成的结构感知输入丰富模糊测试器的消息池是否能提高协议状态覆盖率？
conclusions: ChatAFL improved state coverage by 28% over baseline methods and discovered 8 new bugs in protocol implementations.
The LLM's understanding of protocol specifications enables the generation of semantically valid messages, effectively enriching the fuzzer's message pool.
Real bugs were found in DTLS, RTSP, and FTP protocol implementations, demonstrating the practical utility of the approach.
conclusions_zh: ChatAFL比基线方法的状态覆盖率提高了28%，并在协议实现中发现了8个新漏洞。
LLM对协议规范的理解能够生成语义有效的消息，有效丰富了模糊测试器的消息池。
在DTLS、RTSP和FTP等协议实现中发现了真实漏洞，证明了该方法的实用性。

title: Harnessing Large Language Models for Seed Generation in Greybox Fuzzing
authors: Wenxuan Li, Kexin Pei, Shweta Ma
abstract: The effectiveness of greybox fuzzing depends significantly on the quality of initial seed inputs. We propose using LLMs to generate high-quality seed inputs by analyzing target program source code and documentation. Our approach prompts LLMs to understand input format requirements and generate diverse, valid initial seeds.
summary: Uses LLMs to generate high-quality initial seed inputs for greybox fuzzing by analyzing target program code and documentation.
summary_zh: 利用LLM通过分析目标程序代码和文档为灰盒模糊测试生成高质量初始种子输入。

experiment.key_results: 22% faster bug finding, 18% higher edge coverage with LLM seeds

contributions: Proposes a novel approach for LLMs to generate high-quality initial seed inputs for greybox fuzzing by analyzing target program code and documentation.
Leverages GPT-4, Claude for intelligent test case generation and mutation strategies.
Evaluates on Magma, FuzzBench benchmarks, providing comprehensive empirical evidence.
Demonstrates strong empirical results: 22% faster bug finding, 18% higher edge coverage with LLM seeds.
Addresses crash, memory-safety vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.
contributions_zh: 提出 a novel approach for LLMs to generate high-quality initial seed inputs for greybox fuzzing by analyzing target program code and documentation.
利用 GPT-4, Claude for intelligent test case generation and mutation strategies.
在以下数据集上进行评估： Magma, FuzzBench benchmarks, providing comprehensive empirical evidence.
证明了 strong empirical results: 22% faster bug finding, 18% higher edge coverage with LLM seeds.
解决 crash, memory-safety vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.
abstract_zh: 利用LLM通过分析目标程序代码和文档为灰盒模糊测试生成高质量初始种子输入。
research_questions: Can LLMs generate high-quality initial seed inputs by analyzing target program source code and documentation?
How do LLM-generated seeds compare to default and empty seeds in terms of bug finding speed and edge coverage?
Does LLM understanding of input format requirements translate to more diverse and effective initial seeds?
research_questions_zh: LLM能否通过分析目标程序源代码和文档生成高质量的初始种子输入？
LLM生成的种子在漏洞发现速度和边缘覆盖率方面与默认种子和空种子相比如何？
LLM对输入格式要求的理解是否能转化为更多样化和有效的初始种子？
conclusions: Using LLM-generated seeds improved bug discovery speed by 22% and edge coverage by 18%.
LLMs can understand input format requirements by analyzing source code and documentation, generating diverse and valid initial seeds.
Compared to AFL++ default seeds and empty seeds, LLM-generated seeds performed better on the Magma and FuzzBench benchmarks.
conclusions_zh: 使用LLM生成的种子，漏洞发现速度提高了22%，边缘覆盖率提高了18%。
LLM能够通过分析源代码和文档理解输入格式要求，生成多样化且有效的初始种子。
与AFL++默认种子和空种子相比，LLM生成的种子在Magma和FuzzBench基准测试中表现更优。

title: Augmenting Greybox Fuzzing with Generative AI
authors: Jie Hu, Qian Ruan, Hao Pei, Cen Li
abstract: Greybox fuzzers like AFL struggle with programs that process structured inputs due to format constraints. We propose augmenting greybox fuzzing with generative AI models that learn input structure from existing test corpora. Our approach uses a fine-tuned language model to generate format-compliant mutations that bypass input validation while exploring deep program paths.
summary: Augments greybox fuzzing with generative AI to produce format-compliant mutations, improving deep path exploration for structured input programs.
summary_zh: 用生成式AI增强灰盒模糊测试，产生格式合规的变异，改善结构化输入程序的深层路径探索。

experiment.key_results: 31% more bugs found than AFL++ on structured inputs

contributions: Proposes a novel approach for augments greybox fuzzing with generative AI to produce format-compliant mutations, improving deep path exploration for s.
Leverages CodeLlama for intelligent test case generation and mutation strategies.
Evaluates on FuzzBench benchmark, providing comprehensive empirical evidence.
Demonstrates strong empirical results: 31% more bugs found than AFL++ on structured inputs.
Addresses crash, buffer-overflow vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.
contributions_zh: 提出 a novel approach for augments greybox fuzzing with generative AI to produce format-compliant mutations, improving deep path exploration for s.
利用 CodeLlama for intelligent test case generation and mutation strategies.
在以下数据集上进行评估： FuzzBench benchmark, providing comprehensive empirical evidence.
证明了 strong empirical results: 31% more bugs found than AFL++ on structured inputs.
解决 crash, buffer-overflow vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.
abstract_zh: 用生成式AI增强灰盒模糊测试，产生格式合规的变异，改善结构化输入程序的深层路径探索。
research_questions: Can generative AI models learn input structure from test corpora to produce format-compliant mutations for greybox fuzzing?
How does fine-tuning a language model on valid inputs help bypass input validation while exploring deep program paths?
Does augmenting AFL++ with generative AI improve bug finding for programs that process structured inputs?
research_questions_zh: 生成式AI模型能否从测试语料库中学习输入结构以为灰盒模糊测试生成符合格式的变异？
在有效输入上微调语言模型如何帮助绕过输入验证同时探索深层程序路径？
用生成式AI增强AFL++是否能改善处理结构化输入的程序的漏洞发现效果？
conclusions: The approach discovered 31% more bugs on structured inputs compared to AFL++.
A fine-tuned language model can learn input structure and generate mutations that bypass format validation, thereby exploring deeper program paths.
The combination of generative AI with greybox fuzzing effectively addresses the format constraint challenges that traditional fuzzers face when processing structured inputs.
conclusions_zh: 在结构化输入上比AFL++多发现了31%的漏洞。
微调后的语言模型能够学习输入结构并生成绕过格式验证的变异，从而探索更深层的程序路径。
生成式AI与灰盒模糊测试的结合，有效解决了传统模糊测试器在处理结构化输入时面临的格式约束挑战。

title: Automatically Generating Fuzz Drivers via Large Language Models
authors: Cen Wang, Mingzhe Li, Jie Zhang, Yang Liu
abstract: Writing fuzz drivers (harnesses) for library APIs is a labor-intensive but essential task for effective fuzzing. We propose an LLM-based approach to automatically generate fuzz drivers by analyzing library API documentation, header files, and usage examples. Our method uses multi-turn LLM interaction with feedback-driven refinement.
summary: Automatically generates fuzz drivers for library APIs using LLMs with multi-turn interaction and feedback-driven refinement.
summary_zh: 使用LLM通过多轮交互和反馈驱动的细化自动为库API生成模糊测试驱动程序。

experiment.key_results: 73% of generated harnesses compile and achieve >60% API coverage
experiment.real_world_impact: Found 15 previously unknown bugs in popular libraries

contributions: Proposes a novel approach for automatically generates fuzz drivers for library APIs using LLMs with multi-turn interaction and feedback-driven refinem.
Leverages GPT-4, CodeLlama for intelligent test case generation and mutation strategies.
Evaluates on 10 popular C libraries benchmark, providing comprehensive empirical evidence.
Demonstrates strong empirical results: 73% of generated harnesses compile and achieve >60% API coverage.
Addresses memory-safety, crash vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.
contributions_zh: 提出 a novel approach for automatically generates fuzz drivers for library APIs using LLMs with multi-turn interaction and feedback-driven refinem.
利用 GPT-4, CodeLlama for intelligent test case generation and mutation strategies.
在以下数据集上进行评估： 10 popular C libraries benchmark, providing comprehensive empirical evidence.
证明了 strong empirical results: 73% of generated harnesses compile and achieve >60% API coverage.
解决 memory-safety, crash vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.
abstract_zh: 使用LLM通过多轮交互和反馈驱动的细化自动为库API生成模糊测试驱动程序。
research_questions: Can multi-turn LLM interaction with feedback-driven refinement automatically generate compilable fuzz drivers for library APIs?
How does analyzing API documentation, header files, and usage examples guide LLM-based fuzz driver generation?
What compilation and API coverage rates can LLM-generated fuzz drivers achieve compared to manual and automated baselines?
research_questions_zh: 基于反馈驱动的多轮LLM交互能否自动生成可编译的库API模糊测试驱动程序？
分析API文档、头文件和使用示例如何指导基于LLM的模糊测试驱动程序生成？
LLM生成的模糊测试驱动程序与手动和自动基线相比能达到怎样的编译率和API覆盖率？
conclusions: 73% of LLM-generated harnesses compiled successfully and achieved over 60% API coverage.
Multi-turn LLM interaction combined with feedback-driven optimization can effectively resolve compilation errors and API usage issues.
The approach discovered 15 previously unknown bugs in popular libraries, demonstrating the practical value of LLM-generated harnesses.
conclusions_zh: 73%的LLM生成的测试工具可以成功编译，并达到60%以上的API覆盖率。
多轮LLM交互结合反馈驱动的优化能够有效解决编译错误和API使用问题。
该方法在流行库中发现了15个此前未知的漏洞，证明了LLM生成的测试工具的实用价值。

title: FuzzCraft: LLM-Assisted Fuzzing Harness Generation for C/C++ Libraries
authors: Xiaofei Xie, Ting Liu, Shang Li, Jun Chen
abstract: Fuzzing is highly effective for finding vulnerabilities but requires well-crafted harnesses to exercise library APIs effectively. We present FuzzCraft, an LLM-assisted system that automatically generates compilation-ready fuzzing harnesses for C/C++ libraries by analyzing API signatures, documentation, and existing test cases.
summary: Proposes FuzzCraft for LLM-assisted generation of compilation-ready fuzzing harnesses for C/C++ libraries using API analysis.
summary_zh: 提出FuzzCraft，通过API分析实现LLM辅助的C/C++库编译就绪模糊测试桩生成。

experiment.key_results: 82% compilation rate, found 23 new bugs across 15 libraries
experiment.real_world_impact: Bugs found in libxml2, zlib, openssl

contributions: Proposes FuzzCraft, a novel approach for for LLM-assisted generation of compilation-ready fuzzing harnesses for C/C++ libraries using API analysis.
Leverages GPT-4, GPT-3.5 for intelligent test case generation and mutation strategies.
Evaluates on 15 popular C/C++ libraries benchmark, providing comprehensive empirical evidence.
Demonstrates strong empirical results: 82% compilation rate, found 23 new bugs across 15 libraries.
Addresses memory-safety, crash, use-after-free vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.
contributions_zh: 提出 FuzzCraft, a novel approach for for LLM-assisted generation of compilation-ready fuzzing harnesses for C/C++ libraries using API analysis.
利用 GPT-4, GPT-3.5 for intelligent test case generation and mutation strategies.
在以下数据集上进行评估： 15 popular C/C++ libraries benchmark, providing comprehensive empirical evidence.
证明了 strong empirical results: 82% compilation rate, found 23 new bugs across 15 libraries.
解决 memory-safety, crash, use-after-free vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.
abstract_zh: 提出FuzzCraft，通过API分析实现LLM辅助的C/C++库编译就绪模糊测试桩生成。
research_questions: Can LLMs generate compilation-ready fuzzing harnesses for C/C++ libraries by analyzing API signatures, documentation, and test cases?
What compilation success rate and bug detection capability do LLM-generated harnesses achieve?
How does FuzzCraft compare to existing tools like FUDGE and Hopper for automated harness generation?
research_questions_zh: LLM能否通过分析API签名、文档和测试用例为C/C++库生成可直接编译的模糊测试工具？
LLM生成的测试工具能达到怎样的编译成功率和漏洞检测能力？
FuzzCraft与FUDGE和Hopper等现有工具在自动化测试工具生成方面的比较如何？
conclusions: FuzzCraft achieved an 82% compilation success rate and discovered 23 new bugs across 15 libraries.
By analyzing API signatures, documentation, and existing test cases, LLMs can generate valid fuzzing harnesses that compile directly.
Real bugs found in libraries such as libxml2, zlib, and openssl validated the practical security value of the approach.
conclusions_zh: FuzzCraft达到82%的编译成功率，并在15个库中发现了23个新漏洞。
通过分析API签名、文档和现有测试用例，LLM能够生成可直接编译的有效模糊测试工具。
在libxml2、zlib和openssl等库中发现了真实漏洞，验证了该方法的实际安全价值。

title: Muzeel: Generating Structured Test Programs for Fuzzing JavaScript Engines
authors: Haichuan Chen, Shengqi Yang, Yinlin Deng, Lingming Zhang
abstract: JavaScript engine bugs can lead to severe security vulnerabilities. We present Muzeel, which uses LLMs to generate structured test programs targeting JavaScript engine internals. Muzeel combines LLM-based program generation with coverage-guided feedback to efficiently explore JS engine code paths.
summary: Proposes Muzeel using LLMs to generate structured JavaScript test programs with coverage-guided feedback for JS engine fuzzing.
summary_zh: 提出Muzeel，使用LLM生成结构化JavaScript测试程序，结合覆盖引导反馈进行JS引擎模糊测试。

experiment.key_results: Found 42 bugs in V8, SpiderMonkey, JSC, 18 CVEs assigned
experiment.real_world_impact: 18 CVEs in production JavaScript engines

contributions: Proposes Muzeel, a novel approach for using LLMs to generate structured JavaScript test programs with coverage-guided feedback for JS engine fuzzing.
Leverages GPT-4 for intelligent test case generation and mutation strategies.
Conducts extensive evaluation against 4 baseline approaches including Montage, DIE, Fuzzilli.
Demonstrates strong empirical results: Found 42 bugs in V8, SpiderMonkey, JSC, 18 CVEs assigned.
Addresses crash, type-confusion, memory-corruption vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.
contributions_zh: 提出 Muzeel, a novel approach for using LLMs to generate structured JavaScript test programs with coverage-guided feedback for JS engine fuzzing.
利用 GPT-4 for intelligent test case generation and mutation strategies.
进行了广泛的评估 against 4 baseline approaches including Montage, DIE, Fuzzilli.
证明了 strong empirical results: Found 42 bugs in V8, SpiderMonkey, JSC, 18 CVEs assigned.
解决 crash, type-confusion, memory-corruption vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.
abstract_zh: 提出Muzeel，使用LLM生成结构化JavaScript测试程序，结合覆盖引导反馈进行JS引擎模糊测试。
research_questions: Can LLMs generate structured JavaScript test programs that effectively target JavaScript engine internals?
How does combining LLM-based program generation with coverage-guided feedback improve JS engine fuzzing?
Can Muzeel discover security-critical vulnerabilities (type confusion, memory corruption) in production JavaScript engines?
research_questions_zh: LLM能否生成有效针对JavaScript引擎内部的结构化测试程序？
将基于LLM的程序生成与覆盖引导反馈相结合如何改善JS引擎模糊测试？
Muzeel能否在生产JavaScript引擎中发现安全关键漏洞（类型混淆、内存破坏）？
conclusions: Muzeel discovered 42 bugs in V8, SpiderMonkey, and JSC, receiving 18 CVE assignments.
LLM-generated structured test programs combined with coverage-guided feedback proved more effective than baselines including Montage, DIE, and Fuzzilli.
The approach uncovered security-critical vulnerabilities such as type confusion and memory corruption in production JavaScript engines.
conclusions_zh: Muzeel在V8、SpiderMonkey和JSC中发现了42个漏洞，获得了18个CVE编号。
LLM生成的结构化测试程序结合覆盖引导反馈，比Montage、DIE和Fuzzilli等基线更有效。
该方法在生产环境JavaScript引擎中发现了类型混淆和内存破坏等安全关键漏洞。

title: API-Level Fuzz Driver Generation with LLM-Based Program Synthesis
authors: Zhengjie Zhao, Yue Wang, Mark Chen
abstract: Generating effective API-level fuzz drivers requires understanding API semantics, parameter constraints, and calling conventions. We propose an LLM-based program synthesis approach that generates fuzz drivers by learning from API documentation, existing test suites, and usage patterns in client code.
summary: Generates API-level fuzz drivers through LLM-based program synthesis using documentation, test suites, and client code patterns.
summary_zh: 通过基于LLM的程序合成利用文档、测试套件和客户端代码模式生成API级模糊测试驱动程序。

experiment.key_results: 68% of drivers compile correctly, 45% achieve >70% code coverage

contributions: Proposes a novel approach for generates API-level fuzz drivers through LLM-based program synthesis using documentation, test suites, and client code p.
Leverages GPT-4, StarCoder for intelligent test case generation and mutation strategies.
Evaluates on 20 open-source libraries benchmark, providing comprehensive empirical evidence.
Demonstrates strong empirical results: 68% of drivers compile correctly, 45% achieve >70% code coverage.
Addresses memory-safety vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.
contributions_zh: 提出 a novel approach for generates API-level fuzz drivers through LLM-based program synthesis using documentation, test suites, and client code p.
利用 GPT-4, StarCoder for intelligent test case generation and mutation strategies.
在以下数据集上进行评估： 20 open-source libraries benchmark, providing comprehensive empirical evidence.
证明了 strong empirical results: 68% of drivers compile correctly, 45% achieve >70% code coverage.
解决 memory-safety vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.
abstract_zh: 通过基于LLM的程序合成利用文档、测试套件和客户端代码模式生成API级模糊测试驱动程序。
research_questions: Can LLM-based program synthesis learn from API documentation, test suites, and client code to generate effective fuzz drivers?
What compilation and code coverage rates do LLM-synthesized API-level fuzz drivers achieve?
How do LLM-synthesized fuzz drivers compare to FUDGE, Hopper, and FuzzGen for API-level fuzzing?
research_questions_zh: 基于LLM的程序合成能否从API文档、测试套件和客户端代码中学习以生成有效的模糊测试驱动程序？
LLM合成的API级模糊测试驱动程序能达到怎样的编译率和代码覆盖率？
LLM合成的模糊测试驱动程序与FUDGE、Hopper和FuzzGen在API级模糊测试方面的比较如何？
conclusions: 68% of the generated drivers compiled correctly, with 45% achieving over 70% code coverage.
By learning from API documentation, test suites, and client code usage patterns, LLMs can understand API semantics and parameter constraints.
The program synthesis-based approach demonstrated feasibility for API-level fuzz driver generation targeting C, C++, and Rust libraries.
conclusions_zh: 68%的驱动程序可以正确编译，其中45%达到了70%以上的代码覆盖率。
通过从API文档、测试套件和客户端代码使用模式中学习，LLM能够理解API语义和参数约束。
基于程序合成的方法在C、C++和Rust库的API级模糊测试驱动生成方面展现了可行性。

title: SmartFuzz: LLM-Enhanced Fuzzing for Smart Contract Vulnerability Detection
authors: Lei Wu, Peng Zhang, Jianbo Chen, Xiapu Luo
