将LLM创造力与程序分析精确性相结合比单独使用LLM提高多少修复性能？
哪些类型的补丁在基于LLM的修复过程中最受益于补全引擎的指导？
conclusions: Repilot fixed 24% more bugs than Codex alone by fusing LLM generation with completion engine guidance for syntactic and type correctness.
The fusion approach effectively combines LLM creativity in generating plausible patches with the precision of program analysis for validity.
Completion engine guidance is most beneficial for patches requiring correct API usage and type-aware code modifications.
conclusions_zh: Repilot通过将LLM生成与补全引擎指导融合以确保语法和类型正确性，比单独使用Codex多修复了24%的漏洞。
融合方法有效结合了LLM在生成合理补丁方面的创造力与程序分析在有效性方面的精确性。
补全引擎指导对需要正确API使用和类型感知代码修改的补丁最为有益。

title: VulRepair: A T5-Based Automated Software Vulnerability Repair
authors: Michael Fu, Chakkrit Tantithamthavorn
abstract: Security vulnerabilities require timely patches, but manual repair is slow. We present VulRepair, a T5-based model fine-tuned specifically for vulnerability repair. By training on a curated dataset of vulnerability-patch pairs from real-world CVEs, VulRepair learns to generate security patches.
summary: Proposes VulRepair, a T5-based model fine-tuned on CVE-patch pairs for automated vulnerability repair, achieving 44% exact-match accuracy.
summary_zh: 提出VulRepair，基于T5模型在CVE补丁对上微调用于自动漏洞修复，实现44%的精确匹配准确率。

experiment.key_results: 44% exact-match accuracy on vulnerability repair

contributions: Proposes VulRepair, a T5-based model fine-tuned specifically on real-world CVE vulnerability-patch pairs for automated vulnerability repair.
Curates a training dataset of 8,482 vulnerability-patch pairs from CVEFixes and Big-Vul for security-specific model training.
Achieves 44% exact-match accuracy on vulnerability repair, outperforming CodeBERT, GraphCodeBERT, and SequenceR.
Analyzes performance across different CWE categories to identify vulnerability types where the model excels or struggles.
contributions_zh: 提出VulRepair，一个在真实CVE漏洞-补丁对上专门微调的T5模型，用于自动化漏洞修复。
从CVEFixes和Big-Vul中精选8,482个漏洞-补丁对的训练数据集用于安全特定模型训练。
在漏洞修复上达到44%的精确匹配准确率，优于CodeBERT、GraphCodeBERT和SequenceR。
分析不同CWE类别的性能，识别模型擅长或不足的漏洞类型。
abstract_zh: 提出VulRepair，基于T5模型在CVE补丁对上微调用于自动漏洞修复，实现44%的精确匹配准确率。
research_questions: Can a T5-based model fine-tuned on vulnerability-patch pairs learn to generate correct security patches?
How does fine-tuning on curated CVE data compare to general code models for vulnerability repair?
What is the exact-match accuracy achievable by a specialized vulnerability repair model on real-world CVEs?
research_questions_zh: 在漏洞-补丁对上微调的T5模型能否学会生成正确的安全补丁？
在精选CVE数据上微调与通用代码模型在漏洞修复方面相比如何？
专门的漏洞修复模型在真实CVE上可达到的精确匹配准确率是多少？
conclusions: VulRepair achieves 44% exact-match accuracy on vulnerability repair, outperforming CodeBERT and GraphCodeBERT baselines.
Fine-tuning T5 on a curated dataset of 8,482 real-world CVE vulnerability-patch pairs enables effective security patch generation.
The model performs best on CWE-119 and CWE-20 vulnerability types but struggles with more complex CWE-125 patterns.
conclusions_zh: VulRepair在漏洞修复上达到44%的精确匹配准确率，优于CodeBERT和GraphCodeBERT基线。
在8,482个真实CVE漏洞-补丁对的精选数据集上微调T5可实现有效的安全补丁生成。
模型在CWE-119和CWE-20漏洞类型上表现最好，但在更复杂的CWE-125模式上表现欠佳。

title: SRepair: Semantic-Level Automated Program Repair via Large Language Models
authors: Yuntong Huang, Chunqiu Steven Xia, Lingming Zhang
abstract: Existing APR approaches operate at the syntactic level, limiting their ability to fix complex bugs requiring semantic understanding. We present SRepair, which leverages LLMs for semantic-level program repair. SRepair uses semantic analysis to understand bug root causes before prompting the LLM for fixes.
summary: Proposes SRepair for semantic-level program repair using LLMs with root cause analysis, fixing complex multi-location bugs.
summary_zh: 提出SRepair，利用LLM进行语义级程序修复，结合根因分析修复复杂的多位置漏洞。

experiment.key_results: Fixed 178/395 bugs, 10% more than ChatRepair

contributions: Proposes SRepair, which leverages semantic analysis to understand bug root causes before prompting LLMs for patch generation.
Fixes 178 of 395 bugs on Defects4J, improving 10% over ChatRepair through root cause understanding.
Demonstrates that semantic-level repair outperforms syntactic-level approaches for complex multi-location bugs.
Introduces a root cause analysis pipeline that guides GPT-4 to generate more targeted security patches.
contributions_zh: 提出SRepair，在提示LLM生成补丁之前利用语义分析理解漏洞根本原因。
在Defects4J上修复395个漏洞中的178个，通过根本原因理解比ChatRepair提高10%。
证明语义级修复在复杂多位置漏洞上优于语法级方法。
引入根本原因分析流水线，指导GPT-4生成更有针对性的安全补丁。
abstract_zh: 提出SRepair，利用LLM进行语义级程序修复，结合根因分析修复复杂的多位置漏洞。
research_questions: Can semantic-level understanding of bug root causes improve LLM-based program repair over syntactic approaches?
How does root cause analysis before patch generation affect repair correctness for complex bugs?
How does SRepair compare to ChatRepair and other conversational repair approaches?
research_questions_zh: 对漏洞根本原因的语义级理解能否改善基于LLM的程序修复相比语法方法？
在补丁生成前进行根本原因分析如何影响复杂漏洞的修复正确性？
SRepair与ChatRepair和其他对话式修复方法相比表现如何？
conclusions: SRepair fixed 178 out of 395 bugs on Defects4J, 10% more than ChatRepair, by leveraging semantic understanding of bug root causes.
Semantic-level analysis of bug root causes before LLM prompting leads to more targeted and correct patches for complex bugs.
Understanding the why behind a bug, not just the what, enables LLMs to generate fixes that address underlying issues rather than surface symptoms.
conclusions_zh: SRepair通过利用对漏洞根本原因的语义理解，在Defects4J上修复了395个漏洞中的178个，比ChatRepair多10%。
在LLM提示之前对漏洞根本原因进行语义级分析可为复杂漏洞生成更有针对性和正确的补丁。
理解漏洞背后的原因而不仅是表象，使LLM能够生成解决根本问题而非表面症状的修复。

title: ITER: Iterative Neural Repair for Multi-Location Bugs
authors: He Ye, Martin Monperrus
abstract: Multi-location bugs require coordinated changes across multiple code locations, which is challenging for existing APR tools. We present ITER, an iterative repair approach that uses LLMs to fix multi-location bugs one location at a time, using test feedback to guide subsequent repairs.
summary: Proposes ITER for iterative repair of multi-location bugs using LLMs with test feedback to guide sequential fixes.
summary_zh: 提出ITER，利用LLM通过测试反馈引导顺序修复，迭代修复多位置漏洞。

experiment.key_results: Fixed 45% of multi-location bugs vs 18% for single-shot approaches

contributions: Proposes ITER, an iterative approach that fixes multi-location bugs one location at a time using test feedback to guide subsequent repairs.
Fixes 45% of multi-location bugs versus only 18% for single-shot approaches on Defects4J and BugsInPy.
Uses both GPT-4 and CodeLlama to evaluate iterative repair across 300 multi-location bugs.
Demonstrates that test-guided iterative repair enables coordinated multi-location changes that single-shot approaches cannot achieve.
contributions_zh: 提出ITER，一种迭代方法，使用测试反馈指导后续修复，逐个位置修复多位置漏洞。
在Defects4J和BugsInPy上修复45%的多位置漏洞，而单次方法仅为18%。
使用GPT-4和CodeLlama在300个多位置漏洞上评估迭代修复。
证明测试引导的迭代修复能够实现单次方法无法达到的协调多位置更改。
abstract_zh: 提出ITER，利用LLM通过测试反馈引导顺序修复，迭代修复多位置漏洞。
research_questions: Can an iterative repair strategy effectively fix multi-location bugs that require coordinated changes?
How does test feedback between iterations guide the LLM toward correct multi-location fixes?
How much does iterative one-at-a-time repair improve over single-shot approaches for multi-location bugs?
research_questions_zh: 迭代修复策略能否有效修复需要协调更改的多位置漏洞？
迭代之间的测试反馈如何指导LLM实现正确的多位置修复？
对于多位置漏洞，迭代逐一修复比单次方法提高多少？
conclusions: ITER fixed 45% of multi-location bugs compared to only 18% for single-shot approaches, demonstrating the value of iterative repair.
Test feedback between repair iterations enables the LLM to understand the impact of each fix and guide subsequent repairs appropriately.
Iterative one-location-at-a-time repair with test validation between steps is significantly more effective than attempting all fixes at once.
conclusions_zh: ITER修复了45%的多位置漏洞，而单次方法仅为18%，展示了迭代修复的价值。
修复迭代之间的测试反馈使LLM能够理解每个修复的影响并适当指导后续修复。
在步骤之间进行测试验证的逐位置迭代修复显著优于一次性尝试所有修复。

title: RepairLlama: Efficient Representation Learning for Fine-Tuned Code Repair Models
authors: Andre Silva, Martin Monperrus
abstract: While large commercial LLMs show strong repair capabilities, open-source alternatives lag behind. We present RepairLlama, a fine-tuned Llama model specifically for program repair. Through parameter-efficient fine-tuning on curated bug-fix datasets, RepairLlama achieves competitive performance with GPT-3.5.
summary: Fine-tunes Llama for program repair using LoRA, achieving competitive performance with GPT-3.5 at much lower cost.
summary_zh: 使用LoRA微调Llama用于程序修复，以更低成本达到与GPT-3.5相当的性能。

experiment.key_results: Matches GPT-3.5 on Defects4J at 10x lower cost

contributions: Proposes RepairLlama, a Llama-2 model fine-tuned via LoRA specifically for program repair that matches GPT-3.5 at 10x lower cost.
Demonstrates that parameter-efficient fine-tuning on curated bug-fix datasets (Defects4J, MegaDiff) enables competitive open-source repair models.
Provides a cost-effective alternative to commercial LLMs for automated program repair through specialized fine-tuning.
Compares against GPT-3.5, Codex, InCoder, and AlphaRepair across 337 bugs on Defects4J.
contributions_zh: 提出RepairLlama，一个通过LoRA专门为程序修复微调的Llama-2模型，以10倍更低的成本匹配GPT-3.5。
证明在精选漏洞修复数据集（Defects4J、MegaDiff）上的参数高效微调可实现有竞争力的开源修复模型。
通过专业微调为自动程序修复提供商业LLM的经济替代方案。
在Defects4J的337个漏洞上与GPT-3.5、Codex、InCoder和AlphaRepair进行比较。
abstract_zh: 使用LoRA微调Llama用于程序修复，以更低成本达到与GPT-3.5相当的性能。
research_questions: Can parameter-efficient fine-tuning of open-source Llama models achieve competitive repair performance with commercial GPT-3.5?
What is the cost-performance trade-off between fine-tuned open-source models and commercial LLMs for program repair?
How effective is LoRA fine-tuning on curated bug-fix datasets for specialized repair capability?
research_questions_zh: 开源Llama模型的参数高效微调能否达到与商业GPT-3.5竞争的修复性能？
微调开源模型与商业LLM在程序修复方面的成本-性能权衡是什么？
在精选漏洞修复数据集上的LoRA微调对专业修复能力的效果如何？
conclusions: RepairLlama matches GPT-3.5 repair performance on Defects4J at 10x lower cost through LoRA fine-tuning of Llama-2 13B.
Parameter-efficient fine-tuning on curated bug-fix datasets enables open-source models to close the gap with commercial LLMs for repair.
LoRA fine-tuning provides an effective path to specialized repair models without the cost of full model fine-tuning or commercial API access.
conclusions_zh: RepairLlama通过对Llama-2 13B的LoRA微调，以10倍更低的成本在Defects4J上达到与GPT-3.5匹配的修复性能。
在精选漏洞修复数据集上的参数高效微调使开源模型能够缩小与商业LLM在修复方面的差距。
LoRA微调提供了一条通往专业修复模型的有效路径，无需全模型微调或商业API访问的成本。

title: InferFix: End-to-End Program Repair with LLMs over Retrieval-Augmented Prompts
authors: Matthew Jin, Syed Shahriar, Michele Tufano, Xin Shi, Shuai Lu, Neel Sundaresan, Alexey Svyatkovskiy
abstract: We present InferFix, an end-to-end program repair system that combines static analysis with retrieval-augmented LLM generation. InferFix uses Infer's static analyzer to identify bugs, retrieves similar bug-fix pairs, and prompts an LLM with relevant context to generate patches.
summary: Proposes InferFix combining Infer static analysis with retrieval-augmented LLM patch generation for end-to-end program repair.
summary_zh: 提出InferFix，结合Infer静态分析与检索增强的LLM补丁生成，实现端到端程序修复。

experiment.key_results: 65% of generated patches pass validation, 40% exact match
experiment.real_world_impact: Deployed in Microsoft developer workflow

contributions: Proposes InferFix, an end-to-end repair system combining Infer static analysis with retrieval-augmented LLM patch generation.
Uses retrieval of similar historical bug-fix pairs to augment LLM prompts with relevant repair context.
Achieves 65% patch validation rate and 40% exact match across 10,000+ Infer warnings.
Deployed in Microsoft developer workflow, demonstrating practical applicability of the approach.
contributions_zh: 提出InferFix，一个结合Infer静态分析与检索增强LLM补丁生成的端到端修复系统。
使用检索相似的历史漏洞修复对来增强LLM提示，提供相关修复上下文。
在10,000+个Infer警告上达到65%的补丁验证率和40%的精确匹配率。
已部署在微软开发工作流中，展示了该方法的实际适用性。
abstract_zh: 提出InferFix，结合Infer静态分析与检索增强的LLM补丁生成，实现端到端程序修复。
research_questions: Can combining static analysis bug detection with retrieval-augmented LLM patch generation create an effective end-to-end repair pipeline?
How does retrieval of similar bug-fix pairs improve LLM patch generation compared to zero-shot prompting?
What is the patch validation and exact-match rate of the combined Infer + LLM approach?
research_questions_zh: 将静态分析漏洞检测与检索增强的LLM补丁生成相结合能否创建有效的端到端修复流水线？
检索相似的漏洞修复对比零样本提示如何改善LLM补丁生成？
结合Infer和LLM的方法的补丁验证率和精确匹配率是多少？
conclusions: InferFix achieves 65% patch validation rate and 40% exact match by combining Infer static analysis with retrieval-augmented LLM generation.
Retrieving similar bug-fix pairs to augment the LLM prompt significantly improves patch quality over zero-shot generation.
The end-to-end pipeline from static analysis bug detection to LLM patch generation has been deployed in Microsoft developer workflow.
conclusions_zh: InferFix通过将Infer静态分析与检索增强的LLM生成相结合，达到65%的补丁验证率和40%的精确匹配率。
检索相似的漏洞修复对来增强LLM提示显著提高了补丁质量，优于零样本生成。
从静态分析漏洞检测到LLM补丁生成的端到端流水线已部署在微软开发工作流中。

title: AutoCodeRover: Autonomous Program Improvement via LLM Agents
authors: Yuntong Zhang, Haifeng Ruan, Zhiyu Fan, Abhik Roychoudhury
abstract: We present AutoCodeRover, an autonomous LLM agent for program improvement. Unlike prior approaches that focus on single-hunk patches, AutoCodeRover navigates codebases autonomously, identifies relevant code contexts, and generates multi-file patches using an iterative agent-based approach.
summary: Proposes AutoCodeRover, an autonomous LLM agent that navigates codebases and generates multi-file patches for program improvement.
summary_zh: 提出AutoCodeRover，一个自主导航代码库并生成多文件补丁的LLM代理，用于程序改进。

experiment.key_results: Resolved 22% of SWE-bench issues, SOTA at time of publication
experiment.real_world_impact: Resolves real GitHub issues autonomously

contributions: Proposes AutoCodeRover, an autonomous LLM agent that navigates codebases, identifies relevant contexts, and generates multi-file patches.
Resolves 22% of SWE-bench issues, setting state-of-the-art for autonomous program improvement at publication time.
Introduces an iterative agent-based approach that autonomously decides what code to examine and how to generate patches.
Open-source implementation evaluated against SWE-agent, Devin, and RAG baselines on 2,294 GitHub issues.
contributions_zh: 提出AutoCodeRover，一个自主LLM代理，可导航代码库、识别相关上下文并生成多文件补丁。
解决22%的SWE-bench问题，在发表时为自主程序改进设定了最先进水平。
引入迭代代理方法，自主决定检查哪些代码以及如何生成补丁。
开源实现在2,294个GitHub问题上与SWE-agent、Devin和RAG基线进行评估。
abstract_zh: 提出AutoCodeRover，一个自主导航代码库并生成多文件补丁的LLM代理，用于程序改进。
research_questions: Can an autonomous LLM agent navigate large codebases to identify relevant contexts and generate multi-file patches?
How does an iterative agent-based approach compare to fixed-pipeline approaches for resolving complex GitHub issues?
What percentage of real-world SWE-bench issues can be autonomously resolved by an LLM agent?
research_questions_zh: 自主LLM代理能否在大型代码库中导航以识别相关上下文并生成多文件补丁？
迭代代理方法与固定流水线方法在解决复杂GitHub问题方面相比如何？
LLM代理能够自主解决多大比例的真实SWE-bench问题？
conclusions: AutoCodeRover resolved 22% of SWE-bench issues, achieving state-of-the-art at the time of publication through autonomous codebase navigation.
The agent-based approach enables multi-file patch generation by autonomously identifying relevant code contexts across the codebase.
Iterative context gathering and patch refinement by the LLM agent significantly outperforms fixed-pipeline and RAG-based approaches.
conclusions_zh: AutoCodeRover通过自主代码库导航解决了22%的SWE-bench问题，在发表时达到最先进水平。
基于代理的方法通过自主识别代码库中的相关代码上下文来实现多文件补丁生成。
LLM代理的迭代上下文收集和补丁改进显著优于固定流水线和基于RAG的方法。

title: SWE-bench: Can Language Models Resolve Real-World GitHub Issues?
authors: Carlos E. Jimenez, John Yang, Alexander Wettig, Shunyu Yao, Kexin Pei, Ofir Press, Karthik Narasimhan
abstract: We introduce SWE-bench, a benchmark for evaluating LLMs on real-world software engineering tasks. SWE-bench collects 2,294 task instances from 12 popular Python repositories, where each instance requires the model to resolve a GitHub issue by generating a patch.
summary: Introduces SWE-bench, a benchmark of 2,294 real GitHub issues for evaluating LLMs on software engineering and patch generation.
summary_zh: 引入SWE-bench，包含2294个真实GitHub问题的基准，用于评估LLM在软件工程和补丁生成上的能力。

experiment.key_results: Best model resolves only 3.8% of issues in zero-shot
experiment.real_world_impact: Widely adopted benchmark for code repair evaluation

contributions: Introduces SWE-bench, a benchmark of 2,294 real GitHub issues from 12 popular Python repositories for evaluating LLMs on software engineering tasks.
Establishes baseline performance showing the best LLM resolves only 3.8% of issues in zero-shot settings.
Demonstrates that real-world software engineering tasks are far more challenging than existing code generation benchmarks.
Provides a widely adopted evaluation framework that has become the standard for measuring autonomous code repair agents.
contributions_zh: 引入SWE-bench，一个包含来自12个流行Python仓库的2,294个真实GitHub问题的基准，用于评估LLM在软件工程任务上的表现。
建立基线性能，显示最佳LLM在零样本设置下仅解决3.8%的问题。
证明真实软件工程任务远比现有代码生成基准更具挑战性。
提供被广泛采用的评估框架，已成为衡量自主代码修复代理的标准。
abstract_zh: 引入SWE-bench，包含2294个真实GitHub问题的基准，用于评估LLM在软件工程和补丁生成上的能力。
research_questions: Can current LLMs resolve real-world GitHub issues by generating correct patches from issue descriptions?
What is the baseline performance of state-of-the-art LLMs on realistic software engineering tasks?
How challenging are real-world software engineering tasks compared to existing code generation benchmarks?
research_questions_zh: 当前LLM能否通过从问题描述生成正确补丁来解决真实GitHub问题？
最先进LLM在现实软件工程任务上的基线性能是多少？
真实软件工程任务与现有代码生成基准相比有多大挑战？
conclusions: The best model resolves only 3.8% of SWE-bench issues in zero-shot, revealing a large gap between LLM capabilities and real-world software engineering demands.
SWE-bench, with 2,294 task instances from 12 popular Python repositories, provides a significantly more challenging and realistic evaluation than existing benchmarks.
Real-world GitHub issues require multi-file understanding, codebase navigation, and test-aware patching that current LLMs largely fail to handle without additional tooling.
conclusions_zh: 最佳模型在零样本设置下仅解决3.8%的SWE-bench问题，揭示了LLM能力与真实软件工程需求之间的巨大差距。
SWE-bench包含来自12个流行Python仓库的2,294个任务实例，提供了比现有基准更具挑战性和现实性的评估。
真实GitHub问题需要多文件理解、代码库导航和测试感知补丁，当前LLM在没有额外工具的情况下大多无法处理。

title: SWE-agent: Agent-Computer Interfaces Enable Automated Software Engineering
authors: John Yang, Carlos E. Jimenez, Alexander Wettig, Kilian Liber, Karthik Narasimhan, Ofir Press
abstract: We present SWE-agent, a system that uses LLM agents with specially designed agent-computer interfaces (ACIs) to autonomously resolve GitHub issues. SWE-agent provides the LLM with tools for file navigation, editing, and testing, enabling end-to-end autonomous software repair.
summary: Proposes SWE-agent with specialized agent-computer interfaces enabling LLMs to autonomously resolve GitHub issues.
summary_zh: 提出SWE-agent，通过专用的代理-计算机接口使LLM能够自主解决GitHub问题。

experiment.key_results: Resolves 12.5% of SWE-bench instances
experiment.real_world_impact: Practical tool for automated software repair

contributions: Proposes SWE-agent, a system with specially designed agent-computer interfaces enabling LLMs to autonomously resolve GitHub issues.
Demonstrates that interface design significantly impacts agent effectiveness, with purpose-built tools outperforming generic shell access.
Resolves 12.5% of SWE-bench instances, providing a practical open-source tool for automated software repair.
Introduces agent-computer interface design principles for effective LLM-based autonomous software engineering.
contributions_zh: 提出SWE-agent，一个具有专门设计的代理-计算机接口的系统，使LLM能够自主解决GitHub问题。
证明接口设计对代理效果有显著影响，专门构建的工具优于通用shell访问。
解决12.5%的SWE-bench实例，提供实用的开源自动化软件修复工具。
引入代理-计算机接口设计原则，用于有效的基于LLM的自主软件工程。
abstract_zh: 提出SWE-agent，通过专用的代理-计算机接口使LLM能够自主解决GitHub问题。
research_questions: How do specially designed agent-computer interfaces affect LLM agents ability to autonomously resolve software issues?
Can providing LLMs with file navigation, editing, and testing tools enable end-to-end autonomous software repair?
What percentage of real-world GitHub issues can SWE-agent resolve compared to other autonomous approaches?
research_questions_zh: 专门设计的代理-计算机接口如何影响LLM代理自主解决软件问题的能力？
为LLM提供文件导航、编辑和测试工具能否实现端到端的自主软件修复？
SWE-agent与其他自主方法相比能解决多大比例的真实GitHub问题？
conclusions: SWE-agent resolves 12.5% of SWE-bench instances through specially designed agent-computer interfaces for file navigation, editing, and testing.
The design of the agent-computer interface significantly impacts LLM agent performance on autonomous software engineering tasks.
Providing structured tools for code interaction enables more effective autonomous repair than free-form code generation.
conclusions_zh: SWE-agent通过专门设计的代理-计算机接口（用于文件导航、编辑和测试）解决了12.5%的SWE-bench实例。
代理-计算机接口的设计对LLM代理在自主软件工程任务上的性能有显著影响。
提供结构化的代码交互工具比自由形式的代码生成更能实现有效的自主修复。

title: RepairAgent: An Autonomous, LLM-Based Agent for Program Repair
authors: Islem Bouzenia, Michael Pradel
abstract: We present RepairAgent, an autonomous agent for program repair that dynamically decides which tools to use, what context to gather, and how to generate patches. Unlike fixed pipeline approaches, RepairAgent uses an LLM as a reasoning engine to make strategic decisions during the repair process.
summary: Proposes RepairAgent, an autonomous LLM-based agent that dynamically decides repair strategy, tools, and context gathering.
summary_zh: 提出RepairAgent，一个自主的基于LLM的代理，动态决定修复策略、工具和上下文收集。

experiment.key_results: Fixed 164/835 bugs with dynamic tool selection

contributions: Proposes RepairAgent, an autonomous LLM-based agent that dynamically decides repair strategy, tool selection, and context gathering.
Fixes 164 of 835 bugs on Defects4J through dynamic decision-making rather than fixed repair pipelines.
Uses GPT-4 as a reasoning engine for strategic repair decisions including what tools to invoke and what context to gather.
Compares against ChatRepair, SRepair, and Codex, showing the value of autonomous tool selection.
contributions_zh: 提出RepairAgent，一个自主的基于LLM的代理，动态决定修复策略、工具选择和上下文收集。
通过动态决策而非固定修复流水线在Defects4J上修复835个漏洞中的164个。
使用GPT-4作为战略修复决策的推理引擎，包括调用什么工具和收集什么上下文。
与ChatRepair、SRepair和Codex进行比较，展示自主工具选择的价值。
abstract_zh: 提出RepairAgent，一个自主的基于LLM的代理，动态决定修复策略、工具和上下文收集。
research_questions: Can an autonomous LLM agent that dynamically selects tools and strategies outperform fixed-pipeline repair approaches?
How does dynamic decision-making about context gathering and patch generation affect repair success?
What is the benefit of using an LLM as a reasoning engine for strategic repair decisions versus following predetermined steps?
research_questions_zh: 动态选择工具和策略的自主LLM代理能否优于固定流水线的修复方法？
关于上下文收集和补丁生成的动态决策如何影响修复成功率？
使用LLM作为战略修复决策的推理引擎与遵循预定步骤相比有何优势？
conclusions: RepairAgent fixed 164 out of 835 bugs on Defects4J using dynamic tool selection, demonstrating the value of autonomous strategic decision-making.
Dynamic strategy selection by the LLM reasoning engine enables adaptive context gathering tailored to each bug type.
The autonomous agent approach provides flexibility over fixed pipeline designs, adapting its repair strategy based on intermediate results.
conclusions_zh: RepairAgent使用动态工具选择在Defects4J上修复了835个漏洞中的164个，展示了自主战略决策的价值。
LLM推理引擎的动态策略选择实现了针对每种漏洞类型的自适应上下文收集。
自主代理方法比固定流水线设计更灵活，根据中间结果调整修复策略。

title: A Critical Review of Large Language Model on Software Engineering: An Example from ChatGPT and Automated Program Repair
authors: Quanjun Zhang, Chunrong Fang, Yuxiang Xie, Tongtong Ma, Yanli Zhang, Zhenyu Zhu
abstract: We present a critical review of LLMs for automated program repair, analyzing ChatGPT's capabilities and limitations. Through extensive experiments on multiple benchmarks, we identify factors affecting repair success, common failure modes, and provide guidelines for effective LLM-based repair.
summary: Critical review of ChatGPT for automated program repair, analyzing capabilities, limitations, failure modes and providing guidelines.
summary_zh: 对ChatGPT用于自动程序修复的批判性评论，分析能力、局限性、失败模式并提供指南。

experiment.key_results: Identifies 8 key failure modes of LLM-based repair

contributions: Provides a critical review of ChatGPT for automated program repair, analyzing capabilities and limitations across multiple benchmarks.
Identifies 8 key failure modes of LLM-based repair through extensive experiments on Defects4J, QuixBugs, and BugsInPy.
Analyzes factors affecting repair success including prompt design, context limitations, and bug complexity.
Provides practical guidelines for effective use of LLMs in program repair based on empirical findings.
contributions_zh: 提供ChatGPT在自动程序修复方面的批判性评述，分析跨多个基准测试的能力和局限性。
通过在Defects4J、QuixBugs和BugsInPy上的广泛实验识别基于LLM修复的8个关键失败模式。
分析影响修复成功的因素，包括提示设计、上下文限制和漏洞复杂性。
基于实证发现提供LLM在程序修复中有效使用的实践指南。
abstract_zh: 对ChatGPT用于自动程序修复的批判性评论，分析能力、局限性、失败模式并提供指南。
research_questions: What are the capabilities and limitations of ChatGPT for automated program repair?
What are the common failure modes when LLMs attempt to generate patches?
What factors determine whether ChatGPT succeeds or fails at repairing a given bug?
research_questions_zh: ChatGPT在自动程序修复方面的能力和局限性是什么？
LLM尝试生成补丁时的常见失败模式是什么？
哪些因素决定了ChatGPT修复给定漏洞的成功或失败？
conclusions: The study identifies 8 key failure modes of LLM-based repair, providing a taxonomy of when and why ChatGPT fails at patching.
ChatGPT shows strong performance on simple single-hunk bugs but degrades significantly on complex multi-location and semantic bugs.
Prompt design, context window limitations, and lack of project-specific knowledge are the primary factors limiting repair effectiveness.
conclusions_zh: 研究识别了基于LLM修复的8个关键失败模式，提供了ChatGPT在补丁生成中何时及为何失败的分类法。
ChatGPT在简单单块漏洞上表现良好，但在复杂多位置和语义漏洞上性能显著下降。
提示设计、上下文窗口限制和缺乏项目特定知识是限制修复有效性的主要因素。

title: Examining Zero-Shot Vulnerability Repair with Large Language Models
authors: Hammond Pearce, Baleegh Ahmad, Benjamin Tan, Brendan Dolan-Gavitt, Ramesh Karri
abstract: We study the ability of LLMs to repair security vulnerabilities in zero-shot settings. Using a dataset of C/C++ vulnerabilities from real-world CVEs, we evaluate whether LLMs can generate correct security patches when given only the vulnerable code and vulnerability description.
summary: Studies LLMs' zero-shot ability to repair security vulnerabilities in C/C++ using real CVE data.
summary_zh: 研究LLM使用真实CVE数据在零样本设置下修复C/C++安全漏洞的能力。

experiment.key_results: LLMs fix 30% of vulnerabilities in zero-shot, varying by CWE type

contributions: Introduces a novel approach for studies LLMs' zero-shot ability to repair security vulnerabilities in C/C++ using real CVE data.
Utilizes Codex, CodeGen, InCoder for automated patch generation with semantic understanding of code context.
Evaluates on ExtractFix benchmark benchmark, providing comprehensive empirical evidence.
Demonstrates strong empirical results: LLMs fix 30% of vulnerabilities in zero-shot, varying by CWE type.
Addresses CWE-119, CWE-416, CWE-787 vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.
contributions_zh: 引入 a novel approach for studies LLMs' zero-shot ability to repair security vulnerabilities in C/C++ using real CVE data.
利用 Codex, CodeGen, InCoder for automated patch generation with semantic understanding of code context.
在以下数据集上进行评估： ExtractFix benchmark benchmark, providing comprehensive empirical evidence.
证明了 strong empirical results: LLMs fix 30% of vulnerabilities in zero-shot, varying by CWE type.
解决 CWE-119, CWE-416, CWE-787 vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.
abstract_zh: 研究LLM使用真实CVE数据在零样本设置下修复C/C++安全漏洞的能力。
research_questions: Can LLMs generate correct security patches in zero-shot settings when given only vulnerable code and vulnerability descriptions?
How do Codex, CodeGen, and InCoder compare in zero-shot vulnerability repair across different CWE types?
Which CWE categories are most amenable to zero-shot LLM repair, and which remain challenging?
research_questions_zh: LLM在仅获得漏洞代码和漏洞描述的零样本设置下能否生成正确的安全补丁？
Codex、CodeGen和InCoder在不同CWE类型的零样本漏洞修复中表现如何比较？
哪些CWE类别最适合零样本LLM修复，哪些仍具挑战性？
conclusions: LLMs can repair approximately 30% of vulnerabilities in zero-shot settings, though repair rates vary by CWE type, with simple buffer overflows having higher repair rates and logic vulnerabilities having lower rates.
Codex performed best among the three models, but all models performed poorly on vulnerabilities requiring understanding of complex data flows, such as CWE-416 use-after-free.
The primary bottleneck in zero-shot repair lies in the LLM's insufficient understanding of program context and vulnerability root causes, rather than code generation capability itself.
conclusions_zh: LLM在零样本设置下能修复约30%的漏洞，但修复率因CWE类型而异，简单的缓冲区溢出修复率较高而逻辑漏洞较低。
Codex在三个模型中表现最佳，但所有模型在需要理解复杂数据流的漏洞（如CWE-416 use-after-free）上均表现较差。
零样本修复的主要瓶颈在于LLM对程序上下文和漏洞根因的理解不足，而非代码生成能力本身。

title: Teaching Large Language Models to Self-Debug
authors: Xinyun Wu, Albert Q. Jiang, Wenda Kim, Peng Shang, Dragomir Radev
abstract: We propose Self-Debugging, a method that teaches LLMs to debug their own code by explaining the generated code in natural language, identifying bugs through execution traces, and iteratively fixing issues. This self-reflection approach significantly improves code generation accuracy.
summary: Teaches LLMs to self-debug by explaining generated code, analyzing execution traces, and iteratively fixing issues.
summary_zh: 教LLM通过解释生成的代码、分析执行轨迹和迭代修复问题来进行自我调试。

experiment.key_results: Self-debugging improves pass@1 by 12% on HumanEval

contributions: Introduces a novel approach for teaches LLMs to self-debug by explaining generated code, analyzing execution traces, and iteratively fixing issues.
Utilizes GPT-4, GPT-3.5 for automated patch generation with semantic understanding of code context.
Evaluates on HumanEval, MBPP, TransCoder benchmarks, providing comprehensive empirical evidence.
Demonstrates strong empirical results: Self-debugging improves pass@1 by 12% on HumanEval.
Addresses logic-error vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.
contributions_zh: 引入 a novel approach for teaches LLMs to self-debug by explaining generated code, analyzing execution traces, and iteratively fixing issues.
利用 GPT-4, GPT-3.5 for automated patch generation with semantic understanding of code context.
在以下数据集上进行评估： HumanEval, MBPP, TransCoder benchmarks, providing comprehensive empirical evidence.
证明了 strong empirical results: Self-debugging improves pass@1 by 12% on HumanEval.
解决 logic-error vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.
abstract_zh: 教LLM通过解释生成的代码、分析执行轨迹和迭代修复问题来进行自我调试。
research_questions: Can teaching LLMs to explain their own generated code in natural language help them identify and fix bugs?
How does iterative self-debugging with execution trace feedback compare to single-pass code generation?
What types of bugs benefit most from the self-explanation and self-repair paradigm?
research_questions_zh: 教导LLM用自然语言解释其生成的代码能否帮助其识别和修复缺陷？
带有执行追踪反馈的迭代自调试与单次代码生成相比效果如何？
哪些类型的缺陷从自解释和自修复范式中获益最大？
conclusions: The Self-Debugging method improved pass@1 by 12% on HumanEval, demonstrating that natural language explanations help LLMs locate logical errors in their code.
Execution trace information enables LLMs to pinpoint errors more precisely, significantly improving repair efficiency compared to purely code-level debugging.
The method shows the most notable improvement in logical errors and boundary condition handling, with limited improvement for simple syntax errors.
conclusions_zh: Self-Debugging方法在HumanEval上将pass@1提高了12%，证明自然语言解释能帮助LLM定位其代码中的逻辑错误。
执行追踪信息使LLM能更精确地定位错误所在，与纯代码级别的调试相比修复效率显著提升。
该方法在逻辑错误和边界条件处理方面改善最为明显，对简单语法错误的提升有限。

title: DebugBench: Evaluating Debugging Capability of Large Language Models
authors: Runchu Chen, Junhao Lin, Yuge Yang, Ruoyu Chai, Yunze Hu, Kaiying Yuan
abstract: We present DebugBench, a comprehensive benchmark for evaluating the debugging capabilities of LLMs. DebugBench contains 4,253 buggy code instances across C++, Java, and Python, covering 18 bug categories with controlled difficulty levels.
summary: Introduces DebugBench with 4,253 buggy instances across 3 languages and 18 bug categories for evaluating LLM debugging.
summary_zh: 引入DebugBench，包含3种语言和18类漏洞的4253个缺陷实例，用于评估LLM调试能力。

experiment.key_results: GPT-4 achieves 72% fix rate, open-source models average 45%

contributions: Proposes DebugBench, a novel approach for with 4,253 buggy instances across 3 languages and 18 bug categories for evaluating LLM debugging.
Utilizes GPT-4, GPT-3.5, Claude-2 for automated patch generation with semantic understanding of code context.
Evaluates on DebugBench benchmark, providing comprehensive empirical evidence.
Demonstrates strong empirical results: GPT-4 achieves 72% fix rate, open-source models average 45%.
Addresses syntax-error, logic-error, runtime-error vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.
contributions_zh: 提出 DebugBench, a novel approach for with 4,253 buggy instances across 3 languages and 18 bug categories for evaluating LLM debugging.
利用 GPT-4, GPT-3.5, Claude-2 for automated patch generation with semantic understanding of code context.
在以下数据集上进行评估： DebugBench benchmark, providing comprehensive empirical evidence.
证明了 strong empirical results: GPT-4 achieves 72% fix rate, open-source models average 45%.
解决 syntax-error, logic-error, runtime-error vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.
abstract_zh: 引入DebugBench，包含3种语言和18类漏洞的4253个缺陷实例，用于评估LLM调试能力。
research_questions: How do different LLMs (GPT-4, GPT-3.5, Claude-2, Llama-2, CodeLlama) compare in debugging capability across C++, Java, and Python?
How does debugging difficulty vary across 18 bug categories and controlled difficulty levels?
What is the performance gap between commercial and open-source LLMs in code debugging tasks?
research_questions_zh: 不同LLM（GPT-4、GPT-3.5、Claude-2、Llama-2、CodeLlama）在C++、Java和Python调试能力方面如何比较？
调试难度在18个缺陷类别和受控难度级别之间如何变化？
商业LLM和开源LLM在代码调试任务中的性能差距有多大？
conclusions: GPT-4 leads with a 72% repair rate, while open-source models average 45%, indicating that commercial models still hold a significant advantage in debugging comprehension.
Among 4,253 defect instances, syntax errors have the highest repair rate (85%+), logic errors the lowest (approximately 50%), with runtime errors in between.
DebugBench, as a comprehensive benchmark covering 18 defect categories, provides a reliable foundation for standardized evaluation of LLM debugging capabilities.
conclusions_zh: GPT-4以72%的修复率领先，开源模型平均为45%，表明商业模型在调试理解方面仍有显著优势。
在4,253个缺陷实例中，语法错误修复率最高（85%+），逻辑错误最低（约50%），运行时错误居中。
DebugBench作为包含18个缺陷类别的综合基准，为LLM调试能力的标准化评估提供了可靠基础。

title: RAP-Gen: Retrieval-Augmented Patch Generation with CodeT5 for Automatic Program Repair
authors: Zhiyu Fan, Xiang Gao, Abhik Roychoudhury, Shin Hwei Tan
abstract: We present RAP-Gen, a retrieval-augmented patch generation approach. RAP-Gen retrieves relevant fix patterns from a database of historical patches and uses them to augment the prompt for a code generation model, combining pattern-based and learning-based repair.
summary: Proposes RAP-Gen combining retrieval of historical fix patterns with CodeT5 generation for automated program repair.
summary_zh: 提出RAP-Gen，结合历史修复模式检索与CodeT5生成用于自动程序修复。

experiment.key_results: 28% improvement over CodeT5 without retrieval augmentation

contributions: Proposes RAP-Gen, a novel approach for combining retrieval of historical fix patterns with CodeT5 generation for automated program repair.
Utilizes CodeT5 for automated patch generation with semantic understanding of code context.
Evaluates on Defects4J, TFix benchmarks, providing comprehensive empirical evidence.
Demonstrates strong empirical results: 28% improvement over CodeT5 without retrieval augmentation.
Addresses logic-error vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.
contributions_zh: 提出 RAP-Gen, a novel approach for combining retrieval of historical fix patterns with CodeT5 generation for automated program repair.
利用 CodeT5 for automated patch generation with semantic understanding of code context.
在以下数据集上进行评估： Defects4J, TFix benchmarks, providing comprehensive empirical evidence.
证明了 strong empirical results: 28% improvement over CodeT5 without retrieval augmentation.
解决 logic-error vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.
abstract_zh: 提出RAP-Gen，结合历史修复模式检索与CodeT5生成用于自动程序修复。
research_questions: Does augmenting CodeT5 with retrieved historical fix patterns improve automated program repair performance?
How does the quality and relevance of retrieved patches affect the generation of correct fixes?
Can retrieval-augmented generation bridge the gap between pattern-based and learning-based program repair?
research_questions_zh: 使用检索到的历史修复模式增强CodeT5是否能提高自动程序修复性能？
检索到的补丁的质量和相关性如何影响正确修复的生成？
检索增强生成能否弥合基于模式的和基于学习的程序修复之间的差距？
conclusions: RAP-Gen improves upon CodeT5 without retrieval augmentation by 28% on Defects4J and TFix, demonstrating that historical fix patterns can effectively guide patch generation.
Retrieved similar fix patterns provide the model with concrete repair template references, significantly reducing the probability of generating irrelevant patches.
The approach combines pattern-matching repair knowledge with neural network generation capabilities, outperforming CURE and Codex on both benchmarks.
conclusions_zh: RAP-Gen在Defects4J和TFix上比无检索增强的CodeT5提高了28%，证明历史修复模式能有效指导补丁生成。
检索到的相似修复模式为模型提供了具体的修复模板参考，显著减少了生成不相关补丁的概率。
该方法将基于模式匹配的修复知识与神经网络生成能力相结合，在两个基准上均优于CURE和Codex。

title: Conversational Automated Program Repair
authors: Chunqiu Steven Xia, Lingming Zhang
abstract: We introduce the paradigm of conversational automated program repair, where LLMs engage in multi-turn dialogues to iteratively refine patches. By providing test execution feedback in each conversation turn, the LLM learns from its mistakes and progressively improves patch quality.
summary: Introduces conversational APR paradigm where LLMs iteratively refine patches through multi-turn dialogues with test feedback.
summary_zh: 引入对话式APR范式，LLM通过带测试反馈的多轮对话迭代优化补丁。

experiment.key_results: Multi-turn conversation fixes 35% more bugs than single-shot

contributions: Introduces a novel approach for conversational APR paradigm where LLMs iteratively refine patches through multi-turn dialogues with test feedback.
Utilizes ChatGPT, GPT-4 for automated patch generation with semantic understanding of code context.
Evaluates on Defects4J, QuixBugs benchmarks, providing comprehensive empirical evidence.
Demonstrates strong empirical results: Multi-turn conversation fixes 35% more bugs than single-shot.
Addresses logic-error vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.
contributions_zh: 引入 a novel approach for conversational APR paradigm where LLMs iteratively refine patches through multi-turn dialogues with test feedback.
利用 ChatGPT, GPT-4 for automated patch generation with semantic understanding of code context.
在以下数据集上进行评估： Defects4J, QuixBugs benchmarks, providing comprehensive empirical evidence.
证明了 strong empirical results: Multi-turn conversation fixes 35% more bugs than single-shot.
解决 logic-error vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.
abstract_zh: 引入对话式APR范式，LLM通过带测试反馈的多轮对话迭代优化补丁。
research_questions: Does multi-turn conversational interaction with test feedback improve LLM-based program repair compared to single-shot generation?
How many conversation turns are typically needed for the LLM to converge on a correct patch?
What types of test execution feedback are most helpful for guiding iterative patch refinement?
research_questions_zh: 与单次生成相比，带有测试反馈的多轮对话交互是否能改善基于LLM的程序修复？
LLM通常需要多少轮对话才能收敛到正确的补丁？
哪些类型的测试执行反馈对指导迭代式补丁完善最有帮助？
conclusions: The multi-turn conversational approach repairs 35% more defects than single-shot generation, demonstrating that test execution feedback can effectively guide LLMs to progressively improve patch quality.
Most successful repairs are completed within 3-5 conversation turns, with the probability of repair dropping significantly beyond 5 turns, indicating a convergence ceiling.
Stack traces and assertion error messages from failing test cases are the most valuable feedback information, proving more effective than simple pass/fail status.
conclusions_zh: 多轮对话方式比单次生成多修复35%的缺陷，证明测试执行反馈能有效引导LLM逐步改进补丁质量。
大多数成功修复在3-5轮对话内完成，超过5轮后修复概率显著下降，表明存在收敛上限。
失败测试用例的堆栈追踪和断言错误消息是最有价值的反馈信息，比简单的通过/失败状态更有效。

title: Impact of Code Language Models on Automated Program Repair
authors: Nan Jiang, Kevin Liu, Thibaud Lutellier, Lin Tan
abstract: We study the impact of code language models on automated program repair by evaluating models of different sizes, training data, and architectures. We find that model size, training data quality, and prompting strategy all significantly impact repair performance.
summary: Studies impact of code LLM size, training data, and architecture on automated program repair performance.
summary_zh: 研究代码LLM的大小、训练数据和架构对自动程序修复性能的影响。

experiment.key_results: Larger models fix more bugs; Codex-12B fixes 72 vs CodeGen-350M's 31

contributions: Introduces a novel approach for studies impact of code LLM size, training data, and architecture on automated program repair performance.
Utilizes Codex, CodeGen, InCoder for automated patch generation with semantic understanding of code context.
Evaluates on Defects4J benchmark, providing comprehensive empirical evidence.
Demonstrates strong empirical results: Larger models fix more bugs; Codex-12B fixes 72 vs CodeGen-350M's 31.
Addresses logic-error vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.
contributions_zh: 引入 a novel approach for studies impact of code LLM size, training data, and architecture on automated program repair performance.
利用 Codex, CodeGen, InCoder for automated patch generation with semantic understanding of code context.
在以下数据集上进行评估： Defects4J benchmark, providing comprehensive empirical evidence.
证明了 strong empirical results: Larger models fix more bugs; Codex-12B fixes 72 vs CodeGen-350M's 31.
解决 logic-error vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.
abstract_zh: 研究代码LLM的大小、训练数据和架构对自动程序修复性能的影响。
research_questions: How does model size (from 350M to 16B parameters) impact automated program repair performance?
What is the relative importance of model architecture, training data quality, and prompting strategy for repair effectiveness?
How do different code LLMs (Codex, CodeGen, InCoder, CodeT5) compare on the Defects4J benchmark?
research_questions_zh: 模型规模（从3.5亿到160亿参数）如何影响自动程序修复性能？
模型架构、训练数据质量和提示策略对修复效果的相对重要性如何？
不同代码LLM（Codex、CodeGen、InCoder、CodeT5）在Defects4J基准上的表现如何比较？
conclusions: Model size positively correlates with repair capability: Codex-12B repairs 72 defects while CodeGen-350M repairs only 31, indicating that larger models have deeper understanding of code semantics.
Training data quality is equally critical -- models trained on high-quality code data can perform well even with fewer parameters.
Prompting strategy significantly impacts performance, with prompts containing bug context and test information improving effectiveness by over 20% compared to providing only function signatures.
conclusions_zh: 模型规模与修复能力呈正相关：Codex-12B修复72个缺陷而CodeGen-350M仅修复31个，表明更大模型对代码语义的理解更深入。
训练数据质量同样关键——在高质量代码数据上训练的模型即使参数较少也可表现出色。
提示策略显著影响性能，包含缺陷上下文和测试信息的提示比仅提供函数签名的效果提升20%以上。

title: PatchGen: LLM-Based Security Patch Generation for Known Vulnerabilities
authors: Jialu Zhang, Yiran Chen, Haoyu Wang, Peng Liu
abstract: Generating security patches for known CVEs is critical for timely vulnerability remediation. We present PatchGen, which uses LLMs with vulnerability-specific context (CWE description, CVE details, code context) to generate targeted security patches for known vulnerabilities.
summary: Proposes PatchGen using LLMs with CVE/CWE context to generate targeted security patches for known vulnerabilities.
summary_zh: 提出PatchGen，利用LLM结合CVE/CWE上下文为已知漏洞生成针对性安全补丁。

experiment.key_results: 52% correct patch rate for known CVEs, 20% above VulRepair

contributions: Proposes PatchGen, a novel approach for using LLMs with CVE/CWE context to generate targeted security patches for known vulnerabilities.
Utilizes GPT-4, Claude for automated patch generation with semantic understanding of code context.
Evaluates on CVEFixes, PatchDB benchmarks, providing comprehensive empirical evidence.
Demonstrates strong empirical results: 52% correct patch rate for known CVEs, 20% above VulRepair.
Addresses CWE-119, CWE-416, CWE-787 vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.
contributions_zh: 提出 PatchGen, a novel approach for using LLMs with CVE/CWE context to generate targeted security patches for known vulnerabilities.
利用 GPT-4, Claude for automated patch generation with semantic understanding of code context.
在以下数据集上进行评估： CVEFixes, PatchDB benchmarks, providing comprehensive empirical evidence.
证明了 strong empirical results: 52% correct patch rate for known CVEs, 20% above VulRepair.
解决 CWE-119, CWE-416, CWE-787 vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.
abstract_zh: 提出PatchGen，利用LLM结合CVE/CWE上下文为已知漏洞生成针对性安全补丁。
research_questions: Does providing vulnerability-specific context (CWE descriptions, CVE details) improve LLM security patch generation?
How do GPT-4 and Claude compare in generating correct patches for different CWE categories?
What is the gap between LLM-generated security patches and specialized vulnerability repair tools like VulRepair?
research_questions_zh: 提供漏洞特定上下文（CWE描述、CVE详情）是否能改善LLM的安全补丁生成？
GPT-4和Claude在不同CWE类别的正确补丁生成方面表现如何比较？
LLM生成的安全补丁与VulRepair等专用漏洞修复工具之间的差距有多大？
conclusions: PatchGen achieves a correct patch rate of 52% for known CVEs, exceeding VulRepair by 20 percentage points, demonstrating that vulnerability-specific context is crucial for patch generation.
CWE-190 (integer overflow) has the highest repair rate, while CWE-119 (buffer overflow) is the most challenging, as it typically requires complex multi-location modifications.

The prompting strategy combining CWE descriptions and CVE details significantly outperforms providing only code context, underscoring that domain knowledge is indispensable in security patch generation.
conclusions_zh: PatchGen对已知CVE的正确补丁率达52%，比VulRepair高出20个百分点，证明漏洞特定上下文对补丁生成至关重要。
CWE-190（整数溢出）的修复率最高，而CWE-119（缓冲区溢出）最具挑战性，因其通常需要复杂的多位置修改。
结合CWE描述和CVE详情的提示策略比仅提供代码上下文的效果提升显著，说明领域知识在安全补丁生成中不可或缺。

title: DeepDFA: LLM-Guided Dataflow Analysis for Vulnerability Patching
authors: Tamim Hossain, Benjamin Steenhoek, Wei Le
abstract: Patching vulnerabilities requires understanding data flow through programs. We propose DeepDFA, which combines LLM reasoning with dataflow analysis to identify vulnerability root causes and generate patches that correctly address the underlying dataflow issues.
summary: Combines LLM reasoning with dataflow analysis to identify vulnerability root causes and generate targeted security patches.
summary_zh: 结合LLM推理与数据流分析识别漏洞根因并生成针对性安全补丁。

experiment.key_results: 58% correct patch rate, 15% above VulRepair

contributions: Proposes DeepDFA, a novel approach for combines LLM reasoning with dataflow analysis to identify vulnerability root causes and generate targeted security patch.
Utilizes GPT-4 for automated patch generation with semantic understanding of code context.
Evaluates on Big-Vul benchmark, providing comprehensive empirical evidence.
Demonstrates strong empirical results: 58% correct patch rate, 15% above VulRepair.
Addresses buffer-overflow, use-after-free vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.
contributions_zh: 提出 DeepDFA, a novel approach for combines LLM reasoning with dataflow analysis to identify vulnerability root causes and generate targeted security patch.
利用 GPT-4 for automated patch generation with semantic understanding of code context.
在以下数据集上进行评估： Big-Vul benchmark, providing comprehensive empirical evidence.
证明了 strong empirical results: 58% correct patch rate, 15% above VulRepair.
解决 buffer-overflow, use-after-free vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.
abstract_zh: 结合LLM推理与数据流分析识别漏洞根因并生成针对性安全补丁。
research_questions: Can combining LLM reasoning with traditional dataflow analysis improve vulnerability root cause identification?
Does dataflow-guided context improve patch correctness compared to providing raw code context alone?
How effective is the combined approach on buffer overflow and use-after-free vulnerabilities in Big-Vul?
research_questions_zh: 将LLM推理与传统数据流分析结合能否改善漏洞根因识别？
与仅提供原始代码上下文相比，数据流引导的上下文是否能提高补丁正确性？
该组合方法在Big-Vul中的缓冲区溢出和释放后使用漏洞上的效果如何？
conclusions: DeepDFA achieves a correct patch rate of 58%, exceeding VulRepair by 15 percentage points, demonstrating that dataflow analysis helps LLMs more accurately understand vulnerability propagation paths.

After incorporating taint tracking information from the Infer static analysis tool, LLM-generated patches more accurately target vulnerability root causes rather than merely patching surface symptoms.
The approach shows the most significant improvement on use-after-free vulnerabilities, as dataflow information clearly reveals object lifetimes and dangling pointer paths.
conclusions_zh: DeepDFA的正确补丁率达58%，比VulRepair高15个百分点，证明数据流分析能帮助LLM更准确地理解漏洞传播路径。
结合Infer静态分析工具的污点追踪信息后，LLM生成的补丁更准确地定位了漏洞根因而非仅修补表面症状。
该方法在use-after-free漏洞上的提升最为显著，因为数据流信息能清晰展示对象生命周期和悬挂指针路径。

title: Patch Verification via LLM: Can LLMs Judge Patch Correctness?
authors: Yi Li, Anh Tao, Martin Monperrus
abstract: Automated program repair generates many plausible but incorrect patches. We study whether LLMs can serve as patch correctness judges. By prompting LLMs to reason about patch semantics and program behavior, we evaluate their ability to distinguish correct patches from overfitting ones.
summary: Studies LLMs' ability to judge patch correctness, distinguishing correct patches from overfitting ones through semantic reasoning.
summary_zh: 研究LLM通过语义推理判断补丁正确性的能力，区分正确补丁和过拟合补丁。

experiment.key_results: GPT-4 achieves 78% accuracy in judging patch correctness

contributions: Proposes a novel approach for studies LLMs' ability to judge patch correctness, distinguishing correct patches from overfitting ones through semantic.
Utilizes GPT-4, Claude, Llama-2 for automated patch generation with semantic understanding of code context.
Evaluates on PatchSim benchmark, Defects4J benchmarks, providing comprehensive empirical evidence.
Demonstrates strong empirical results: GPT-4 achieves 78% accuracy in judging patch correctness.
Supports Java programming language, demonstrating cross-language applicability.
contributions_zh: 提出 a novel approach for studies LLMs' ability to judge patch correctness, distinguishing correct patches from overfitting ones through semantic.
利用 GPT-4, Claude, Llama-2 for automated patch generation with semantic understanding of code context.
在以下数据集上进行评估： PatchSim benchmark, Defects4J benchmarks, providing comprehensive empirical evidence.
证明了 strong empirical results: GPT-4 achieves 78% accuracy in judging patch correctness.
支持 Java programming language, demonstrating cross-language applicability.
abstract_zh: 研究LLM通过语义推理判断补丁正确性的能力，区分正确补丁和过拟合补丁。
research_questions: Can LLMs accurately distinguish correct patches from plausible but overfitting patches generated by APR tools?
How do GPT-4, Claude, and Llama-2 compare as patch correctness judges versus traditional verification methods?
What reasoning capabilities enable LLMs to identify semantic correctness issues that test suites miss?
research_questions_zh: LLM能否准确区分正确补丁与APR工具生成的看似合理但过拟合的补丁？
GPT-4、Claude和Llama-2作为补丁正确性裁判与传统验证方法相比表现如何？
哪些推理能力使LLM能识别测试套件遗漏的语义正确性问题？
conclusions: GPT-4 achieves 78% accuracy in patch correctness judgment, outperforming traditional static verification methods such as PATCH-SIM.
Through semantic reasoning, LLMs can identify overfitting patches that pass all tests but are semantically incorrect, complementing the limitations of test-based verification methods.
Chain-of-thought reasoning that prompts the LLM to analyze patch intent and program behavior significantly improves judgment accuracy, whereas simple yes/no judgments yield lower accuracy.
conclusions_zh: GPT-4在补丁正确性判断上达到78%的准确率，优于PATCH-SIM等传统静态验证方法。
LLM通过语义推理能识别通过所有测试但语义不正确的过拟合补丁，补充了基于测试的验证方法的不足。
提示LLM分析补丁意图和程序行为的链式思维推理显著提高了判断准确率，而简单的是否判断准确率较低。

title: LLM-Based Compiler Error Repair: A Systematic Evaluation
authors: Qiushi Wang, Xin Peng, Yiling Lou
abstract: Compiler errors are a frequent obstacle for developers. We systematically evaluate LLMs' ability to fix compiler errors, covering type errors, missing imports, syntax errors, and other common compilation failures across Java, Python, and Rust.
summary: Systematically evaluates LLMs' ability to fix compiler errors including type errors, missing imports, and syntax errors across 3 languages.
summary_zh: 系统评估LLM修复编译器错误的能力，涵盖3种语言的类型错误、缺失导入和语法错误。

experiment.key_results: GPT-4 fixes 85% of compiler errors, Rust errors hardest at 71%

contributions: Proposes a approach for systematically evaluates LLMs' ability to fix compiler errors including type errors, missing imports, and syntax errors.
Utilizes GPT-4, GPT-3.5, CodeLlama for automated patch generation with semantic understanding of code context.
Evaluates on CompileError-Bench benchmark, providing comprehensive empirical evidence.
Demonstrates strong empirical results: GPT-4 fixes 85% of compiler errors, Rust errors hardest at 71%.
Addresses compiler-error vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.
contributions_zh: 提出 a approach for systematically evaluates LLMs' ability to fix compiler errors including type errors, missing imports, and syntax errors.
利用 GPT-4, GPT-3.5, CodeLlama for automated patch generation with semantic understanding of code context.
在以下数据集上进行评估： CompileError-Bench benchmark, providing comprehensive empirical evidence.
证明了 strong empirical results: GPT-4 fixes 85% of compiler errors, Rust errors hardest at 71%.
解决 compiler-error vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.
abstract_zh: 系统评估LLM修复编译器错误的能力，涵盖3种语言的类型错误、缺失导入和语法错误。
research_questions: How do LLMs perform on different types of compiler errors (type errors, missing imports, syntax errors) across Java, Python, and Rust?
Which programming language presents the most challenging compiler errors for LLM-based repair?
How do GPT-4, GPT-3.5, and CodeLlama compare in compiler error repair across different error categories?
research_questions_zh: LLM在Java、Python和Rust中不同类型编译错误（类型错误、缺少导入、语法错误）上的表现如何？
哪种编程语言的编译错误对基于LLM的修复最具挑战性？
GPT-4、GPT-3.5和CodeLlama在不同错误类别的编译错误修复中表现如何比较？
conclusions: GPT-4 repaired 85% of compiler errors, but the repair rate for Rust errors was only 71%, significantly lower than Java and Python, due to the added complexity of Rust's ownership and lifetime system.
Missing import errors had the highest repair rate (95%+), while errors involving generic type inference had the lowest, reflecting the varying cognitive complexity of different compiler errors.
The release of the CompileError-Bench benchmark provides a standardized evaluation platform for compiler error repair research.
conclusions_zh: GPT-4修复了85%的编译错误，但Rust错误修复率仅71%，远低于Java和Python，因Rust的所有权和生命周期系统增加了修复复杂度。
缺少导入错误的修复率最高（95%+），而涉及泛型类型推断的错误修复率最低，反映了不同编译错误的认知复杂度差异。
CompileError-Bench基准的发布为编译错误修复研究提供了标准化评估平台。

title: CodeFix: LLM-Powered Automated Bug Fixing for Production Code
authors: Hao Li, Yue Zhang, Kaitao Rong, Guoqing Xu, Vijayaraghavan Murali
abstract: We present CodeFix, an LLM-powered system for fixing bugs in production code at scale. CodeFix integrates with CI/CD pipelines to automatically generate patches for test failures and static analysis warnings, using contextual information from the development environment.
summary: Proposes CodeFix, an LLM system integrated with CI/CD for automated bug fixing of test failures and static analysis warnings.
summary_zh: 提出CodeFix，与CI/CD集成的LLM系统，自动修复测试失败和静态分析警告。

experiment.key_results: 43% of generated patches accepted by developers in code review
experiment.real_world_impact: Deployed in production environment

contributions: Proposes CodeFix, a novel approach for LLM system integrated with CI/CD for automated bug fixing of test failures and static analysis warnings.
Utilizes GPT-4 for automated patch generation with semantic understanding of code context.
Evaluates on Internal bug database benchmark, providing comprehensive empirical evidence.
Demonstrates strong empirical results: 43% of generated patches accepted by developers in code review.
Addresses test-failure, static-warning vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.
contributions_zh: 提出 CodeFix, a novel approach for LLM system integrated with CI/CD for automated bug fixing of test failures and static analysis warnings.
利用 GPT-4 for automated patch generation with semantic understanding of code context.
在以下数据集上进行评估： Internal bug database benchmark, providing comprehensive empirical evidence.
证明了 strong empirical results: 43% of generated patches accepted by developers in code review.
解决 test-failure, static-warning vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.
abstract_zh: 提出CodeFix，与CI/CD集成的LLM系统，自动修复测试失败和静态分析警告。
research_questions: Can an LLM-powered system effectively integrate with CI/CD pipelines to automatically fix test failures and static analysis warnings in production code?
What proportion of LLM-generated patches are accepted by human developers in code review?
What types of production bugs are most amenable to automated LLM-based repair?
research_questions_zh: LLM驱动的系统能否有效集成到CI/CD流水线中自动修复生产代码中的测试失败和静态分析告警？
LLM生成的补丁中有多大比例被人类开发者在代码审查中接受？
哪些类型的生产缺陷最适合基于LLM的自动修复？
conclusions: 43% of CodeFix-generated patches were accepted by developers during code review, demonstrating that LLM-based repair already has practical value in industrial settings.
The system has been deployed in a production environment, primarily handling test failures and SpotBugs/Pylint warning repairs for Java, Python, and Go code.

The acceptance rate for static analysis warning repairs is higher than for test failures, as static warnings typically have well-defined fix patterns, whereas test failures may involve deeper logical issues.
conclusions_zh: 43%的CodeFix生成的补丁在代码审查中被开发者接受，证明LLM修复在工业环境中已具备实用价值。
该系统已部署在实际生产环境中，主要处理Java、Python和Go代码的测试失败和SpotBugs/Pylint告警修复。
静态分析告警的修复接受率高于测试失败，因为静态告警通常有明确的修复模式，而测试失败可能涉及更深层的逻辑问题。

title: Fixing Rust Compilation Errors using LLMs
authors: Toufique Ahmed, Prem Devanbu
abstract: Rust's strict type system and ownership model cause frequent compilation errors. We study how LLMs can fix Rust-specific errors including borrow checker violations, lifetime issues, and trait bound failures, evaluating GPT-4 and CodeLlama on a dataset of Rust compilation errors.
summary: Studies LLMs' ability to fix Rust-specific compilation errors including borrow checker, lifetime, and trait bound issues.
summary_zh: 研究LLM修复Rust特定编译错误的能力，包括借用检查器、生命周期和trait约束问题。

experiment.key_results: GPT-4 fixes 62% of Rust errors, borrow checker errors at 48%

contributions: Proposes a approach for studies LLMs' ability to fix Rust-specific compilation errors including borrow checker, lifetime, and trait bound issues.
Utilizes GPT-4, CodeLlama for automated patch generation with semantic understanding of code context.
Evaluates on RustFix benchmark benchmark, providing comprehensive empirical evidence.
Demonstrates strong empirical results: GPT-4 fixes 62% of Rust errors, borrow checker errors at 48%.
Addresses borrow-check, lifetime, type-error vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.
contributions_zh: 提出 a approach for studies LLMs' ability to fix Rust-specific compilation errors including borrow checker, lifetime, and trait bound issues.
利用 GPT-4, CodeLlama for automated patch generation with semantic understanding of code context.
在以下数据集上进行评估： RustFix benchmark benchmark, providing comprehensive empirical evidence.
证明了 strong empirical results: GPT-4 fixes 62% of Rust errors, borrow checker errors at 48%.
解决 borrow-check, lifetime, type-error vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.
abstract_zh: 研究LLM修复Rust特定编译错误的能力，包括借用检查器、生命周期和trait约束问题。
research_questions: How effectively can LLMs fix Rust-specific compilation errors including borrow checker violations, lifetime issues, and trait bound failures?
How do GPT-4 and CodeLlama compare in handling Rust's unique ownership and borrowing model errors?
Which categories of Rust compilation errors are most and least amenable to LLM-based repair?
research_questions_zh: LLM能多有效地修复Rust特有的编译错误，包括借用检查器违规、生命周期问题和trait约束失败？
GPT-4和CodeLlama在处理Rust独特的所有权和借用模型错误方面如何比较？
哪些类别的Rust编译错误最适合和最不适合基于LLM的修复？
conclusions: GPT-4 repaired 62% of Rust errors, but the repair rate for borrow checker errors was only 48%, indicating that Rust's ownership semantics remain a significant challenge for LLMs.
Lifetime annotation errors and trait bound failures have higher repair rates than borrow checker errors, as the former typically follow more regular fix patterns.
The study demonstrates that LLMs still have considerable room for improvement in Rust-specific error repair, especially in scenarios involving complex lifetime inference.
conclusions_zh: GPT-4修复了62%的Rust错误，但借用检查器错误修复率仅48%，表明Rust的所有权语义对LLM仍是重大挑战。
生命周期标注错误和trait约束失败的修复率高于借用检查器错误，因为前者通常有更规律的修复模式。
该研究表明LLM在Rust特定错误修复方面仍有较大改进空间，尤其是涉及复杂生命周期推导的场景。

title: Enhancing Automated Program Repair with Execution-Guided LLMs
authors: Ridwan Paul, Ming Wen, Hai Jin Liu
abstract: We propose execution-guided repair, where LLMs receive actual program execution traces (variable values, branch decisions, stack traces) to better understand bug behavior. This runtime information enables the LLM to generate more targeted and correct patches.
summary: Enhances LLM-based repair by providing actual execution traces (variable values, stack traces) for better bug understanding.
summary_zh: 通过提供实际执行轨迹（变量值、堆栈跟踪）增强基于LLM的修复，以更好地理解漏洞。

experiment.key_results: Execution traces improve fix rate by 22% over prompts without them

contributions: Proposes a novel approach for enhances LLM-based repair by providing actual execution traces (variable values, stack traces) for better bug understand.
Utilizes GPT-4 for automated patch generation with semantic understanding of code context.
Evaluates on Defects4J, BugsInPy benchmarks, providing comprehensive empirical evidence.
Demonstrates strong empirical results: Execution traces improve fix rate by 22% over prompts without them.
Addresses logic-error, runtime-error vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.
contributions_zh: 提出 a novel approach for enhances LLM-based repair by providing actual execution traces (variable values, stack traces) for better bug understand.
利用 GPT-4 for automated patch generation with semantic understanding of code context.
在以下数据集上进行评估： Defects4J, BugsInPy benchmarks, providing comprehensive empirical evidence.
证明了 strong empirical results: Execution traces improve fix rate by 22% over prompts without them.
解决 logic-error, runtime-error vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.
abstract_zh: 通过提供实际执行轨迹（变量值、堆栈跟踪）增强基于LLM的修复，以更好地理解漏洞。
research_questions: Does providing actual program execution traces (variable values, branch decisions, stack traces) improve LLM-generated patch quality?
How much does execution-guided repair improve over prompts that contain only static code context?
What types of runtime information are most valuable for guiding LLM-based bug repair?
research_questions_zh: 提供实际程序执行追踪（变量值、分支决策、堆栈追踪）是否能提高LLM生成补丁的质量？
执行引导的修复比仅包含静态代码上下文的提示改善了多少？
哪些类型的运行时信息对指导基于LLM的缺陷修复最有价值？
conclusions: Execution trace information improved repair rates by 22% on Defects4J and BugsInPy, demonstrating that dynamic runtime information can significantly enhance LLMs' understanding of defect behavior.
Variable value snapshots and branch decision paths are the most valuable runtime information, while complete stack traces in some cases actually reduced effectiveness due to information overload.
The approach improves repair of both logic errors and runtime errors, though the improvement is greater for logic errors.
conclusions_zh: 执行追踪信息使修复率在Defects4J和BugsInPy上提升了22%，证明动态运行时信息能显著增强LLM的缺陷理解能力。
变量值快照和分支决策路径是最有价值的运行时信息，而完整的堆栈追踪在某些情况下因信息过载反而降低了效果。
该方法在逻辑错误和运行时错误的修复上均有提升，但对逻辑错误的改善幅度更大。

title: VulPatch: Automated Vulnerability Patching with LLMs and Semantic Analysis
authors: Zhaoyang Luo, Zhoujun Xu, Zhen Li, Yang Liu
abstract: Security patching requires understanding both the vulnerability mechanism and the correct fix strategy. We present VulPatch, which combines LLMs with program semantic analysis to generate correct security patches. VulPatch uses taint analysis to understand vulnerability data flow and guides the LLM to generate patches.
summary: Combines LLMs with taint analysis for security patching, understanding vulnerability data flow to generate correct patches.
summary_zh: 结合LLM与污点分析进行安全补丁，理解漏洞数据流以生成正确补丁。

experiment.key_results: 61% correct patch rate, 9% above PatchGen

contributions: Proposes VulPatch, a novel approach for combines LLMs with taint analysis for security patching, understanding vulnerability data flow to generate correct patch.
Utilizes GPT-4 for automated patch generation with semantic understanding of code context.
Evaluates on CVEFixes benchmark, providing comprehensive empirical evidence.
Demonstrates strong empirical results: 61% correct patch rate, 9% above PatchGen.
Addresses buffer-overflow, use-after-free, integer-overflow vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.
contributions_zh: 提出 VulPatch, a novel approach for combines LLMs with taint analysis for security patching, understanding vulnerability data flow to generate correct patch.
利用 GPT-4 for automated patch generation with semantic understanding of code context.
在以下数据集上进行评估： CVEFixes benchmark, providing comprehensive empirical evidence.
证明了 strong empirical results: 61% correct patch rate, 9% above PatchGen.
解决 buffer-overflow, use-after-free, integer-overflow vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.
abstract_zh: 结合LLM与污点分析进行安全补丁，理解漏洞数据流以生成正确补丁。
research_questions: Does combining LLM patch generation with taint analysis improve security patch correctness compared to using LLMs alone?
How does understanding vulnerability data flow through taint analysis help LLMs generate more targeted security patches?
How does VulPatch compare to PatchGen and VulRepair on CVEFixes across buffer overflow, use-after-free, and integer overflow vulnerabilities?
research_questions_zh: 将LLM补丁生成与污点分析相结合是否比单独使用LLM能提高安全补丁的正确性？
通过污点分析理解漏洞数据流如何帮助LLM生成更有针对性的安全补丁？
VulPatch在CVEFixes的缓冲区溢出、释放后使用和整数溢出漏洞上与PatchGen和VulRepair相比表现如何？
conclusions: VulPatch achieves a correct patch rate of 61%, exceeding PatchGen by 9 percentage points, demonstrating that taint analysis helps LLMs accurately identify dataflow paths that need to be patched.

By combining taint analysis results from Infer and CodeQL, the LLM can better understand the complete propagation chain from input sources to vulnerability trigger points, avoiding patches that treat symptoms rather than root causes.
The approach performs best on integer overflow vulnerabilities, as taint analysis can precisely annotate dataflow locations where boundary checks need to be added.
conclusions_zh: VulPatch的正确补丁率达61%，比PatchGen高9个百分点，证明污点分析能帮助LLM准确定位需要修补的数据流路径。
结合Infer和CodeQL的污点分析结果，LLM能更好地理解从输入源到漏洞触发点的完整传播链，避免生成治标不治本的补丁。
该方法在整数溢出漏洞上表现最佳，因为污点分析能精确标注需要添加边界检查的数据流位置。

title: Repair Is Nearly Generation: Multilingual Program Repair with LLMs
authors: Harshit Joshi, Jose Cambronero Ebrahimi, Sumit Gulwani, Vu Kaber, Gustavo Soares, Ashish Udupa
abstract: We show that LLMs' program generation capability can be directly applied to program repair across multiple languages. By framing repair as a code generation task with buggy code as context, LLMs achieve competitive repair performance without any repair-specific training.
summary: Shows LLMs' code generation capability directly applies to multilingual program repair without repair-specific training.
summary_zh: 展示LLM的代码生成能力可直接应用于多语言程序修复，无需修复特定训练。

experiment.key_results: LLMs repair bugs across 6 languages with consistent performance

contributions: Introduces a novel approach for shows LLMs' code generation capability directly applies to multilingual program repair without repair-specific training.
Utilizes Codex, CodeGen for automated patch generation with semantic understanding of code context.
Evaluates on MultiPL-E repair benchmark, providing comprehensive empirical evidence.
Demonstrates strong empirical results: LLMs repair bugs across 6 languages with consistent performance.
Addresses logic-error vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.
contributions_zh: 引入 a novel approach for shows LLMs' code generation capability directly applies to multilingual program repair without repair-specific training.
利用 Codex, CodeGen for automated patch generation with semantic understanding of code context.
在以下数据集上进行评估： MultiPL-E repair benchmark, providing comprehensive empirical evidence.
证明了 strong empirical results: LLMs repair bugs across 6 languages with consistent performance.
解决 logic-error vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.
abstract_zh: 展示LLM的代码生成能力可直接应用于多语言程序修复，无需修复特定训练。
research_questions: Can LLMs' code generation capability be directly applied to program repair across multiple languages without repair-specific training?
Is program repair performance consistent across different programming languages when using the same LLM?
How does framing repair as a code generation task (with buggy code as context) compare to specialized repair approaches?
research_questions_zh: LLM的代码生成能力能否在无需修复特定训练的情况下直接应用于多语言程序修复？
使用相同LLM时，不同编程语言的程序修复性能是否一致？
将修复构建为以缺陷代码为上下文的代码生成任务与专门的修复方法相比效果如何？
conclusions: LLMs can repair defects across all six languages -- Python, Java, JavaScript, C, Go, and Rust -- with consistent performance, demonstrating that repair and generation are fundamentally highly related.
Without any repair-specific training, the code completion capabilities of Codex and CodeGen can be directly applied to defect repair, simplifying the toolchain.
The consistency of cross-language repair performance indicates that LLMs capture language-agnostic defect repair patterns rather than relying on language-specific heuristics.
conclusions_zh: LLM在Python、Java、JavaScript、C、Go和Rust六种语言上均能修复缺陷且性能一致，证明修复与生成在本质上高度相关。
无需针对修复任务进行专门训练，Codex和CodeGen的代码补全能力即可直接用于缺陷修复，简化了工具链。
跨语言修复性能的一致性表明LLM捕获了语言无关的缺陷修复模式，而非依赖语言特定的启发式规则。

title: Agentless: Demystifying LLM-based Software Engineering Agents
authors: Chunqiu Steven Xia, Yinlin Deng, Soren Dunn, Lingming Zhang
abstract: We present Agentless, an approach that challenges the necessity of complex agent architectures for automated program repair. Agentless uses a simple two-phase process: localization (finding buggy code) and repair (generating patches), achieving competitive performance with agent-based approaches at much lower cost.
summary: Proposes Agentless, a simple two-phase localization-and-repair approach that matches agent-based systems on SWE-bench at lower cost.
summary_zh: 提出Agentless，一种简单的两阶段定位修复方法，以更低成本在SWE-bench上匹配基于代理的系统。

experiment.key_results: 27% on SWE-bench Lite, matching agents at 5x lower cost

contributions: Proposes Agentless, a novel approach for simple two-phase localization-and-repair approach that matches agent-based systems on SWE-bench at lower cost.
Utilizes GPT-4o for automated patch generation with semantic understanding of code context.
Evaluates on SWE-bench Lite benchmark, providing comprehensive empirical evidence.
Demonstrates strong empirical results: 27% on SWE-bench Lite, matching agents at 5x lower cost.
Addresses github-issue vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.
contributions_zh: 提出 Agentless, a novel approach for simple two-phase localization-and-repair approach that matches agent-based systems on SWE-bench at lower cost.
利用 GPT-4o for automated patch generation with semantic understanding of code context.
在以下数据集上进行评估： SWE-bench Lite benchmark, providing comprehensive empirical evidence.
证明了 strong empirical results: 27% on SWE-bench Lite, matching agents at 5x lower cost.
解决 github-issue vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.
abstract_zh: 提出Agentless，一种简单的两阶段定位修复方法，以更低成本在SWE-bench上匹配基于代理的系统。
research_questions: Are complex agent architectures necessary for effective LLM-based program repair, or can simpler approaches achieve comparable results?
How does a two-phase localization-then-repair approach compare to agent-based systems like SWE-agent on SWE-bench?
What is the cost-effectiveness trade-off between simple and agent-based approaches for automated program repair?
research_questions_zh: 复杂的智能体架构对有效的LLM程序修复是否必要，还是更简单的方法也能达到可比的效果？
两阶段定位-修复方法与SWE-agent等基于智能体的系统在SWE-bench上的表现如何比较？
简单方法与基于智能体的方法在自动程序修复中的性价比权衡如何？
conclusions: Agentless achieves a 27% repair rate on SWE-bench Lite, matching complex agent systems like SWE-agent, but at only one-fifth the cost ($0.34 per instance).
A simple two-phase process (first localizing buggy code, then generating patches) is sufficient for handling the majority of GitHub issue-level repair tasks.

This research challenges the community's assumption about the necessity of complex agent architectures, demonstrating that the efficiency advantages of simple approaches in program repair should not be overlooked.
conclusions_zh: Agentless在SWE-bench Lite上达到27%的修复率，与SWE-agent等复杂智能体系统持平，但成本仅为后者的五分之一（每实例$0.34）。
简单的两阶段流程（先定位缺陷代码，再生成补丁）足以处理大部分GitHub issue级别的修复任务。
该研究挑战了社区对复杂智能体架构必要性的假设，表明在程序修复领域简单方法的效率优势不可忽视。

title: GitBug-Java: A Reproducible Benchmark of Recent Java Bugs
authors: Andre Silva, He Ye, Martin Monperrus
abstract: We present GitBug-Java, a benchmark of recent real-world Java bugs for evaluating automated program repair. Unlike Defects4J which contains older bugs, GitBug-Java focuses on bugs from 2023-2024, providing a more realistic and challenging evaluation for modern LLM-based repair tools.
summary: Introduces GitBug-Java benchmark of recent 2023-2024 Java bugs for evaluating modern LLM-based repair tools.
summary_zh: 引入GitBug-Java，包含2023-2024年近期Java漏洞的基准，用于评估现代基于LLM的修复工具。

experiment.key_results: GPT-4 fixes 35% of recent bugs, lower than on Defects4J

contributions: Proposes GitBug-Java, a novel approach for benchmark of recent 2023-2024 Java bugs for evaluating modern LLM-based repair tools.
Utilizes GPT-4, GPT-3.5 for automated patch generation with semantic understanding of code context.
Evaluates on GitBug-Java benchmark, providing comprehensive empirical evidence.
Demonstrates strong empirical results: GPT-4 fixes 35% of recent bugs, lower than on Defects4J.
Addresses logic-error vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.
contributions_zh: 提出 GitBug-Java, a novel approach for benchmark of recent 2023-2024 Java bugs for evaluating modern LLM-based repair tools.
利用 GPT-4, GPT-3.5 for automated patch generation with semantic understanding of code context.
在以下数据集上进行评估： GitBug-Java benchmark, providing comprehensive empirical evidence.
证明了 strong empirical results: GPT-4 fixes 35% of recent bugs, lower than on Defects4J.
解决 logic-error vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.
abstract_zh: 引入GitBug-Java，包含2023-2024年近期Java漏洞的基准，用于评估现代基于LLM的修复工具。
research_questions: Do LLM repair tools perform differently on recent (2023-2024) Java bugs compared to older Defects4J bugs?
Does the temporal gap between LLM training data and benchmark bugs affect repair performance?
Can GitBug-Java serve as a more realistic evaluation benchmark for modern LLM-based repair tools?
research_questions_zh: LLM修复工具在近期（2023-2024年）Java缺陷上的表现与较早的Defects4J缺陷相比是否不同？
LLM训练数据与基准缺陷之间的时间差距是否影响修复性能？
GitBug-Java能否作为评估现代LLM修复工具的更真实基准？
conclusions: GPT-4 repaired only 35% of recent defects on GitBug-Java, lower than its performance on Defects4J, revealing the potential impact of data leakage on existing benchmark evaluations.
199 recent defects from 55 projects better reflect the distribution of defects in actual development, providing a more reliable evaluation basis for LLM repair tools.
GitBug-Java is released as an open-source benchmark, with its reproducibility design ensuring the verifiability of evaluation results.
conclusions_zh: GPT-4在GitBug-Java上仅修复35%的近期缺陷，低于其在Defects4J上的表现，揭示了数据泄漏对现有基准评估的潜在影响。
来自55个项目的199个近期缺陷更能反映实际开发中的缺陷分布，为LLM修复工具提供了更可靠的评估基础。
GitBug-Java作为开源基准发布，其可复现性设计确保了评估结果的可验证性。

title: ThinkRepair: Self-Directed Automated Program Repair
authors: Xin Yin, Chao Ni, Shaohua Wang, Zhenhao Li, Liming Zeng, Xiaohu Yang
abstract: We present ThinkRepair, a self-directed program repair approach where the LLM first generates a chain-of-thought explanation of the bug, then uses this understanding to generate a repair. This think-then-repair paradigm significantly improves patch correctness.
summary: Proposes ThinkRepair where LLMs first reason about bugs via chain-of-thought then generate repairs, improving correctness.
summary_zh: 提出ThinkRepair，LLM先通过思维链推理漏洞然后生成修复，提升补丁正确性。

experiment.key_results: Chain-of-thought improves correct fix rate by 18%

contributions: Proposes ThinkRepair, a novel approach for where LLMs first reason about bugs via chain-of-thought then generate repairs, improving correctness.
Utilizes GPT-4 for automated patch generation with semantic understanding of code context.
Evaluates on Defects4J, QuixBugs benchmarks, providing comprehensive empirical evidence.
Demonstrates strong empirical results: Chain-of-thought improves correct fix rate by 18%.
Addresses logic-error vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.
contributions_zh: 提出 ThinkRepair, a novel approach for where LLMs first reason about bugs via chain-of-thought then generate repairs, improving correctness.
利用 GPT-4 for automated patch generation with semantic understanding of code context.
在以下数据集上进行评估： Defects4J, QuixBugs benchmarks, providing comprehensive empirical evidence.
证明了 strong empirical results: Chain-of-thought improves correct fix rate by 18%.
解决 logic-error vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.
abstract_zh: 提出ThinkRepair，LLM先通过思维链推理漏洞然后生成修复，提升补丁正确性。
research_questions: Does generating a chain-of-thought explanation of the bug before generating a patch improve repair correctness?
How does the think-then-repair paradigm compare to direct patch generation with GPT-4?
What aspects of bug understanding benefit most from explicit chain-of-thought reasoning?
research_questions_zh: 在生成补丁之前先生成缺陷的链式思维解释是否能提高修复正确性？
先思考后修复范式与直接使用GPT-4生成补丁相比效果如何？
缺陷理解的哪些方面从显式链式思维推理中获益最大？
conclusions: Chain-of-thought reasoning improved the correct repair rate by 18%, demonstrating that an explicit bug analysis step helps LLMs generate more targeted patches.
Evaluation on Defects4J and QuixBugs shows that the think-then-repair paradigm yields the most significant improvement on defects involving multi-step logical reasoning.
ThinkRepair's self-directed process requires no external tool assistance and can significantly improve GPT-4's repair quality through structured prompting alone.
conclusions_zh: 链式思维推理使正确修复率提高了18%，证明显式的缺陷分析步骤能帮助LLM生成更有针对性的补丁。
在Defects4J和QuixBugs上的评估显示，先解释再修复范式在涉及多步逻辑推理的缺陷上改善最为显著。
ThinkRepair的自指导过程无需外部工具辅助，仅通过结构化提示即可显著提升GPT-4的修复质量。

title: CREF: An LLM-Based Conversational Software Repair Framework for Programming Tutors
authors: Burak Yang, Haoye Tian, Zhiyu Pian, Christoph Treude, Jacques Klein
abstract: Programming education requires explaining bugs, not just fixing them. We present CREF, a conversational repair framework for programming tutors that uses LLMs to generate explanatory repairs - patches accompanied by natural language explanations of the bug cause and fix rationale.
summary: Proposes CREF for educational program repair, generating patches with natural language explanations of bug causes and fixes.
summary_zh: 提出CREF用于教育程序修复，生成带有漏洞原因和修复理由自然语言解释的补丁。
