experiment.key_results: 83% of explanations rated helpful by students
experiment.real_world_impact: Deployed in university programming courses

contributions: Proposes CREF, a novel framework for for educational program repair, generating patches with natural language explanations of bug causes and fixes.
Utilizes GPT-4, GPT-3.5 for automated patch generation with semantic understanding of code context.
Evaluates on Student code submissions benchmark, providing comprehensive empirical evidence.
Demonstrates strong empirical results: 83% of explanations rated helpful by students.
Addresses logic-error, syntax-error vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.
contributions_zh: 提出 CREF, a novel framework for for educational program repair, generating patches with natural language explanations of bug causes and fixes.
利用 GPT-4, GPT-3.5 for automated patch generation with semantic understanding of code context.
在以下数据集上进行评估： Student code submissions benchmark, providing comprehensive empirical evidence.
证明了 strong empirical results: 83% of explanations rated helpful by students.
解决 logic-error, syntax-error vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.
abstract_zh: 提出CREF用于教育程序修复，生成带有漏洞原因和修复理由自然语言解释的补丁。
research_questions: Can LLMs generate not only correct patches but also pedagogically useful explanations of bug causes and fix rationales?
How do students perceive the helpfulness of LLM-generated explanatory repairs compared to patches alone?
How does CREF compare to traditional educational repair tools (CLARA, Refactory) in both repair quality and explanation quality?
research_questions_zh: LLM能否不仅生成正确的补丁，还能生成对教学有用的缺陷原因和修复理由解释？
学生如何评价LLM生成的解释性修复与仅提供补丁相比的帮助程度？
CREF在修复质量和解释质量方面与CLARA和Refactory等传统教育修复工具相比表现如何？
conclusions: 83% of students found the explanations generated by CREF helpful for understanding defects, demonstrating that LLMs can effectively serve in an explanatory repair role in programming tutoring.
The system has been deployed in a university programming course, handling student code submissions in Python and Java, covering both logic and syntax errors.

Compared to approaches that only provide patches, repairs accompanied by natural language explanations significantly improved students' understanding of defects and subsequent programming performance.
conclusions_zh: 83%的学生认为CREF生成的解释对理解缺陷有帮助，证明LLM能有效承担编程辅导中的解释性修复角色。
该系统已部署在大学编程课程中，处理Python和Java的学生代码提交，涵盖逻辑错误和语法错误。
与仅提供补丁的方法相比，带有自然语言解释的修复显著提高了学生的缺陷理解能力和后续编程表现。

title: LLM-Assisted Merge Conflict Resolution
authors: Yannic Noller, Lingming Zhang
abstract: Merge conflicts are a common issue in collaborative development. We propose using LLMs to understand the intent of conflicting changes and generate correct merge resolutions. By analyzing both branches' changes and their test suites, the LLM generates semantically correct merges.
summary: Uses LLMs to understand conflicting code changes and generate correct merge conflict resolutions.
summary_zh: 利用LLM理解冲突代码变更并生成正确的合并冲突解决方案。

experiment.key_results: Correctly resolves 72% of merge conflicts vs 45% for IntelliMerge

contributions: Proposes a novel approach for LLMs to understand conflicting code changes and generate correct merge conflict resolutions.
Utilizes GPT-4 for automated patch generation with semantic understanding of code context.
Evaluates on MergeConflict benchmark benchmark, providing comprehensive empirical evidence.
Demonstrates strong empirical results: Correctly resolves 72% of merge conflicts vs 45% for IntelliMerge.
Addresses merge-conflict vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.
contributions_zh: 提出 a novel approach for LLMs to understand conflicting code changes and generate correct merge conflict resolutions.
利用 GPT-4 for automated patch generation with semantic understanding of code context.
在以下数据集上进行评估： MergeConflict benchmark benchmark, providing comprehensive empirical evidence.
证明了 strong empirical results: Correctly resolves 72% of merge conflicts vs 45% for IntelliMerge.
解决 merge-conflict vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.
abstract_zh: 利用LLM理解冲突代码变更并生成正确的合并冲突解决方案。
research_questions: Can LLMs understand the intent behind conflicting code changes from different branches and generate semantically correct merge resolutions?
How does LLM-assisted merge resolution compare to IntelliMerge and git's default three-way merge?
What types of merge conflicts benefit most from LLM-based semantic understanding?
research_questions_zh: LLM能否理解不同分支冲突代码更改背后的意图并生成语义正确的合并解决方案？
LLM辅助的合并解决与IntelliMerge和git的默认三方合并相比效果如何？
哪些类型的合并冲突从基于LLM的语义理解中获益最大？
conclusions: The LLM correctly resolved 72% of merge conflicts, compared to only 45% for IntelliMerge, indicating that the LLM's understanding of code change intent produces more accurate merge resolutions.
Conflicts involving semantically overlapping changes (e.g., two branches modifying different aspects of the same logic) benefit the most from LLM comprehension.
By simultaneously analyzing changes from both branches and their test suites, the LLM can infer the optimal merge strategy that preserves the intent of both sides.
conclusions_zh: LLM正确解决了72%的合并冲突，而IntelliMerge仅为45%，表明LLM对代码变更意图的理解能生成更准确的合并方案。
涉及语义重叠变更（如两个分支修改同一逻辑的不同方面）的冲突从LLM理解中受益最大。
通过同时分析两个分支的变更及其测试套件，LLM能推断出保留双方意图的最优合并策略。

title: A Systematic Study of LLMs as Fault Localizers and Repairers
authors: Yonghao Cao, Quanjun Zhang, Chunrong Fang
abstract: We conduct a systematic study of LLMs' capabilities in both fault localization and program repair. We evaluate whether LLMs can accurately identify buggy locations and whether this localization improves repair. Our study covers 5 LLMs on 3 benchmarks across 3 languages.
summary: Systematic study of LLMs for both fault localization and program repair across 5 models, 3 benchmarks, and 3 languages.
summary_zh: 系统研究LLM在5个模型、3个基准和3种语言上的故障定位和程序修复能力。

experiment.key_results: LLMs localize 55% of bugs correctly, joint localization+repair improves by 20%

contributions: Proposes a novel approach for systematic study of LLMs for both fault localization and program repair across 5 models, 3 benchmarks, and 3 languages.
Utilizes GPT-4, GPT-3.5, Claude for automated patch generation with semantic understanding of code context.
Evaluates on Defects4J, BugsInPy, ManyBugs benchmarks, providing comprehensive empirical evidence.
Demonstrates strong empirical results: LLMs localize 55% of bugs correctly, joint localization+repair improves by 20%.
Addresses logic-error vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.
contributions_zh: 提出 a novel approach for systematic study of LLMs for both fault localization and program repair across 5 models, 3 benchmarks, and 3 languages.
利用 GPT-4, GPT-3.5, Claude for automated patch generation with semantic understanding of code context.
在以下数据集上进行评估： Defects4J, BugsInPy, ManyBugs benchmarks, providing comprehensive empirical evidence.
证明了 strong empirical results: LLMs localize 55% of bugs correctly, joint localization+repair improves by 20%.
解决 logic-error vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.
abstract_zh: 系统研究LLM在5个模型、3个基准和3种语言上的故障定位和程序修复能力。
research_questions: Can LLMs accurately perform fault localization, and does LLM-based localization improve subsequent repair?
How do 5 different LLMs (GPT-4, GPT-3.5, Claude, Llama-2, CodeLlama) compare in combined fault localization and repair across 3 benchmarks?
What is the interaction between localization accuracy and repair success in LLM-based program repair?
research_questions_zh: LLM能否准确执行缺陷定位，且基于LLM的定位是否能改善后续修复？
5种不同LLM（GPT-4、GPT-3.5、Claude、Llama-2、CodeLlama）在3个基准上的联合缺陷定位和修复表现如何比较？
在基于LLM的程序修复中，定位准确性与修复成功之间的相互作用如何？
conclusions: LLMs can correctly localize 55% of defects, and the joint localization-repair approach improves performance by 20% over the separated approach, indicating that an end-to-end localization-repair pipeline is more effective.
GPT-4 leads in both localization and repair dimensions, though the open-source model CodeLlama has approached Claude's performance in Java repair.
The systematic evaluation across three cross-language benchmarks -- Defects4J, BugsInPy, and ManyBugs -- provides a comprehensive reference for future LLM repair research.
conclusions_zh: LLM能正确定位55%的缺陷，联合定位与修复方法比分离式方法提升20%，表明端到端的定位修复流程更有效。
GPT-4在定位和修复两个维度上均领先，但开源模型CodeLlama在Java修复上的性能已接近Claude。
在Defects4J、BugsInPy和ManyBugs三个跨语言基准上的系统评估为未来LLM修复研究提供了全面的对比参考。

title: TypeFix: LLM-Based Type Error Repair for TypeScript
authors: Hao Wu, Yanlin Chen, Yiling Lou, Xin Peng
abstract: TypeScript's complex type system creates challenging type errors for developers. We present TypeFix, which uses LLMs to understand TypeScript's type system and generate correct fixes for type errors, handling union types, generics, conditional types, and complex type inference.
summary: Uses LLMs to fix TypeScript type errors including union types, generics, and complex type inference.
summary_zh: 利用LLM修复TypeScript类型错误，包括联合类型、泛型和复杂类型推断。

experiment.key_results: 75% fix rate for TypeScript type errors

contributions: Proposes TypeFix, a novel approach for LLMs to fix TypeScript type errors including union types, generics, and complex type inference.
Utilizes GPT-4, GPT-3.5 for automated patch generation with semantic understanding of code context.
Evaluates on TypeBugs benchmark benchmark, providing comprehensive empirical evidence.
Demonstrates strong empirical results: 75% fix rate for TypeScript type errors.
Addresses type-error vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.
contributions_zh: 提出 TypeFix, a novel approach for LLMs to fix TypeScript type errors including union types, generics, and complex type inference.
利用 GPT-4, GPT-3.5 for automated patch generation with semantic understanding of code context.
在以下数据集上进行评估： TypeBugs benchmark benchmark, providing comprehensive empirical evidence.
证明了 strong empirical results: 75% fix rate for TypeScript type errors.
解决 type-error vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.
abstract_zh: 利用LLM修复TypeScript类型错误，包括联合类型、泛型和复杂类型推断。
research_questions: How effectively can LLMs fix TypeScript's complex type errors involving union types, generics, conditional types, and type inference?
Which categories of TypeScript type errors are most and least amenable to LLM-based repair?
Does providing TypeScript-specific type system knowledge in prompts improve fix rates over generic code repair prompts?
research_questions_zh: LLM能多有效地修复TypeScript涉及联合类型、泛型、条件类型和类型推断的复杂类型错误？
哪些类别的TypeScript类型错误最适合和最不适合基于LLM的修复？
在提示中提供TypeScript特定的类型系统知识是否比通用代码修复提示能提高修复率？
conclusions: TypeFix achieves a 75% repair rate for TypeScript type errors, with simple type annotation errors exceeding 90% and complex conditional type errors at approximately 50%.
Errors involving generic constraints and conditional type inference are the most challenging categories, requiring the LLM to deeply understand TypeScript's type computation logic.
TypeScript-specific prompting strategies improve the repair rate by approximately 15% compared to generic code repair prompts, demonstrating the importance of domain knowledge in type error repair.
conclusions_zh: TypeFix对TypeScript类型错误的修复率达75%，其中简单类型标注错误修复率超过90%，而复杂条件类型错误仅约50%。
涉及泛型约束和条件类型推断的错误是最具挑战性的类别，需要LLM深入理解TypeScript的类型计算逻辑。
TypeScript特定的提示策略比通用代码修复提示提高了约15%的修复率，证明领域知识在类型错误修复中的重要性。

title: How Well Do LLMs Generate Security Patches? A Comprehensive Evaluation
authors: Shunyu Zhang, Zhen Li, Yan Cai, Tao Wang
abstract: We comprehensively evaluate LLMs' ability to generate security patches for real-world CVEs. Our evaluation covers 1,000 CVEs across different CWE categories, evaluating multiple LLMs on patch correctness, completeness, and security impact.
summary: Comprehensive evaluation of LLMs generating security patches for 1,000 CVEs across different CWE categories.
summary_zh: 全面评估LLM为不同CWE类别的1000个CVE生成安全补丁的能力。

experiment.key_results: GPT-4 generates correct patches for 45% of CVEs, CWE-119 hardest

contributions: Proposes a novel approach for comprehensive evaluation of LLMs generating security patches for 1,000 CVEs across different CWE categories.
Utilizes GPT-4, Claude, Llama-2 for automated patch generation with semantic understanding of code context.
Evaluates on CVEFixes, Big-Vul benchmarks, providing comprehensive empirical evidence.
Demonstrates strong empirical results: GPT-4 generates correct patches for 45% of CVEs, CWE-119 hardest.
Addresses CWE-119, CWE-416, CWE-787 vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.
contributions_zh: 提出 a novel approach for comprehensive evaluation of LLMs generating security patches for 1,000 CVEs across different CWE categories.
利用 GPT-4, Claude, Llama-2 for automated patch generation with semantic understanding of code context.
在以下数据集上进行评估： CVEFixes, Big-Vul benchmarks, providing comprehensive empirical evidence.
证明了 strong empirical results: GPT-4 generates correct patches for 45% of CVEs, CWE-119 hardest.
解决 CWE-119, CWE-416, CWE-787 vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.
abstract_zh: 全面评估LLM为不同CWE类别的1000个CVE生成安全补丁的能力。
research_questions: How well do different LLMs (GPT-4, Claude, Llama-2, CodeLlama) generate correct security patches across diverse CWE categories?
Which CWE categories are most challenging for LLM-based security patch generation?
How do factors like patch completeness and security impact vary across different LLMs and vulnerability types?
research_questions_zh: 不同LLM（GPT-4、Claude、Llama-2、CodeLlama）在不同CWE类别中生成正确安全补丁的能力如何？
哪些CWE类别对基于LLM的安全补丁生成最具挑战性？
补丁完整性和安全影响等因素在不同LLM和漏洞类型之间如何变化？
conclusions: GPT-4 generated correct patches for 45% of CVEs, with CWE-119 (buffer overflow) being the most difficult to repair due to the typical need to understand complex memory layouts.
In the large-scale evaluation of 1,000 CVEs, all LLMs performed best on CWE-20 (improper input validation), while the repair rate for CWE-416 (use-after-free) was generally below 30%.
Commercial models (GPT-4, Claude) significantly outperform open-source models (Llama-2, CodeLlama) in security patch generation, with a gap of 15-20 percentage points.
conclusions_zh: GPT-4为45%的CVE生成了正确补丁，其中CWE-119（缓冲区溢出）最难修复，因其通常需要理解复杂的内存布局。
在1,000个CVE的大规模评估中，所有LLM在CWE-20（输入验证不当）上表现最佳，而CWE-416（释放后使用）的修复率普遍低于30%。
商业模型（GPT-4、Claude）在安全补丁生成上显著优于开源模型（Llama-2、CodeLlama），差距达15-20个百分点。

title: APR-Bench: A Unified Benchmark for Automated Program Repair
authors: Yuxiang Wu, He Ye, Martin Monperrus
abstract: Existing APR benchmarks are fragmented across languages and evaluation criteria. We present APR-Bench, a unified benchmark spanning Java, Python, C, and JavaScript with standardized evaluation metrics for comparing APR tools including LLM-based approaches.
summary: Introduces APR-Bench, a unified multilingual benchmark with standardized metrics for comparing APR tools including LLM-based approaches.
summary_zh: 引入APR-Bench，统一的多语言基准，使用标准化指标比较包括基于LLM方法在内的APR工具。

experiment.key_results: GPT-4 leads across all languages, Python easiest, C hardest

contributions: Proposes APR-Bench, a novel approach for unified multilingual benchmark with standardized metrics for comparing APR tools including LLM-based approaches.
Utilizes GPT-4, CodeLlama, StarCoder for automated patch generation with semantic understanding of code context.
Evaluates on APR-Bench benchmark, providing comprehensive empirical evidence.
Demonstrates strong empirical results: GPT-4 leads across all languages, Python easiest, C hardest.
Addresses logic-error vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.
contributions_zh: 提出 APR-Bench, a novel approach for unified multilingual benchmark with standardized metrics for comparing APR tools including LLM-based approaches.
利用 GPT-4, CodeLlama, StarCoder for automated patch generation with semantic understanding of code context.
在以下数据集上进行评估： APR-Bench benchmark, providing comprehensive empirical evidence.
证明了 strong empirical results: GPT-4 leads across all languages, Python easiest, C hardest.
解决 logic-error vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.
abstract_zh: 引入APR-Bench，统一的多语言基准，使用标准化指标比较包括基于LLM方法在内的APR工具。
research_questions: Can a unified benchmark with standardized evaluation metrics enable fair comparison of APR tools across Java, Python, C, and JavaScript?
How does repair difficulty vary across programming languages for the same classes of bugs?
How do LLM-based repair approaches (GPT-4, CodeLlama, StarCoder) compare to traditional APR tools (TBar, CURE) on a unified benchmark?
research_questions_zh: 具有标准化评估指标的统一基准能否实现APR工具在Java、Python、C和JavaScript之间的公平比较？
对于相同类别的缺陷，修复难度在不同编程语言之间如何变化？
基于LLM的修复方法（GPT-4、CodeLlama、StarCoder）在统一基准上与传统APR工具（TBar、CURE）相比表现如何？
conclusions: GPT-4 leads across all four languages, with Python defects being the easiest to repair and C defects the most difficult, reflecting the impact of language complexity on repair.
The unified benchmark eliminates methodological differences in cross-study comparisons, revealing that some previously claimed tool advantages no longer hold under standardized evaluation.
APR-Bench contains 2,000 defects across four languages and is released as an open-source benchmark, providing the APR research community with a much-needed unified evaluation platform.
conclusions_zh: GPT-4在所有四种语言上均领先，Python缺陷最易修复而C缺陷最难，反映了语言复杂性对修复的影响。
统一基准消除了跨研究比较中的方法论差异，发现此前声称的部分工具优势在标准化评估下不再成立。
APR-Bench包含2,000个跨四种语言的缺陷，作为开源基准发布，为APR研究社区提供了急需的统一评估平台。

title: CodeAgent: Autonomous Code Repair Agent via Retrieval-Augmented Generation
authors: Kechi Zhang, Jia Li, Ge Li, Zhi Jin
abstract: We present CodeAgent, an autonomous code repair agent that uses retrieval-augmented generation to find relevant code contexts and fix patterns. CodeAgent maintains a long-term memory of successful repairs and retrieves relevant patterns when encountering similar bugs.
summary: Proposes CodeAgent, an autonomous repair agent with long-term memory and retrieval-augmented generation for bug fixing.
summary_zh: 提出CodeAgent，具有长期记忆和检索增强生成能力的自主修复代理，用于漏洞修复。

experiment.key_results: RAG improves repair success by 15% over non-RAG approaches

contributions: Proposes CodeAgent, a novel approach for autonomous repair agent with long-term memory and retrieval-augmented generation for bug fixing.
Utilizes GPT-4 for automated patch generation with semantic understanding of code context.
Evaluates on SWE-bench, Defects4J benchmarks, providing comprehensive empirical evidence.
Demonstrates strong empirical results: RAG improves repair success by 15% over non-RAG approaches.
Addresses github-issue, logic-error vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.
contributions_zh: 提出 CodeAgent, a novel approach for autonomous repair agent with long-term memory and retrieval-augmented generation for bug fixing.
利用 GPT-4 for automated patch generation with semantic understanding of code context.
在以下数据集上进行评估： SWE-bench, Defects4J benchmarks, providing comprehensive empirical evidence.
证明了 strong empirical results: RAG improves repair success by 15% over non-RAG approaches.
解决 github-issue, logic-error vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.
abstract_zh: 提出CodeAgent，具有长期记忆和检索增强生成能力的自主修复代理，用于漏洞修复。
research_questions: Does maintaining a long-term memory of successful repairs and retrieving relevant patterns improve autonomous code repair?
How does retrieval-augmented generation compare to non-RAG approaches for LLM-based program repair?
Can CodeAgent leverage past repair experiences to handle novel but similar bugs more effectively?
research_questions_zh: 维护成功修复的长期记忆并检索相关模式是否能改善自主代码修复？
检索增强生成在基于LLM的程序修复中与非RAG方法相比效果如何？
CodeAgent能否利用过去的修复经验更有效地处理新的但相似的缺陷？
conclusions: Retrieval-augmented generation improves the repair success rate by 15% over non-RAG methods, demonstrating that retrieval of historical fix patterns can effectively guide the repair of new defects.
The long-term memory mechanism enables CodeAgent to accumulate experience during the repair process, progressively improving repair efficiency for subsequent defects in the same project.
Evaluation on SWE-bench and Defects4J shows that CodeAgent outperforms SWE-agent and Agentless in complex multi-file repair scenarios.
conclusions_zh: 检索增强生成使修复成功率比非RAG方法提高了15%，证明历史修复模式的检索能有效指导新缺陷的修复。
长期记忆机制使CodeAgent能在修复过程中积累经验，对同一项目中的后续缺陷修复效率逐步提升。
在SWE-bench和Defects4J上的评估显示，CodeAgent与SWE-agent和Agentless相比在复杂多文件修复场景中表现更优。

title: CodeRL: Program Repair via Reinforcement Learning with LLMs
authors: Hung Le, Yue Wang, Akhilesh Gotmare, Silvio Savarese, Steven Hoi
abstract: We propose CodeRL, which uses reinforcement learning to fine-tune LLMs for program repair. By using test outcomes as reward signals, CodeRL trains the LLM to generate patches that are more likely to pass test suites, combining the generative capability of LLMs with RL optimization.
summary: Uses reinforcement learning with test outcomes as rewards to fine-tune LLMs for program repair.
summary_zh: 使用测试结果作为奖励的强化学习来微调LLM进行程序修复。

experiment.key_results: RL training improves pass@1 by 25% over supervised fine-tuning

contributions: Proposes CodeRL, a novel approach for reinforcement learning with test outcomes as rewards to fine-tune LLMs for program repair.
Utilizes CodeT5 for automated patch generation with semantic understanding of code context.
Evaluates on APPS, MBPP benchmarks, providing comprehensive empirical evidence.
Demonstrates strong empirical results: RL training improves pass@1 by 25% over supervised fine-tuning.
Addresses logic-error vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.
contributions_zh: 提出 CodeRL, a novel approach for reinforcement learning with test outcomes as rewards to fine-tune LLMs for program repair.
利用 CodeT5 for automated patch generation with semantic understanding of code context.
在以下数据集上进行评估： APPS, MBPP benchmarks, providing comprehensive empirical evidence.
证明了 strong empirical results: RL training improves pass@1 by 25% over supervised fine-tuning.
解决 logic-error vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.
abstract_zh: 使用测试结果作为奖励的强化学习来微调LLM进行程序修复。
research_questions: Can reinforcement learning with test outcomes as reward signals improve LLM-based program repair over supervised fine-tuning?
How does RL-optimized patch generation compare to supervised fine-tuning and zero-shot approaches?
What reward signal design is most effective for training code repair models via reinforcement learning?
research_questions_zh: 以测试结果作为奖励信号的强化学习能否比监督微调改善基于LLM的程序修复？
RL优化的补丁生成与监督微调和零样本方法相比效果如何？
哪种奖励信号设计对通过强化学习训练代码修复模型最有效？
conclusions: RL training improved pass@1 by 25% over supervised fine-tuning, demonstrating that test execution feedback as a reward signal can effectively guide the model to generate patches more likely to pass test suites.
PPO-based RL fine-tuning enables CodeT5-770M to outperform Codex's zero-shot performance on APPS and MBPP, showcasing the potential for smaller models to become competitive through RL.

A composite reward function combining compilation success and test passing is more effective than a single reward signal, as it simultaneously guides both code correctness and functional completeness.
conclusions_zh: RL训练使pass@1比监督微调提高了25%，证明测试执行反馈作为奖励信号能有效引导模型生成更可能通过测试的补丁。
基于PPO算法的RL微调使CodeT5-770M在APPS和MBPP上优于Codex的零样本性能，展现了小模型通过RL获得竞争力的潜力。
结合编译成功和测试通过的复合奖励函数比单一奖励信号更有效，因其能同时引导代码正确性和功能完整性。

title: Automated Smart Contract Vulnerability Repair using LLMs
authors: Jiawei Chen, Hai Zhong, Zibin Zheng, Xiapu Luo
abstract: Smart contract vulnerabilities require timely patching to prevent financial losses. We propose an LLM-based approach for automatically repairing common Solidity vulnerabilities including reentrancy, integer overflow, and access control issues, while preserving contract functionality.
summary: Uses LLMs to automatically repair Solidity vulnerabilities (reentrancy, overflow, access control) while preserving functionality.
summary_zh: 利用LLM自动修复Solidity漏洞（重入、溢出、访问控制），同时保持合约功能。

experiment.key_results: 68% of patches correctly fix vulnerability without breaking functionality

contributions: Proposes a novel approach for LLMs to automatically repair Solidity vulnerabilities (reentrancy, overflow, access control) while preserving functional.
Utilizes GPT-4 for automated patch generation with semantic understanding of code context.
Evaluates on SmartBugs, SWC Registry benchmarks, providing comprehensive empirical evidence.
Demonstrates strong empirical results: 68% of patches correctly fix vulnerability without breaking functionality.
Addresses reentrancy, integer-overflow, access-control vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.
contributions_zh: 提出 a novel approach for LLMs to automatically repair Solidity vulnerabilities (reentrancy, overflow, access control) while preserving functional.
利用 GPT-4 for automated patch generation with semantic understanding of code context.
在以下数据集上进行评估： SmartBugs, SWC Registry benchmarks, providing comprehensive empirical evidence.
证明了 strong empirical results: 68% of patches correctly fix vulnerability without breaking functionality.
解决 reentrancy, integer-overflow, access-control vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.
abstract_zh: 利用LLM自动修复Solidity漏洞（重入、溢出、访问控制），同时保持合约功能。
research_questions: Can LLMs automatically repair common Solidity vulnerabilities (reentrancy, integer overflow, access control) while preserving contract functionality?
How do LLM-generated patches compare to rule-based tools (sGuard, SmartShield) in both correctness and functionality preservation?
What proportion of LLM-generated smart contract patches introduce new vulnerabilities or break existing functionality?
research_questions_zh: LLM能否自动修复常见的Solidity漏洞（重入攻击、整数溢出、访问控制）同时保持合约功能？
LLM生成的补丁在正确性和功能保持方面与sGuard和SmartShield等基于规则的工具相比如何？
LLM生成的智能合约补丁中有多大比例会引入新漏洞或破坏现有功能？
conclusions: 68% of LLM-generated patches correctly repaired vulnerabilities without breaking contract functionality, outperforming pattern-based tools such as sGuard and SmartShield.
Reentrancy attack vulnerabilities had the highest repair rate due to their well-defined fix pattern (checks-effects-interactions), while access control vulnerabilities were the most challenging.
Combining Slither static analysis for patch verification can effectively filter out incorrect patches that introduce new vulnerabilities, further improving the final effective patch rate.
conclusions_zh: 68%的LLM生成补丁正确修复了漏洞且不破坏合约功能，优于sGuard和SmartShield等基于模式的工具。
重入攻击漏洞的修复率最高，因其有明确的修复模式（checks-effects-interactions），而访问控制漏洞最具挑战性。
结合Slither静态分析进行补丁验证，可有效过滤引入新漏洞的不正确补丁，将最终有效补丁率进一步提升。

title: A Large-Scale Evaluation of LLMs for Code Repair Across 15 Languages
authors: Raymond Li, Loubna Ben Allal, Niklas Muennighoff, Leandro von Werra
abstract: We present the largest evaluation of LLM-based code repair to date, covering 15 programming languages and 10,000 bugs. Our evaluation reveals significant variation in repair performance across languages and identifies language features that make repair easier or harder.
summary: Largest evaluation of LLM code repair covering 15 languages and 10,000 bugs, revealing language-specific repair difficulty patterns.
summary_zh: 迄今最大规模的LLM代码修复评估，覆盖15种语言和10000个漏洞，揭示语言特定的修复难度模式。

experiment.key_results: Python easiest (72% fix rate), Haskell hardest (28%), strong model size correlation

contributions: Proposes a novel approach for largest evaluation of LLM code repair covering 15 languages and 10,000 bugs, revealing language-specific repair difficul.
Utilizes GPT-4, Claude-3, Llama-3 for automated patch generation with semantic understanding of code context.
Evaluates on MultiRepair benchmark, providing comprehensive empirical evidence.
Demonstrates strong empirical results: Python easiest (72% fix rate), Haskell hardest (28%), strong model size correlation.
Addresses logic-error, syntax-error, runtime-error vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.
contributions_zh: 提出 a novel approach for largest evaluation of LLM code repair covering 15 languages and 10,000 bugs, revealing language-specific repair difficul.
利用 GPT-4, Claude-3, Llama-3 for automated patch generation with semantic understanding of code context.
在以下数据集上进行评估： MultiRepair benchmark, providing comprehensive empirical evidence.
证明了 strong empirical results: Python easiest (72% fix rate), Haskell hardest (28%), strong model size correlation.
解决 logic-error, syntax-error, runtime-error vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.
abstract_zh: 迄今最大规模的LLM代码修复评估，覆盖15种语言和10000个漏洞，揭示语言特定的修复难度模式。
research_questions: How does LLM code repair performance vary across 15 programming languages, and what language features drive this variation?
Is there a correlation between model size and repair effectiveness across different programming languages?
Which programming language families (functional, imperative, dynamic, static) are easiest and hardest for LLMs to repair?
research_questions_zh: LLM代码修复性能在15种编程语言之间如何变化，哪些语言特征驱动了这种差异？
模型规模与不同编程语言的修复效果之间是否存在相关性？
哪些编程语言家族（函数式、命令式、动态、静态）对LLM修复最容易和最困难？
conclusions: Python had the highest repair rate (72%) and Haskell the lowest (28%), with dynamically typed languages generally being easier to repair than statically typed languages.
There is a strong positive correlation between model size and repair effectiveness, but language-specific differences persist even in the largest models.
This is the largest-scale LLM code repair evaluation to date (15 languages, 10,000 defects), revealing uneven LLM capabilities across different programming paradigms.
conclusions_zh: Python修复率最高（72%），Haskell最低（28%），动态类型语言普遍比静态类型语言更易修复。
模型规模与修复效果之间存在强正相关，但语言特异性差异即使在最大模型上也持续存在。
这是迄今最大规模的LLM代码修复评估（15种语言、10,000个缺陷），揭示了LLM在不同编程范式上的能力不均衡。

title: Domain-Specific LLM Fine-Tuning for Automated Security Patch Generation
authors: Xin Peng, Zihan Chen, Xiang Gao, Shin Hwei Tan
abstract: Generic LLMs struggle with domain-specific security patterns. We fine-tune code LLMs specifically for security patch generation using a curated dataset of security patches from CVE databases. The fine-tuned model significantly outperforms generic LLMs on security-specific repairs.
summary: Fine-tunes code LLMs specifically for security patch generation using curated CVE patch datasets, outperforming generic LLMs.
summary_zh: 使用精选的CVE补丁数据集专门微调代码LLM用于安全补丁生成，超越通用LLM。

experiment.key_results: Fine-tuned 13B model matches GPT-4 on security patches at 100x lower cost

contributions: Proposes a novel approach for fine-tunes code LLMs specifically for security patch generation using curated CVE patch datasets, outperforming generic.
Utilizes CodeLlama, StarCoder for automated patch generation with semantic understanding of code context.
Evaluates on CVEFixes, PatchDB benchmarks, providing comprehensive empirical evidence.
Demonstrates strong empirical results: Fine-tuned 13B model matches GPT-4 on security patches at 100x lower cost.
Addresses CWE-119, CWE-416, CWE-20 vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.
contributions_zh: 提出 a novel approach for fine-tunes code LLMs specifically for security patch generation using curated CVE patch datasets, outperforming generic.
利用 CodeLlama, StarCoder for automated patch generation with semantic understanding of code context.
在以下数据集上进行评估： CVEFixes, PatchDB benchmarks, providing comprehensive empirical evidence.
证明了 strong empirical results: Fine-tuned 13B model matches GPT-4 on security patches at 100x lower cost.
解决 CWE-119, CWE-416, CWE-20 vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.
abstract_zh: 使用精选的CVE补丁数据集专门微调代码LLM用于安全补丁生成，超越通用LLM。
research_questions: Does fine-tuning code LLMs specifically on security patch data improve patch generation compared to generic LLMs?
Can a domain-specific fine-tuned 13B model match GPT-4's performance on security patch generation?
What is the cost-effectiveness trade-off between fine-tuned open-source models and commercial LLMs for security patching?
research_questions_zh: 在安全补丁数据上专门微调代码LLM是否比通用LLM能改善补丁生成？
经领域特定微调的13B模型能否在安全补丁生成上匹配GPT-4的性能？
微调的开源模型与商业LLM在安全补丁生成方面的性价比权衡如何？
conclusions: A LoRA-fine-tuned 13B model matches GPT-4 performance on security patch generation at only one-hundredth of the inference cost.
Domain fine-tuning with 5,000 security patches from CVEFixes and PatchDB significantly improved the model's understanding of security vulnerability patterns such as CWE-119 and CWE-416.

The performance improvement of the base CodeLlama model after fine-tuning on security patch tasks far exceeds its gap on general code generation, demonstrating the high return on investment of security domain fine-tuning.
conclusions_zh: 经LoRA微调的13B模型在安全补丁生成上匹配GPT-4性能，但推理成本仅为后者的百分之一。
使用CVEFixes和PatchDB中的5,000个安全补丁进行领域微调，显著提升了模型对CWE-119、CWE-416等安全漏洞模式的理解。
基础CodeLlama模型微调后在安全补丁任务上的性能提升幅度远超其在通用代码生成上的差距，表明安全领域微调的高回报率。

title: Fixing Flaky Tests with Large Language Models
authors: Wing Li, Jonathan Bader, Steffen Hillemacher
abstract: Flaky tests that non-deterministically pass or fail waste developer time. We evaluate LLMs' ability to fix flaky tests by analyzing test code and identifying sources of non-determinism such as timing issues, shared state, and order dependencies.
summary: Evaluates LLMs fixing flaky tests by identifying non-determinism sources like timing issues and shared state.
summary_zh: 评估LLM通过识别时序问题和共享状态等非确定性来源来修复不稳定测试的能力。

experiment.key_results: GPT-4 fixes 58% of flaky tests correctly

contributions: Proposes a approach for LLMs fixing flaky tests by identifying non-determinism sources like timing issues and shared state.
Utilizes GPT-4, GPT-3.5 for automated patch generation with semantic understanding of code context.
Evaluates on iDFlakies, FlakeFlagger benchmarks, providing comprehensive empirical evidence.
Demonstrates strong empirical results: GPT-4 fixes 58% of flaky tests correctly.
Addresses flaky-test vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.
contributions_zh: 提出 a approach for LLMs fixing flaky tests by identifying non-determinism sources like timing issues and shared state.
利用 GPT-4, GPT-3.5 for automated patch generation with semantic understanding of code context.
在以下数据集上进行评估： iDFlakies, FlakeFlagger benchmarks, providing comprehensive empirical evidence.
证明了 strong empirical results: GPT-4 fixes 58% of flaky tests correctly.
解决 flaky-test vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.
abstract_zh: 评估LLM通过识别时序问题和共享状态等非确定性来源来修复不稳定测试的能力。
research_questions: Can LLMs identify sources of non-determinism (timing issues, shared state, order dependencies) in flaky tests and generate correct fixes?
How does GPT-4 compare to GPT-3.5 in diagnosing and fixing different categories of flaky tests?
What types of flakiness (concurrency, timing, resource, order-dependent) are most amenable to LLM-based repair?
research_questions_zh: LLM能否识别不稳定测试中的非确定性来源（时序问题、共享状态、顺序依赖）并生成正确的修复？
GPT-4与GPT-3.5在诊断和修复不同类别的不稳定测试方面如何比较？
哪些类型的不稳定性（并发、时序、资源、顺序依赖）最适合基于LLM的修复？
conclusions: GPT-4 correctly repaired 58% of flaky tests, with timing-related flakiness having the highest repair rate and complex concurrency issues having the lowest.
LLMs excel at identifying shared state and order dependencies, being able to infer potential sources of non-determinism by analyzing test code.
Evaluation on the iDFlakies and FlakeFlagger benchmarks shows that LLMs' ability to repair flaky tests is closely tied to the depth of their understanding of test intent.
conclusions_zh: GPT-4正确修复了58%的不稳定测试，其中时序相关的不稳定性修复率最高，而涉及复杂并发问题的最低。
LLM在识别共享状态和顺序依赖方面表现出色，能通过分析测试代码推断潜在的非确定性来源。
在iDFlakies和FlakeFlagger基准上的评估表明，LLM修复不稳定测试的能力与其对测试意图的理解深度密切相关。

title: HotFix: LLM-Based Automated Hotfix Generation for Production Incidents
authors: Wen Tao, Mark Chen, Shuai Lu, Neel Sundaresan
abstract: Production incidents require rapid response. We present HotFix, a system that uses LLMs to generate quick-fix patches for production issues by analyzing error logs, stack traces, and recent code changes. HotFix prioritizes fix speed and safety over optimization.
summary: Proposes HotFix using LLMs to generate quick patches for production incidents from error logs and stack traces.
summary_zh: 提出HotFix，利用LLM从错误日志和堆栈跟踪为生产事件生成快速补丁。

experiment.key_results: 47% of generated hotfixes were accepted, median time 3 minutes
experiment.real_world_impact: Reduces mean time to fix by 65%

contributions: Proposes HotFix, a novel approach for using LLMs to generate quick patches for production incidents from error logs and stack traces.
Utilizes GPT-4 for automated patch generation with semantic understanding of code context.
Evaluates on Production incidents benchmark, providing comprehensive empirical evidence.
Demonstrates strong empirical results: 47% of generated hotfixes were accepted, median time 3 minutes.
Addresses production-bug vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.
contributions_zh: 提出 HotFix, a novel approach for using LLMs to generate quick patches for production incidents from error logs and stack traces.
利用 GPT-4 for automated patch generation with semantic understanding of code context.
在以下数据集上进行评估： Production incidents benchmark, providing comprehensive empirical evidence.
证明了 strong empirical results: 47% of generated hotfixes were accepted, median time 3 minutes.
解决 production-bug vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.
abstract_zh: 提出HotFix，利用LLM从错误日志和堆栈跟踪为生产事件生成快速补丁。
research_questions: Can LLMs generate safe and deployable hotfix patches for production incidents by analyzing error logs and stack traces?
How does the speed of LLM-generated hotfixes compare to manual developer response times for production incidents?
What is the acceptance rate of LLM-generated hotfixes, and what characteristics distinguish accepted patches from rejected ones?
research_questions_zh: LLM能否通过分析错误日志和堆栈跟踪为生产事故生成安全且可部署的热修复补丁？
LLM生成的热修复速度与开发者手动响应生产事故的时间相比如何？
LLM生成的热修复的接受率是多少？被接受和被拒绝的补丁之间有何区别特征？
conclusions: HotFix achieves a 47% acceptance rate for generated hotfixes across 500 production incidents, with a median generation time of only 3 minutes.
The system reduces mean time to fix production incidents by 65% compared to manual hotfix processes.
HotFix performs best on configuration errors and single-function bugs, but struggles with multi-component issues requiring cross-service understanding.
conclusions_zh: HotFix在500个生产事故中实现了47%的热修复接受率，中位生成时间仅为3分钟。
与手动热修复流程相比，该系统将平均修复时间缩短了65%。
HotFix在配置错误和单函数缺陷上表现最佳，但在需要跨服务理解的多组件问题上表现欠佳。

title: LLM-Based Fault Localization: How Far Are We?
authors: Yiling Ouyang, Quanjun Zhang, Chunrong Fang
abstract: Fault localization is a prerequisite for automated program repair. We evaluate LLMs' fault localization ability, studying how different prompting strategies, context sizes, and model capabilities affect localization accuracy across multiple benchmarks.
summary: Evaluates LLMs for fault localization, studying how prompting strategies and context affect bug location accuracy.
summary_zh: 评估LLM的故障定位能力，研究提示策略和上下文如何影响漏洞定位准确性。

experiment.key_results: GPT-4 localizes 62% of bugs to correct method, 38% to exact line

contributions: Proposes a novel approach for LLMs for fault localization, studying how prompting strategies and context affect bug location accuracy.
Utilizes GPT-4, GPT-3.5, Claude for automated patch generation with semantic understanding of code context.
Evaluates on Defects4J, BugsInPy benchmarks, providing comprehensive empirical evidence.
Demonstrates strong empirical results: GPT-4 localizes 62% of bugs to correct method, 38% to exact line.
Addresses logic-error vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.
contributions_zh: 提出 a novel approach for LLMs for fault localization, studying how prompting strategies and context affect bug location accuracy.
利用 GPT-4, GPT-3.5, Claude for automated patch generation with semantic understanding of code context.
在以下数据集上进行评估： Defects4J, BugsInPy benchmarks, providing comprehensive empirical evidence.
证明了 strong empirical results: GPT-4 localizes 62% of bugs to correct method, 38% to exact line.
解决 logic-error vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.
abstract_zh: 评估LLM的故障定位能力，研究提示策略和上下文如何影响漏洞定位准确性。
research_questions: How accurately can LLMs localize faults at the method and line level compared to dedicated fault localization techniques?
What is the impact of different prompting strategies and context window sizes on LLM fault localization accuracy?
How do commercial LLMs (GPT-4, Claude) compare to open-source models (CodeLlama) for fault localization tasks?
research_questions_zh: 与专用故障定位技术相比，LLM在方法级和行级故障定位上的准确性如何？
不同的提示策略和上下文窗口大小对LLM故障定位准确性有何影响？
商业LLM（GPT-4、Claude）与开源模型（CodeLlama）在故障定位任务上的表现对比如何？
conclusions: GPT-4 successfully localizes 62% of bugs to the correct method and 38% to the exact buggy line across Defects4J and BugsInPy benchmarks.
Providing more code context generally improves localization accuracy up to a threshold, beyond which irrelevant context degrades performance.
Chain-of-thought prompting improves line-level localization by 12% over direct prompting, while few-shot examples provide diminishing returns beyond 3 examples.
conclusions_zh: GPT-4在Defects4J和BugsInPy基准测试中成功将62%的缺陷定位到正确方法，38%定位到确切的缺陷行。
提供更多代码上下文通常能提高定位准确性，但超过一定阈值后，无关上下文反而会降低性能。
思维链提示比直接提示在行级定位上提高了12%，而少样本示例在超过3个后收益递减。

title: PyRepair: LLM-Powered Repair for Python Type Errors and Runtime Exceptions
authors: Yue Jiang, Xin Xu, Peng Shang
abstract: Python's dynamic typing leads to frequent runtime errors. We present PyRepair, which uses LLMs to fix Python type errors and runtime exceptions by understanding Python's dynamic type system and common error patterns, using error traces as diagnostic context.
summary: Uses LLMs to fix Python type errors and runtime exceptions using error traces as diagnostic context.
summary_zh: 利用LLM使用错误跟踪作为诊断上下文修复Python类型错误和运行时异常。

experiment.key_results: 78% fix rate for Python errors, type errors at 82%

contributions: Proposes PyRepair, a novel approach for LLMs to fix Python type errors and runtime exceptions using error traces as diagnostic context.
Utilizes GPT-4, CodeLlama for automated patch generation with semantic understanding of code context.
Evaluates on BugsInPy, TypeBugs-Py benchmarks, providing comprehensive empirical evidence.
Demonstrates strong empirical results: 78% fix rate for Python errors, type errors at 82%.
Addresses type-error, runtime-error vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.
contributions_zh: 提出 PyRepair, a novel approach for LLMs to fix Python type errors and runtime exceptions using error traces as diagnostic context.
利用 GPT-4, CodeLlama for automated patch generation with semantic understanding of code context.
在以下数据集上进行评估： BugsInPy, TypeBugs-Py benchmarks, providing comprehensive empirical evidence.
证明了 strong empirical results: 78% fix rate for Python errors, type errors at 82%.
解决 type-error, runtime-error vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.
abstract_zh: 利用LLM使用错误跟踪作为诊断上下文修复Python类型错误和运行时异常。
research_questions: How effectively can LLMs fix Python type errors and runtime exceptions using error traces as diagnostic context?
Does leveraging Python's dynamic type system information improve LLM-based repair over generic code repair approaches?
How does PyRepair's fix rate vary across different categories of Python errors (type errors vs. runtime exceptions)?
research_questions_zh: LLM利用错误追踪信息作为诊断上下文修复Python类型错误和运行时异常的效果如何？
利用Python动态类型系统信息是否比通用代码修复方法更能提升LLM的修复效果？
PyRepair的修复率在不同类别的Python错误（类型错误与运行时异常）之间有何差异？
conclusions: PyRepair achieves a 78% overall fix rate for Python errors, with type errors specifically reaching an 82% fix rate.
Incorporating error trace information as context boosts repair success by 20% compared to providing only the buggy code.
Fine-tuned CodeLlama approaches GPT-4's performance on common type error patterns while requiring significantly less inference cost.
conclusions_zh: PyRepair对Python错误的总体修复率达到78%，其中类型错误的修复率高达82%。
将错误追踪信息作为上下文纳入后，修复成功率比仅提供有缺陷代码提升了20%。
经微调的CodeLlama在常见类型错误模式上接近GPT-4的表现，同时推理成本显著降低。

title: VulnLLM: Can Large Language Models Find Software Vulnerabilities?
authors: Hao Li, Chao Zhang, Dawn Song
abstract: We study whether LLMs can detect real-world vulnerabilities in C/C++ code. Through a comprehensive evaluation on datasets of known CVEs, we analyze the strengths and limitations of different LLMs and prompting strategies for vulnerability detection.
summary: Studies LLMs' ability to detect real-world C/C++ vulnerabilities through comprehensive CVE-based evaluation.
summary_zh: 研究LLM通过全面的CVE评估检测真实C/C++漏洞的能力。

experiment.key_results: GPT-4 achieves 72% F1, outperforming fine-tuned models on novel CVEs

contributions: Proposes a novel approach for studies LLMs' ability to detect real-world C/C++ vulnerabilities through comprehensive CVE-based evaluation.
Leverages GPT-4, Claude, Llama-2 for automated vulnerability analysis and detection.
Evaluates on Big-Vul, CVEFixes benchmarks, providing comprehensive empirical evidence.
Demonstrates strong empirical results: GPT-4 achieves 72% F1, outperforming fine-tuned models on novel CVEs.
Addresses CWE-119, CWE-416, CWE-787 vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.
contributions_zh: 提出 a novel approach for studies LLMs' ability to detect real-world C/C++ vulnerabilities through comprehensive CVE-based evaluation.
利用 GPT-4, Claude, Llama-2 for automated vulnerability analysis and detection.
在以下数据集上进行评估： Big-Vul, CVEFixes benchmarks, providing comprehensive empirical evidence.
证明了 strong empirical results: GPT-4 achieves 72% F1, outperforming fine-tuned models on novel CVEs.
解决 CWE-119, CWE-416, CWE-787 vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.
abstract_zh: 研究LLM通过全面的CVE评估检测真实C/C++漏洞的能力。
research_questions: Can LLMs detect real-world C/C++ vulnerabilities based on known CVEs, and how do they compare to fine-tuned models?
Which vulnerability types (CWE categories) are LLMs most and least effective at detecting?
How do GPT-4, Claude, and Llama-2 differ in their vulnerability detection accuracy, false positive rates, and reasoning quality?
research_questions_zh: LLM能否基于已知CVE检测真实的C/C++漏洞？与微调模型相比表现如何？
LLM在哪些漏洞类型（CWE类别）上检测效果最好和最差？
GPT-4、Claude和Llama-2在漏洞检测准确率、误报率和推理质量方面有何差异？
conclusions: GPT-4 achieves 72% F1-score on vulnerability detection, outperforming fine-tuned models specifically on novel CVEs not seen during training.
LLMs perform strongest on memory-related vulnerabilities (CWE-119, CWE-416) but struggle with logic and concurrency bugs.
Providing CWE descriptions and vulnerability context in prompts improves detection F1 by 15% compared to generic vulnerability detection prompts.
conclusions_zh: GPT-4在漏洞检测上达到72%的F1分数，在训练期间未见过的新CVE上表现优于微调模型。
LLM在内存相关漏洞（CWE-119、CWE-416）上表现最强，但在逻辑和并发缺陷方面表现不佳。
在提示中提供CWE描述和漏洞上下文可将检测F1提高15%，优于通用漏洞检测提示。

title: A Comprehensive Study of the Capabilities of Large Language Models for Vulnerability Detection
authors: Benjamin Steenhoek, Md Mahbubur Rahman, Wei Le
abstract: We conduct a comprehensive study of LLMs for vulnerability detection, evaluating 16 models across multiple datasets, prompting strategies, and evaluation metrics to understand when and why LLMs succeed or fail at vulnerability detection.
summary: Comprehensive study of 16 LLMs for vulnerability detection across datasets, prompts, and metrics.
summary_zh: 对16个LLM进行漏洞检测的全面研究，涵盖数据集、提示和指标。

experiment.key_results: Closed-source LLMs outperform open-source by 15-20% on vulnerability detection

contributions: Proposes a novel approach for comprehensive study of 16 LLMs for vulnerability detection across datasets, prompts, and metrics.
Leverages GPT-4, GPT-3.5, Claude for automated vulnerability analysis and detection.
Evaluates on Big-Vul, Devign, SARD benchmarks, providing comprehensive empirical evidence.
Demonstrates strong empirical results: Closed-source LLMs outperform open-source by 15-20% on vulnerability detection.
Addresses CWE-119, CWE-20, CWE-416 vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.
contributions_zh: 提出 a novel approach for comprehensive study of 16 LLMs for vulnerability detection across datasets, prompts, and metrics.
利用 GPT-4, GPT-3.5, Claude for automated vulnerability analysis and detection.
在以下数据集上进行评估： Big-Vul, Devign, SARD benchmarks, providing comprehensive empirical evidence.
证明了 strong empirical results: Closed-source LLMs outperform open-source by 15-20% on vulnerability detection.
解决 CWE-119, CWE-20, CWE-416 vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.
abstract_zh: 对16个LLM进行漏洞检测的全面研究，涵盖数据集、提示和指标。
research_questions: How do 16 different LLMs spanning commercial and open-source families perform on vulnerability detection across multiple datasets?
What factors (model size, training data, prompting strategy) most strongly predict vulnerability detection success?
When and why do LLMs fail at vulnerability detection, and what error patterns are most common?
research_questions_zh: 涵盖商业和开源系列的16个不同LLM在多个数据集上的漏洞检测表现如何？
哪些因素（模型规模、训练数据、提示策略）最能预测漏洞检测的成功？
LLM在漏洞检测中何时以及为何失败？最常见的错误模式是什么？
conclusions: Closed-source LLMs (GPT-4, Claude) outperform open-source models by 15-20% on vulnerability detection accuracy across all three benchmarks.
Model size is the strongest predictor of detection accuracy, but diminishing returns appear beyond 13B parameters for open-source models.
LLMs most frequently fail by producing false positives on complex but benign code patterns and missing subtle vulnerabilities requiring whole-program reasoning.
conclusions_zh: 闭源LLM（GPT-4、Claude）在所有三个基准上的漏洞检测准确率比开源模型高15-20%。
模型规模是检测准确率的最强预测因子，但开源模型在超过130亿参数后收益递减。
LLM最常见的失败模式是对复杂但良性的代码模式产生误报，以及遗漏需要全程序推理的细微漏洞。

title: Transformer-Based Language Models for Software Vulnerability Detection
authors: Chandra Thapa, Seung Ick Jang, Muhammad Ejaz Ahmed, Seyit Camtepe, Josef Piber, Surya Nepal
abstract: We evaluate transformer-based language models including GPT-2, BERT, and RoBERTa for vulnerability detection in C/C++ source code. We compare fine-tuned transformers against traditional ML approaches and static analysis tools.
summary: Evaluates transformer models (GPT-2, BERT, RoBERTa) for C/C++ vulnerability detection against ML and static analysis baselines.
summary_zh: 评估Transformer模型（GPT-2、BERT、RoBERTa）在C/C++漏洞检测中的表现，对比ML和静态分析基线。

experiment.key_results: RoBERTa achieves 92% accuracy, outperforming traditional approaches

contributions: Introduces a novel approach for transformer models (GPT-2, BERT, RoBERTa) for C/C++ vulnerability detection against ML and static analysis baselines.
Leverages GPT-2, BERT, RoBERTa for automated vulnerability analysis and detection.
Evaluates on Draper, SARD, Big-Vul benchmarks, providing comprehensive empirical evidence.
Demonstrates strong empirical results: RoBERTa achieves 92% accuracy, outperforming traditional approaches.
Addresses CWE-119, CWE-20, CWE-399 vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.
contributions_zh: 引入 a novel approach for transformer models (GPT-2, BERT, RoBERTa) for C/C++ vulnerability detection against ML and static analysis baselines.
利用 GPT-2, BERT, RoBERTa for automated vulnerability analysis and detection.
在以下数据集上进行评估： Draper, SARD, Big-Vul benchmarks, providing comprehensive empirical evidence.
证明了 strong empirical results: RoBERTa achieves 92% accuracy, outperforming traditional approaches.
解决 CWE-119, CWE-20, CWE-399 vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.
abstract_zh: 评估Transformer模型（GPT-2、BERT、RoBERTa）在C/C++漏洞检测中的表现，对比ML和静态分析基线。
research_questions: How do fine-tuned transformer models (GPT-2, BERT, RoBERTa) compare to traditional ML approaches and static analysis tools for vulnerability detection?
Which transformer architecture achieves the best trade-off between detection accuracy and computational cost for C/C++ vulnerability detection?
How does the choice of pre-training objective affect downstream vulnerability detection performance?
research_questions_zh: 微调的Transformer模型（GPT-2、BERT、RoBERTa）与传统机器学习方法和静态分析工具在漏洞检测上相比如何？
哪种Transformer架构在C/C++漏洞检测的准确性和计算成本之间实现了最佳权衡？
预训练目标的选择如何影响下游漏洞检测性能？
conclusions: RoBERTa achieves 92% accuracy on vulnerability detection, significantly outperforming traditional tools like Flawfinder and CppCheck.
Fine-tuned transformers reduce false positive rates by 40-60% compared to static analysis baselines while maintaining comparable recall.
Masked language modeling pre-training (BERT/RoBERTa) produces superior vulnerability detection performance compared to autoregressive pre-training (GPT-2).
conclusions_zh: RoBERTa在漏洞检测上达到92%的准确率，显著优于Flawfinder和CppCheck等传统工具。
与静态分析基线相比，微调的Transformer模型在保持相当召回率的同时将误报率降低了40-60%。
掩码语言模型预训练（BERT/RoBERTa）在漏洞检测性能上优于自回归预训练（GPT-2）。

title: DiverseVul: A New Vulnerable Source Code Dataset for Deep Learning Based Vulnerability Detection
authors: Yizheng Chen, Zhoujie Ding, Eslam Alowaisheq, Xinyun Wang, David Wagner
abstract: We present DiverseVul, a large-scale vulnerability dataset with 18,945 vulnerable functions from 7,514 commits covering 150 CWEs. We use DiverseVul to evaluate deep learning models for vulnerability detection and find significant room for improvement.
summary: Introduces DiverseVul dataset with 18,945 vulnerable functions covering 150 CWEs for evaluating DL vulnerability detection.
summary_zh: 引入DiverseVul数据集，包含覆盖150种CWE的18945个漏洞函数，用于评估深度学习漏洞检测。

experiment.key_results: Best model achieves only 35% recall at 5% FPR on DiverseVul

contributions: Proposes DiverseVul, a novel approach for dataset with 18,945 vulnerable functions covering 150 CWEs for evaluating DL vulnerability detection.
Leverages CodeBERT, UniXcoder, CodeT5 for automated vulnerability analysis and detection.
Evaluates on DiverseVul benchmark, providing comprehensive empirical evidence.
Demonstrates strong empirical results: Best model achieves only 35% recall at 5% FPR on DiverseVul.
Addresses 150 CWE types vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.
contributions_zh: 提出 DiverseVul, a novel approach for dataset with 18,945 vulnerable functions covering 150 CWEs for evaluating DL vulnerability detection.
利用 CodeBERT, UniXcoder, CodeT5 for automated vulnerability analysis and detection.
在以下数据集上进行评估： DiverseVul benchmark, providing comprehensive empirical evidence.
证明了 strong empirical results: Best model achieves only 35% recall at 5% FPR on DiverseVul.
解决 150 CWE types vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.
abstract_zh: 引入DiverseVul数据集，包含覆盖150种CWE的18945个漏洞函数，用于评估深度学习漏洞检测。
research_questions: Does existing deep learning-based vulnerability detection generalize to a large-scale, diverse dataset covering 150 CWE types?
What are the limitations of current vulnerability detection models when evaluated on DiverseVul's broader vulnerability coverage?
How does dataset diversity and scale affect the measured performance of vulnerability detection models?
research_questions_zh: 现有基于深度学习的漏洞检测能否推广到覆盖150种CWE类型的大规模多样化数据集？
当前漏洞检测模型在DiverseVul更广泛的漏洞覆盖范围上评估时有哪些局限性？
数据集的多样性和规模如何影响漏洞检测模型的测量性能？
conclusions: The best model achieves only 35% recall at 5% false positive rate on DiverseVul, revealing significant gaps in current detection approaches.
Models trained on smaller, less diverse datasets show inflated performance that drops substantially when evaluated on DiverseVul's 150 CWE types.
DiverseVul with 18,945 vulnerable functions from 7,514 commits provides a more realistic benchmark, exposing overfitting in prior evaluations.
conclusions_zh: 最佳模型在DiverseVul上仅以5%误报率实现35%召回率，揭示了当前检测方法的显著差距。
在较小、多样性较低的数据集上训练的模型表现被高估，在DiverseVul的150种CWE类型上评估时大幅下降。
DiverseVul包含来自7,514次提交的18,945个脆弱函数，提供了更真实的基准，暴露了先前评估中的过拟合问题。

title: LineVul: A Transformer-Based Line-Level Vulnerability Prediction
authors: Zhoujie Ding, Yizheng Chen, David Wagner
abstract: Fine-grained vulnerability detection at the line level is more actionable than function-level detection. We present LineVul, a transformer-based approach that predicts vulnerability at the line level using attention mechanisms to identify the most suspicious lines.
summary: Proposes LineVul for line-level vulnerability prediction using transformer attention to identify suspicious code lines.
summary_zh: 提出LineVul，利用Transformer注意力机制识别可疑代码行，实现行级漏洞预测。

experiment.key_results: 91% F1-score, top-10 line accuracy of 74%

contributions: Proposes LineVul, a novel approach for for line-level vulnerability prediction using transformer attention to identify suspicious code lines.
Leverages RoBERTa, CodeBERT for automated vulnerability analysis and detection.
Evaluates on Big-Vul benchmark, providing comprehensive empirical evidence.
Demonstrates strong empirical results: 91% F1-score, top-10 line accuracy of 74%.
Addresses CWE-119, CWE-20, CWE-416 vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.
contributions_zh: 提出 LineVul, a novel approach for for line-level vulnerability prediction using transformer attention to identify suspicious code lines.
利用 RoBERTa, CodeBERT for automated vulnerability analysis and detection.
在以下数据集上进行评估： Big-Vul benchmark, providing comprehensive empirical evidence.
证明了 strong empirical results: 91% F1-score, top-10 line accuracy of 74%.
解决 CWE-119, CWE-20, CWE-416 vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.
abstract_zh: 提出LineVul，利用Transformer注意力机制识别可疑代码行，实现行级漏洞预测。
research_questions: Can transformer attention mechanisms be leveraged to achieve actionable line-level vulnerability prediction?
How does LineVul's line-level prediction accuracy compare to function-level vulnerability detection approaches?
Which attention patterns in transformers are most indicative of vulnerable code lines?
research_questions_zh: 能否利用Transformer注意力机制实现可操作的行级漏洞预测？
LineVul的行级预测准确率与函数级漏洞检测方法相比如何？
Transformer中哪些注意力模式最能指示脆弱代码行？
conclusions: LineVul achieves 91% F1-score at function level and 74% top-10 line accuracy, substantially outperforming IVDetect and Devign.
Attention-based line ranking correctly identifies the most vulnerable line in the top-3 predictions for 58% of vulnerable functions.
The approach provides actionable results by narrowing developer review to specific suspicious lines rather than entire functions.
conclusions_zh: LineVul在函数级达到91%的F1分数和74%的top-10行准确率，大幅优于IVDetect和Devign。
基于注意力的行排序在58%的脆弱函数中能在前3个预测中正确识别最脆弱的行。
该方法通过将开发者审查范围缩小到特定可疑行而非整个函数，提供了可操作的结果。

title: A Survey of Large Language Models for Cyber Threat Detection
authors: Kai Ni, Yangfan Zhang, Lin Chen
abstract: This survey comprehensively reviews LLM applications in cyber threat detection, covering vulnerability detection, malware analysis, intrusion detection, and phishing detection. We analyze 150+ papers and identify trends, challenges, and future directions.
summary: Comprehensive survey reviewing 150+ papers on LLM applications in cyber threat detection across multiple domains.
summary_zh: 全面综述150+篇关于LLM在多个领域网络威胁检测应用的论文。

experiment.key_results: Identifies 5 key challenges and 8 future directions for LLM-based threat detection

contributions: Proposes a novel approach for comprehensive survey reviewing 150+ papers on LLM applications in cyber threat detection across multiple domains.
Provides empirical evaluation demonstrating significant improvements over prior approaches.
Demonstrates strong empirical results: Identifies 5 key challenges and 8 future directions for LLM-based threat detection.
contributions_zh: 提出 a novel approach for comprehensive survey reviewing 150+ papers on LLM applications in cyber threat detection across multiple domains.
提供了实证评估 demonstrating significant improvements over prior approaches.
证明了 strong empirical results: Identifies 5 key challenges and 8 future directions for LLM-based threat detection.
abstract_zh: 全面综述150+篇关于LLM在多个领域网络威胁检测应用的论文。
research_questions: What is the current landscape of LLM applications across different cyber threat detection domains?
What are the key challenges limiting LLM effectiveness in vulnerability detection, malware analysis, and intrusion detection?
What are the most promising future directions for applying LLMs to cyber threat detection?
research_questions_zh: LLM在不同网络威胁检测领域的应用现状如何？
限制LLM在漏洞检测、恶意软件分析和入侵检测中有效性的关键挑战是什么？
将LLM应用于网络威胁检测最有前景的未来方向是什么？
conclusions: The survey identifies 5 key challenges: data scarcity, domain adaptation, explainability, real-time requirements, and adversarial robustness.
LLMs show strongest potential in vulnerability detection and phishing detection, but lag in intrusion detection due to real-time constraints.
Eight future directions are identified, including multimodal threat analysis, LLM-powered threat intelligence, and federated security models.
conclusions_zh: 该综述识别了5个关键挑战：数据稀缺、领域适应、可解释性、实时性要求和对抗鲁棒性。
LLM在漏洞检测和钓鱼检测方面表现出最大潜力，但由于实时性限制在入侵检测方面落后。
文章确定了八个未来方向，包括多模态威胁分析、LLM驱动的威胁情报和联邦安全模型。

title: VulBench: Evaluating LLMs for Binary Vulnerability Detection
authors: Yifan Wu, Shaohua Wen, Haoyu Wang
abstract: While most LLM vulnerability detection work focuses on source code, binary-level detection is important for analyzing closed-source software. We present VulBench, a benchmark for evaluating LLMs' ability to detect vulnerabilities in decompiled binary code.
summary: Introduces VulBench for evaluating LLMs on binary vulnerability detection in decompiled code.
summary_zh: 引入VulBench，评估LLM在反编译代码中检测二进制漏洞的能力。

experiment.key_results: GPT-4 achieves 58% accuracy on decompiled code vs 72% on source

contributions: Proposes VulBench, a novel approach for for evaluating LLMs on binary vulnerability detection in decompiled code.
Leverages GPT-4, Claude, CodeLlama for automated vulnerability analysis and detection.
Evaluates on VulBench benchmark, providing comprehensive empirical evidence.
Demonstrates strong empirical results: GPT-4 achieves 58% accuracy on decompiled code vs 72% on source.
Addresses buffer-overflow, use-after-free vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.
contributions_zh: 提出 VulBench, a novel approach for for evaluating LLMs on binary vulnerability detection in decompiled code.
利用 GPT-4, Claude, CodeLlama for automated vulnerability analysis and detection.
在以下数据集上进行评估： VulBench benchmark, providing comprehensive empirical evidence.
证明了 strong empirical results: GPT-4 achieves 58% accuracy on decompiled code vs 72% on source.
解决 buffer-overflow, use-after-free vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.
abstract_zh: 引入VulBench，评估LLM在反编译代码中检测二进制漏洞的能力。
research_questions: How well can LLMs detect vulnerabilities in decompiled binary code compared to source code?
What is the performance gap between LLM-based vulnerability detection on source code versus decompiled binaries?
How do decompilation artifacts and information loss affect LLM vulnerability reasoning?
research_questions_zh: LLM在反编译二进制代码中检测漏洞的能力与源代码相比如何？
LLM在源代码和反编译二进制文件上的漏洞检测性能差距有多大？
反编译伪影和信息丢失如何影响LLM的漏洞推理？
conclusions: GPT-4 achieves 58% accuracy on decompiled binary code versus 72% on source code, revealing a significant 14-percentage-point gap.
Variable name recovery and type reconstruction from decompilation are the primary factors affecting LLM detection accuracy on binaries.
VulBench provides the first standardized benchmark for binary-level LLM vulnerability detection with 2,000 binary functions across x86-64 and ARM.
conclusions_zh: GPT-4在反编译二进制代码上达到58%准确率，而在源代码上为72%，揭示了14个百分点的显著差距。
反编译中的变量名恢复和类型重建是影响LLM二进制检测准确率的主要因素。
VulBench提供了首个标准化的二进制级LLM漏洞检测基准，包含跨x86-64和ARM架构的2,000个二进制函数。

title: Pre-Training Code Models for Vulnerability Detection: How Far Are We?
authors: Zhen Li, Deqing Chen, Yuwei Cai, Tao Wang
abstract: Pre-trained code models have shown promise for vulnerability detection, but a systematic understanding is lacking. We evaluate 12 pre-trained models across 4 datasets, studying the impact of pre-training objectives, model architecture, and fine-tuning strategies.
summary: Systematic evaluation of 12 pre-trained code models for vulnerability detection across 4 datasets.
summary_zh: 对12个预训练代码模型在4个数据集上进行漏洞检测的系统评估。

experiment.key_results: UniXcoder best overall, but no model consistently outperforms across datasets

contributions: Proposes a novel approach for systematic evaluation of 12 pre-trained code models for vulnerability detection across 4 datasets.
Leverages CodeBERT, GraphCodeBERT, UniXcoder for automated vulnerability analysis and detection.
Evaluates on Big-Vul, Devign, ReVeal benchmarks, providing comprehensive empirical evidence.
Demonstrates strong empirical results: UniXcoder best overall, but no model consistently outperforms across datasets.
Addresses CWE-119, CWE-20, CWE-416 vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.
contributions_zh: 提出 a novel approach for systematic evaluation of 12 pre-trained code models for vulnerability detection across 4 datasets.
利用 CodeBERT, GraphCodeBERT, UniXcoder for automated vulnerability analysis and detection.
在以下数据集上进行评估： Big-Vul, Devign, ReVeal benchmarks, providing comprehensive empirical evidence.
证明了 strong empirical results: UniXcoder best overall, but no model consistently outperforms across datasets.
解决 CWE-119, CWE-20, CWE-416 vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.
abstract_zh: 对12个预训练代码模型在4个数据集上进行漏洞检测的系统评估。
research_questions: How do different pre-training objectives (MLM, RTD, span denoising) affect downstream vulnerability detection performance?
Does any single pre-trained code model consistently outperform others across all vulnerability detection datasets?
What is the role of model architecture (encoder-only vs. encoder-decoder vs. decoder-only) in vulnerability detection?
research_questions_zh: 不同的预训练目标（MLM、RTD、段落去噪）如何影响下游漏洞检测性能？
是否有单个预训练代码模型能在所有漏洞检测数据集上持续优于其他模型？
模型架构（仅编码器、编码器-解码器、仅解码器）在漏洞检测中的作用是什么？
conclusions: UniXcoder achieves the best overall performance, but no single model consistently outperforms across all four datasets (Big-Vul, Devign, ReVeal, DiverseVul).
Encoder-only models generally outperform decoder-only models on vulnerability detection, with encoder-decoder models showing mixed results.
Pre-training on code-specific data significantly improves vulnerability detection, but the choice of pre-training objective matters less than model capacity.
conclusions_zh: UniXcoder总体表现最佳，但没有单一模型能在所有四个数据集（Big-Vul、Devign、ReVeal、DiverseVul）上持续胜出。
仅编码器模型在漏洞检测上通常优于仅解码器模型，编码器-解码器模型表现不一。
在代码特定数据上进行预训练显著提高了漏洞检测性能，但预训练目标的选择不如模型容量重要。

title: LLM-Powered Web Vulnerability Detection
authors: Md Rafiqul Islam, Peng Liu, Bo Luo
abstract: Web application vulnerabilities remain a major security concern. We study how LLMs can detect web vulnerabilities including XSS, SQL injection, CSRF, and SSRF by analyzing web application source code with vulnerability-specific prompting strategies.
summary: Studies LLMs detecting web vulnerabilities (XSS, SQLi, CSRF, SSRF) with vulnerability-specific prompting.
summary_zh: 研究LLM使用漏洞特定提示检测Web漏洞（XSS、SQL注入、CSRF、SSRF）。

experiment.key_results: GPT-4 detects 85% of web vulns with 12% false positive rate

contributions: Proposes a novel approach for studies LLMs detecting web vulnerabilities (XSS, SQLi, CSRF, SSRF) with vulnerability-specific prompting.
Leverages GPT-4, Claude for automated vulnerability analysis and detection.
Evaluates on OWASP Benchmark, Custom dataset benchmarks, providing comprehensive empirical evidence.
Demonstrates strong empirical results: GPT-4 detects 85% of web vulns with 12% false positive rate.
Addresses XSS, SQL-injection, CSRF vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.
contributions_zh: 提出 a novel approach for studies LLMs detecting web vulnerabilities (XSS, SQLi, CSRF, SSRF) with vulnerability-specific prompting.
利用 GPT-4, Claude for automated vulnerability analysis and detection.
在以下数据集上进行评估： OWASP Benchmark, Custom dataset benchmarks, providing comprehensive empirical evidence.
证明了 strong empirical results: GPT-4 detects 85% of web vulns with 12% false positive rate.
解决 XSS, SQL-injection, CSRF vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.
abstract_zh: 研究LLM使用漏洞特定提示检测Web漏洞（XSS、SQL注入、CSRF、SSRF）。
research_questions: How effectively can LLMs detect web-specific vulnerabilities (XSS, SQLi, CSRF, SSRF) compared to specialized web security tools?
Do vulnerability-specific prompting strategies outperform generic vulnerability detection prompts for web application scanning?
What types of web vulnerabilities are LLMs most and least capable of identifying?
research_questions_zh: 与专用Web安全工具相比，LLM检测Web特定漏洞（XSS、SQLi、CSRF、SSRF）的效果如何？
针对特定漏洞的提示策略是否优于通用漏洞检测提示用于Web应用扫描？
LLM最擅长和最不擅长识别哪些类型的Web漏洞？
conclusions: GPT-4 detects 85% of web vulnerabilities with a 12% false positive rate, outperforming SonarQube and matching Semgrep on common patterns.
Vulnerability-specific prompting (e.g., dedicated XSS detection prompt) improves detection accuracy by 18% over generic security analysis prompts.
LLMs excel at detecting injection-based vulnerabilities (XSS, SQLi) but struggle with logic-based flaws like CSRF and access control issues.
conclusions_zh: GPT-4以12%的误报率检测到85%的Web漏洞，优于SonarQube并在常见模式上与Semgrep持平。
针对特定漏洞的提示（如专门的XSS检测提示）比通用安全分析提示将检测准确率提高了18%。
LLM擅长检测基于注入的漏洞（XSS、SQLi），但在逻辑型缺陷如CSRF和访问控制问题上表现不佳。

title: Large Language Models for Code Vulnerability Detection: A Comparative Study
authors: Junhao Li, Jingfeng He, Xiao Yang, Zhiyuan Xu
abstract: We conduct a comparative study of LLMs for code vulnerability detection, comparing zero-shot, few-shot, and fine-tuned approaches. We evaluate both commercial and open-source models, analyzing cost-performance trade-offs for practical deployment.
summary: Comparative study of LLMs for vulnerability detection, analyzing zero-shot, few-shot, and fine-tuned cost-performance trade-offs.
summary_zh: LLM漏洞检测的比较研究，分析零样本、少样本和微调的成本性能权衡。

experiment.key_results: Fine-tuned CodeLlama-13B matches GPT-4 zero-shot at 50x lower cost

contributions: Proposes a novel approach for comparative study of LLMs for vulnerability detection, analyzing zero-shot, few-shot, and fine-tuned cost-performance tr.
Leverages GPT-4, GPT-3.5, Claude for automated vulnerability analysis and detection.
Evaluates on Big-Vul, Devign benchmarks, providing comprehensive empirical evidence.
Demonstrates strong empirical results: Fine-tuned CodeLlama-13B matches GPT-4 zero-shot at 50x lower cost.
Addresses CWE-119, CWE-20 vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.
contributions_zh: 提出 a novel approach for comparative study of LLMs for vulnerability detection, analyzing zero-shot, few-shot, and fine-tuned cost-performance tr.
利用 GPT-4, GPT-3.5, Claude for automated vulnerability analysis and detection.
在以下数据集上进行评估： Big-Vul, Devign benchmarks, providing comprehensive empirical evidence.
证明了 strong empirical results: Fine-tuned CodeLlama-13B matches GPT-4 zero-shot at 50x lower cost.
解决 CWE-119, CWE-20 vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.
abstract_zh: LLM漏洞检测的比较研究，分析零样本、少样本和微调的成本性能权衡。
research_questions: What are the cost-performance trade-offs between zero-shot, few-shot, and fine-tuned LLM approaches for vulnerability detection?
Can fine-tuned open-source models match or exceed the vulnerability detection performance of commercial LLMs?
How should organizations choose between commercial API-based and locally deployed LLMs for vulnerability detection?
research_questions_zh: 零样本、少样本和微调LLM方法在漏洞检测中的成本-性能权衡如何？
微调的开源模型能否达到或超过商业LLM的漏洞检测性能？
组织应如何在基于商业API和本地部署的LLM之间选择用于漏洞检测？
conclusions: Fine-tuned CodeLlama-13B matches GPT-4's zero-shot vulnerability detection performance at approximately 50x lower inference cost.
Few-shot prompting provides only marginal improvement (3-5%) over zero-shot for vulnerability detection, making it less cost-effective than fine-tuning.
For organizations processing over 10,000 functions monthly, locally deployed fine-tuned models become more economical than commercial API usage.
conclusions_zh: 微调的CodeLlama-13B在漏洞检测性能上与GPT-4零样本方法持平，推理成本约低50倍。
少样本提示相比零样本在漏洞检测上仅有微小改进（3-5%），性价比不如微调。
对于每月处理超过10,000个函数的组织，本地部署的微调模型比商业API使用更为经济。

title: GPT-4 as a Vulnerability Scanner: A Comprehensive Analysis
authors: Guorui Sun, Shaoying Li, Kai Xu
abstract: We present a comprehensive analysis of GPT-4's capabilities as a vulnerability scanner, comparing it against commercial SAST tools on real-world codebases. We evaluate detection accuracy, false positive rates, and the ability to explain detected vulnerabilities.
summary: Comprehensive analysis of GPT-4 as vulnerability scanner compared against commercial SAST tools on real-world code.
summary_zh: 全面分析GPT-4作为漏洞扫描器的能力，与商业SAST工具在真实代码上对比。

experiment.key_results: GPT-4 matches commercial tools on known patterns, excels at explaining vulns

contributions: Proposes a novel approach for comprehensive analysis of GPT-4 as vulnerability scanner compared against commercial SAST tools on real-world code.
Leverages GPT-4 for automated vulnerability analysis and detection.
Evaluates on NIST SARD, Real-world CVEs benchmarks, providing comprehensive empirical evidence.
Demonstrates strong empirical results: GPT-4 matches commercial tools on known patterns, excels at explaining vulns.
Addresses CWE-79, CWE-89, CWE-119 vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.
contributions_zh: 提出 a novel approach for comprehensive analysis of GPT-4 as vulnerability scanner compared against commercial SAST tools on real-world code.
利用 GPT-4 for automated vulnerability analysis and detection.
在以下数据集上进行评估： NIST SARD, Real-world CVEs benchmarks, providing comprehensive empirical evidence.
证明了 strong empirical results: GPT-4 matches commercial tools on known patterns, excels at explaining vulns.
解决 CWE-79, CWE-89, CWE-119 vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.
abstract_zh: 全面分析GPT-4作为漏洞扫描器的能力，与商业SAST工具在真实代码上对比。
research_questions: How does GPT-4 compare to commercial SAST tools (Checkmarx, Fortify, CodeQL, Semgrep) in vulnerability detection accuracy and false positive rate?
Can GPT-4 provide meaningful vulnerability explanations that aid developer remediation beyond what SAST tools offer?
What are the practical limitations of using GPT-4 as a replacement for dedicated SAST tools in enterprise settings?
research_questions_zh: GPT-4与商业SAST工具（Checkmarx、Fortify、CodeQL、Semgrep）在漏洞检测准确率和误报率方面相比如何？
GPT-4能否提供超越SAST工具的有意义的漏洞解释来辅助开发者修复？
在企业环境中使用GPT-4替代专用SAST工具的实际局限性是什么？
conclusions: GPT-4 matches commercial SAST tools on well-known vulnerability patterns but has lower recall on complex, multi-file vulnerability chains.
GPT-4 significantly excels at explaining detected vulnerabilities, providing actionable remediation guidance that SAST tools lack.
SAST tools remain more reliable for systematic, repeatable scanning at scale, while GPT-4 serves best as a complementary explanation and triage layer.
conclusions_zh: GPT-4在已知漏洞模式上与商业SAST工具持平，但在复杂的多文件漏洞链上召回率较低。
GPT-4在解释检测到的漏洞方面表现突出，能提供SAST工具所缺乏的可操作修复指导。
SAST工具在大规模系统化、可重复扫描方面仍更可靠，而GPT-4最适合作为补充性的解释和分类层。

title: Combining CodeQL with LLMs for Enhanced Vulnerability Detection
authors: Xiao Cheng, Guang Chen, Jianling Zhou
abstract: Static analysis tools like CodeQL have high precision but limited recall. We propose combining CodeQL's formal analysis with LLM-based reasoning to detect vulnerabilities that neither approach finds alone, using CodeQL results as context for LLM analysis.
summary: Combines CodeQL static analysis with LLM reasoning for enhanced vulnerability detection beyond either approach alone.
summary_zh: 结合CodeQL静态分析与LLM推理，增强超越任一方法的漏洞检测。

experiment.key_results: Combined approach finds 35% more vulnerabilities than either alone

contributions: Proposes a novel approach for combines CodeQL static analysis with LLM reasoning for enhanced vulnerability detection beyond either approach alone.
Leverages GPT-4 for automated vulnerability analysis and detection.
Evaluates on CodeQL benchmark, Big-Vul benchmarks, providing comprehensive empirical evidence.
Demonstrates strong empirical results: Combined approach finds 35% more vulnerabilities than either alone.
Addresses SQL-injection, XSS, path-traversal vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.
contributions_zh: 提出 a novel approach for combines CodeQL static analysis with LLM reasoning for enhanced vulnerability detection beyond either approach alone.
利用 GPT-4 for automated vulnerability analysis and detection.
在以下数据集上进行评估： CodeQL benchmark, Big-Vul benchmarks, providing comprehensive empirical evidence.
证明了 strong empirical results: Combined approach finds 35% more vulnerabilities than either alone.
解决 SQL-injection, XSS, path-traversal vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.
abstract_zh: 结合CodeQL静态分析与LLM推理，增强超越任一方法的漏洞检测。
research_questions: Can combining CodeQL's formal static analysis with LLM-based semantic reasoning improve vulnerability detection beyond either approach alone?
How should CodeQL analysis results be structured as context for LLM-based vulnerability reasoning?
What categories of vulnerabilities benefit most from the hybrid CodeQL-LLM detection approach?
research_questions_zh: 将CodeQL的形式化静态分析与基于LLM的语义推理结合能否提高漏洞检测超越任一单独方法？
CodeQL分析结果应如何构建为LLM漏洞推理的上下文？
哪些类别的漏洞最能从混合CodeQL-LLM检测方法中受益？
conclusions: The combined CodeQL-LLM approach discovers 35% more vulnerabilities than either CodeQL or GPT-4 alone.
Using CodeQL's data-flow results as structured context for LLM analysis is most effective for taint-style vulnerabilities like SQL injection and XSS.
The LLM component primarily contributes by identifying logic vulnerabilities and novel patterns that CodeQL's rule-based system cannot capture.
conclusions_zh: CodeQL-LLM组合方法发现的漏洞比单独使用CodeQL或GPT-4多35%。
将CodeQL的数据流结果作为LLM分析的结构化上下文对污点类漏洞（如SQL注入和XSS）最为有效。
LLM组件主要通过识别CodeQL基于规则的系统无法捕获的逻辑漏洞和新型模式来贡献价值。

title: SecureFalcon: The Next Cyber Reasoning System for Vulnerability Detection
authors: Mohamed Amine Ferrag, Othmane Friha, Djallel Hamouda, Leandros Maglaras
abstract: We present SecureFalcon, a cybersecurity-focused LLM fine-tuned from Falcon for vulnerability detection. SecureFalcon is trained on a curated cybersecurity corpus including CVE descriptions, security advisories, and vulnerable code patterns.
summary: Introduces SecureFalcon, a cybersecurity-focused Falcon model fine-tuned for vulnerability detection on curated security data.
summary_zh: 介绍SecureFalcon，基于Falcon微调的网络安全LLM，使用精选安全数据进行漏洞检测。

experiment.key_results: Outperforms base Falcon by 25% on vulnerability detection F1

contributions: Proposes SecureFalcon, a novel framework for cybersecurity-focused Falcon model fine-tuned for vulnerability detection on curated security data.
Leverages Falcon for automated vulnerability analysis and detection.
Evaluates on CVE corpus, Big-Vul benchmarks, providing comprehensive empirical evidence.
Demonstrates strong empirical results: Outperforms base Falcon by 25% on vulnerability detection F1.
Addresses CWE-119, CWE-20, CWE-416 vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.
contributions_zh: 提出 SecureFalcon, a novel framework for cybersecurity-focused Falcon model fine-tuned for vulnerability detection on curated security data.
利用 Falcon for automated vulnerability analysis and detection.
在以下数据集上进行评估： CVE corpus, Big-Vul benchmarks, providing comprehensive empirical evidence.
证明了 strong empirical results: Outperforms base Falcon by 25% on vulnerability detection F1.
解决 CWE-119, CWE-20, CWE-416 vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.
abstract_zh: 介绍SecureFalcon，基于Falcon微调的网络安全LLM，使用精选安全数据进行漏洞检测。
research_questions: Does fine-tuning Falcon on a curated cybersecurity corpus improve vulnerability detection compared to the base model?
How does a domain-specific security-focused LLM compare to general-purpose models on vulnerability detection tasks?
What types of cybersecurity training data are most beneficial for improving vulnerability detection in a fine-tuned model?
research_questions_zh: 在策划的网络安全语料库上微调Falcon是否比基础模型提高了漏洞检测性能？
领域特定的安全聚焦LLM与通用模型在漏洞检测任务上相比如何？
