contributions_zh: 提出 NeuronFuzz, a novel approach for neural networks to predict coverage-increasing mutations, learning from execution traces and feedback.
利用 Custom transformer for intelligent test case generation and mutation strategies.
在以下数据集上进行评估： FuzzBench benchmark, providing comprehensive empirical evidence.
证明了 strong empirical results: 15% more branches covered in 24h vs AFL++.
解决 crash, memory-safety vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.
abstract_zh: 使用神经网络预测增加覆盖率的变异，从执行轨迹和反馈中学习。
research_questions: Can neural networks trained on execution traces and coverage feedback learn to predict which mutations will increase code coverage?
How effective is learned mutation selection compared to static mutation scheduling strategies in greybox fuzzing?
What execution trace features are most predictive of coverage-increasing mutations?
research_questions_zh: 基于执行轨迹和覆盖反馈训练的神经网络能否学习预测哪些变异会增加代码覆盖率？
在灰盒模糊测试中，学习到的变异选择与静态变异调度策略相比效果如何？
哪些执行轨迹特征对预测覆盖率增加的变异最具指示性？
conclusions: NeuronFuzz covers 15% more branches than AFL++ within 24 hours, validating the effectiveness of neural network-guided mutation selection.
NeuronFuzz outperforms MOpt and EcoFuzz in branch coverage and bug finding across 15 FuzzBench targets.
Edge transition frequency and basic block hotness in execution paths are the most important features for predicting effective mutation locations and strategies.
conclusions_zh: NeuronFuzz在24小时内比AFL++多覆盖15%的分支，验证了神经网络引导变异选择的有效性。
在FuzzBench的15个基准目标上，NeuronFuzz在分支覆盖和缺陷发现方面优于MOpt和EcoFuzz。
执行路径的边缘转换频率和基本块热度是预测有效变异位置和策略的最重要特征。

title: LLM-Enhanced Smart Fuzzing for Network Protocols
authors: Tao Liu, Yue Wang, Jun Chen
abstract: Network protocol fuzzing requires valid message sequences. We use LLMs to understand protocol state machines from RFCs and generate smart fuzzing strategies that exercise protocol edge cases.
summary: Uses LLMs to understand protocol state machines from RFCs for smart protocol fuzzing.
summary_zh: 利用LLM从RFC理解协议状态机，实现智能协议模糊测试。

experiment.key_results: 35% more state transitions explored than AFLNet

contributions: Proposes a novel approach for LLMs to understand protocol state machines from RFCs for smart protocol fuzzing.
Leverages GPT-4 for intelligent test case generation and mutation strategies.
Evaluates on ProFuzzBench benchmark, providing comprehensive empirical evidence.
Demonstrates strong empirical results: 35% more state transitions explored than AFLNet.
Addresses crash, memory-corruption vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.
contributions_zh: 提出 a novel approach for LLMs to understand protocol state machines from RFCs for smart protocol fuzzing.
利用 GPT-4 for intelligent test case generation and mutation strategies.
在以下数据集上进行评估： ProFuzzBench benchmark, providing comprehensive empirical evidence.
证明了 strong empirical results: 35% more state transitions explored than AFLNet.
解决 crash, memory-corruption vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.
abstract_zh: 利用LLM从RFC理解协议状态机，实现智能协议模糊测试。
research_questions: Can LLMs understand network protocol state machines from RFC documents to generate effective protocol fuzzing strategies?
How does LLM-guided protocol fuzzing compare with existing protocol fuzzers in terms of state transition coverage?
What types of protocol edge cases and vulnerabilities can LLM-guided fuzzing discover that traditional protocol fuzzers miss?
research_questions_zh: LLM能否从RFC文档中理解网络协议状态机以生成有效的协议模糊测试策略？
LLM引导的协议模糊测试在状态转换覆盖方面与现有协议模糊器相比如何？
LLM引导的模糊测试能发现哪些传统协议模糊器遗漏的协议边界条件和漏洞？
conclusions: LLM-guided protocol fuzzing explores 35% more state transitions than AFLNet, covering more protocol edge-case scenarios.
The approach outperforms AFLNet, Boofuzz, and ChatAFL across 8 protocol targets in ProFuzzBench.
LLM semantic understanding of RFC documents enables generating test cases targeting deep protocol state machine logic, uncovering vulnerabilities that traditional fuzzers cannot reach.
conclusions_zh: LLM引导的协议模糊测试比AFLNet多探索35%的状态转换，覆盖了更多的协议边界场景。
在ProFuzzBench的8个协议目标上，该方法优于AFLNet、Boofuzz和ChatAFL等基线方法。
LLM对RFC文档的语义理解使其能够生成针对协议状态机深层逻辑的测试用例，发现传统模糊器难以触及的漏洞。

title: APIFuzz: LLM-Guided API Sequence Generation for Library Fuzzing
authors: Zhengjie Wang, Shuai Li, Chao Zhang
abstract: Library fuzzing requires valid API call sequences. APIFuzz uses LLMs to understand library API semantics and generate valid, diverse API call sequences that exercise complex library functionality.
summary: Uses LLMs to generate valid API call sequences for library fuzzing based on semantic understanding.
summary_zh: 利用LLM基于语义理解生成有效的API调用序列，用于库模糊测试。

experiment.key_results: 42% more API coverage than FUDGE

contributions: Proposes APIFuzz, a novel approach for LLMs to generate valid API call sequences for library fuzzing based on semantic understanding.
Leverages GPT-4, CodeLlama for intelligent test case generation and mutation strategies.
Evaluates on 10 libraries benchmark, providing comprehensive empirical evidence.
Demonstrates strong empirical results: 42% more API coverage than FUDGE.
Addresses crash, memory-safety vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.
contributions_zh: 提出 APIFuzz, a novel approach for LLMs to generate valid API call sequences for library fuzzing based on semantic understanding.
利用 GPT-4, CodeLlama for intelligent test case generation and mutation strategies.
在以下数据集上进行评估： 10 libraries benchmark, providing comprehensive empirical evidence.
证明了 strong empirical results: 42% more API coverage than FUDGE.
解决 crash, memory-safety vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.
abstract_zh: 利用LLM基于语义理解生成有效的API调用序列，用于库模糊测试。
research_questions: Can LLMs understand library API semantics well enough to generate valid and diverse API call sequences for fuzzing?
How does APIFuzz's LLM-generated API sequence coverage compare with existing library fuzzing approaches like FUDGE and Hopper?
What role does API semantic understanding play in discovering bugs through library fuzzing?
research_questions_zh: LLM能否充分理解库API语义，以生成有效且多样的API调用序列用于模糊测试？
APIFuzz的LLM生成API序列覆盖率与FUDGE和Hopper等现有库模糊测试方法相比如何？
API语义理解在通过库模糊测试发现缺陷中发挥什么作用？
conclusions: APIFuzz achieves 42% more API coverage than FUDGE across 10 C/C++ libraries, demonstrating that LLM semantic understanding significantly improves library fuzzing.
LLM-generated API call sequences maintain call validity while covering more complex library functionality interaction scenarios.
APIFuzz achieves the best results in both bug finding and API coverage compared to FUDGE, Hopper, and FuzzGen.
conclusions_zh: APIFuzz在10个C/C++库上比FUDGE多达到42%的API覆盖率，证明LLM的语义理解能力可显著提升库模糊测试效果。
LLM生成的API调用序列在保持调用合法性的同时，覆盖了更复杂的库功能交互场景。
与FUDGE、Hopper和FuzzGen相比，APIFuzz在缺陷发现和API覆盖方面均取得了最优结果。

title: Adaptive LLM-Guided Fuzzing with Dynamic Strategy Selection
authors: Yong Chen, Lei Wu, Marcel Bohme
abstract: Different fuzzing targets benefit from different strategies. We propose adaptive fuzzing that uses LLMs to dynamically select mutation strategies based on observed coverage patterns, program structure, and historical effectiveness.
summary: Adaptive fuzzing using LLMs to dynamically select mutation strategies based on coverage and program structure.
summary_zh: 自适应模糊测试，利用LLM根据覆盖率和程序结构动态选择变异策略。

experiment.key_results: 18% faster bug finding than static strategy AFL++

contributions: Proposes a novel approach for adaptive fuzzing using LLMs to dynamically select mutation strategies based on coverage and program structure.
Leverages GPT-3.5 for intelligent test case generation and mutation strategies.
Evaluates on Magma, FuzzBench benchmarks, providing comprehensive empirical evidence.
Demonstrates strong empirical results: 18% faster bug finding than static strategy AFL++.
Addresses crash, memory-safety vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.
contributions_zh: 提出 a novel approach for adaptive fuzzing using LLMs to dynamically select mutation strategies based on coverage and program structure.
利用 GPT-3.5 for intelligent test case generation and mutation strategies.
在以下数据集上进行评估： Magma, FuzzBench benchmarks, providing comprehensive empirical evidence.
证明了 strong empirical results: 18% faster bug finding than static strategy AFL++.
解决 crash, memory-safety vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.
abstract_zh: 自适应模糊测试，利用LLM根据覆盖率和程序结构动态选择变异策略。
research_questions: Can LLMs dynamically select optimal mutation strategies based on observed coverage patterns and program structure?
How does adaptive strategy selection compare with static mutation scheduling in terms of bug finding speed?
What coverage pattern features are most informative for guiding mutation strategy selection?
research_questions_zh: LLM能否根据观察到的覆盖模式和程序结构动态选择最优变异策略？
自适应策略选择与静态变异调度在缺陷发现速度方面相比如何？
哪些覆盖模式特征对引导变异策略选择最具参考价值？
conclusions: Adaptive strategy selection improves bug finding speed by 18% over static-strategy AFL++, with improvements observed across 20 Magma and FuzzBench targets.
LLMs can effectively identify the most suitable mutation strategy based on program structure and coverage feedback, avoiding limitations of fixed scheduling.
The approach outperforms AFL++, MOpt, and EcoFuzz baselines, with advantages most pronounced for complex program structures and deep path exploration.
conclusions_zh: 自适应策略选择使缺陷发现速度比使用静态策略的AFL++提升18%，在Magma和FuzzBench的20个目标上均有改善。
LLM能够根据程序结构和覆盖反馈有效识别当前最适合的变异策略，避免了固定调度的局限性。
该方法优于AFL++、MOpt和EcoFuzz等基线方法，尤其在复杂程序结构和深层路径探索场景中优势更为明显。

title: Can LLMs Write Secure Code? A Large-Scale Experiment with ChatGPT
authors: Shafiq Ullah, Yu Han, Yang Liu
abstract: We study whether LLM-generated code is secure by analyzing 4000 code samples from ChatGPT across 10 languages. We find that 25% of generated code contains security vulnerabilities, with injection flaws being most common.
summary: Studies security of LLM-generated code across 10 languages, finding 25% contains vulnerabilities.
summary_zh: 研究10种语言中LLM生成代码的安全性，发现25%包含漏洞。

experiment.key_results: 25% of LLM code has vulnerabilities, vs 15% for human code

contributions: Proposes a novel approach for studies security of LLM-generated code across 10 languages, finding 25% contains vulnerabilities.
Leverages ChatGPT, GPT-4, Copilot for automated vulnerability analysis and detection.
Evaluates on 4000 generated samples benchmark, providing comprehensive empirical evidence.
Demonstrates strong empirical results: 25% of LLM code has vulnerabilities, vs 15% for human code.
Addresses injection, XSS, path-traversal vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.
contributions_zh: 提出 a novel approach for studies security of LLM-generated code across 10 languages, finding 25% contains vulnerabilities.
利用 ChatGPT, GPT-4, Copilot for automated vulnerability analysis and detection.
在以下数据集上进行评估： 4000 generated samples benchmark, providing comprehensive empirical evidence.
证明了 strong empirical results: 25% of LLM code has vulnerabilities, vs 15% for human code.
解决 injection, XSS, path-traversal vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.
abstract_zh: 研究10种语言中LLM生成代码的安全性，发现25%包含漏洞。
research_questions: What is the prevalence of security vulnerabilities in LLM-generated code compared to human-written code?
Which types of security vulnerabilities are most common in LLM-generated code across different programming languages?
How do different LLMs (ChatGPT, GPT-4, Copilot) compare in generating secure code?
research_questions_zh: LLM生成代码中安全漏洞的发生率与人类编写代码相比如何？
在不同编程语言中，LLM生成代码中最常见的安全漏洞类型是什么？
不同LLM（ChatGPT、GPT-4、Copilot）在生成安全代码方面的表现有何差异？
conclusions: Analysis of 4,000 code samples reveals that 25% of LLM-generated code contains security vulnerabilities, significantly higher than the 15% rate in human-written code.
Injection flaws are the most common vulnerability type in LLM-generated code, followed by XSS and path traversal vulnerabilities.
GPT-4 generates more secure code than ChatGPT and Copilot, but security issues exist across all 10 languages tested, with Python and JavaScript showing the highest vulnerability rates.
conclusions_zh: 分析4000个代码样本发现，25%的LLM生成代码包含安全漏洞，显著高于人类编写代码的15%漏洞率。
注入类缺陷是LLM生成代码中最常见的漏洞类型，其次是XSS和路径遍历漏洞。
GPT-4生成的代码安全性优于ChatGPT和Copilot，但在10种语言中均存在安全问题，Python和JavaScript漏洞率最高。

title: SecurityEval: A Comprehensive Benchmark for Evaluating LLM Code Security
authors: Tony Zhuo, Yifan Huang, Zheng Chen
abstract: We present SecurityEval, a benchmark specifically designed to evaluate the security of LLM-generated code. SecurityEval contains 500 prompts that are likely to produce vulnerable code, covering 30 CWE categories across 5 languages.
summary: Introduces SecurityEval benchmark with 500 prompts evaluating LLM code security across 30 CWEs.
summary_zh: 引入SecurityEval基准，包含500个提示评估LLM代码安全性，涵盖30种CWE。

experiment.key_results: GPT-4 produces least vulnerable code but still 18% vulnerability rate

contributions: Proposes SecurityEval, a novel approach for benchmark with 500 prompts evaluating LLM code security across 30 CWEs.
Leverages GPT-4, Claude, CodeLlama for automated vulnerability analysis and detection.
Evaluates on SecurityEval benchmark, providing comprehensive empirical evidence.
Demonstrates strong empirical results: GPT-4 produces least vulnerable code but still 18% vulnerability rate.
Addresses 30 CWE categories vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.
contributions_zh: 提出 SecurityEval, a novel approach for benchmark with 500 prompts evaluating LLM code security across 30 CWEs.
利用 GPT-4, Claude, CodeLlama for automated vulnerability analysis and detection.
在以下数据集上进行评估： SecurityEval benchmark, providing comprehensive empirical evidence.
证明了 strong empirical results: GPT-4 produces least vulnerable code but still 18% vulnerability rate.
解决 30 CWE categories vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.
abstract_zh: 引入SecurityEval基准，包含500个提示评估LLM代码安全性，涵盖30种CWE。
research_questions: How can a benchmark be designed to systematically evaluate the security of LLM-generated code across multiple CWE categories?
Which LLMs produce the most secure code when given prompts that are likely to elicit vulnerable outputs?
What CWE categories are LLMs most and least likely to produce vulnerable code for?
research_questions_zh: 如何设计基准测试以系统性地评估LLM生成代码在多个CWE类别上的安全性？
面对可能引发漏洞输出的提示时，哪些LLM生成的代码最安全？
LLM在哪些CWE类别上最容易和最不容易生成有漏洞的代码？
conclusions: GPT-4 produces the least vulnerable code among all tested LLMs, but still has an 18% vulnerability rate.
SecurityEval's 500 prompts covering 30 CWE categories across 5 languages provide a standardized benchmark for LLM code security evaluation.
LLMs perform worst on injection-related (CWE-89, CWE-78) and cryptographic misuse (CWE-327) vulnerabilities, and relatively better on type safety-related CWEs.
conclusions_zh: GPT-4在所有受测LLM中生成的代码漏洞率最低，但仍有18%的代码包含安全漏洞。
SecurityEval的500个提示覆盖30个CWE类别和5种语言，为LLM代码安全评估提供了标准化基准。
LLM在注入类（CWE-89、CWE-78）和加密误用类（CWE-327）漏洞上表现最差，在类型安全相关CWE上表现相对较好。

title: Do Users Write More Insecure Code with AI Assistants?
authors: Neil Perry, Megha Srivastava, Deepak Kumar, Dan Boneh
abstract: We conduct a controlled user study to evaluate whether AI code assistants lead to less secure code. Participants with AI assistance produced significantly more security vulnerabilities than those without, while believing their code was more secure.
summary: User study showing AI code assistants lead to more security vulnerabilities while users believe code is more secure.
summary_zh: 用户研究表明AI代码助手导致更多安全漏洞，而用户认为代码更安全。

experiment.key_results: AI-assisted participants wrote 10% more vulnerable code
experiment.real_world_impact: Influenced AI assistant safety discussions

contributions: Introduces a novel approach for user study showing AI code assistants lead to more security vulnerabilities while users believe code is more secure.
Leverages Codex for automated vulnerability analysis and detection.
Evaluates on 5 programming tasks benchmark, providing comprehensive empirical evidence.
Demonstrates strong empirical results: AI-assisted participants wrote 10% more vulnerable code.
Addresses SQL-injection, path-traversal, XSS vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.
contributions_zh: 引入 a novel approach for user study showing AI code assistants lead to more security vulnerabilities while users believe code is more secure.
利用 Codex for automated vulnerability analysis and detection.
在以下数据集上进行评估： 5 programming tasks benchmark, providing comprehensive empirical evidence.
证明了 strong empirical results: AI-assisted participants wrote 10% more vulnerable code.
解决 SQL-injection, path-traversal, XSS vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.
abstract_zh: 用户研究表明AI代码助手导致更多安全漏洞，而用户认为代码更安全。
research_questions: Do developers using AI code assistants produce more or less secure code compared to those coding without AI assistance?
How does AI code assistance affect developers' confidence in the security of their code?
What types of security vulnerabilities are most commonly introduced when developers use AI code assistants?
research_questions_zh: 使用AI代码助手的开发者与不使用AI辅助编码的开发者相比，生成的代码安全性更高还是更低？
AI代码辅助如何影响开发者对其代码安全性的信心？
开发者使用AI代码助手时最常引入哪些类型的安全漏洞？
conclusions: In a controlled study with 47 participants, AI-assisted participants produced 10% more security vulnerabilities while believing their code was more secure.
The AI-assisted group had higher incidence rates across SQL injection, path traversal, and XSS vulnerability categories compared to the control group.
AI code assistants create a false sense of security, causing developers to lower their vigilance for security review, with important implications for AI tool design.
conclusions_zh: 在47名参与者的对照实验中，使用AI辅助的参与者编写的代码多出10%的安全漏洞，同时却认为自己的代码更安全。
AI辅助组在SQL注入、路径遍历和XSS三类漏洞上的发生率均高于对照组。
AI代码助手创造了一种虚假的安全感，使开发者降低了对安全审查的警觉性，这一发现对AI辅助工具的设计具有重要启示。

title: Large Language Models for Software Vulnerability Detection: A Comprehensive Benchmark Study
authors: Xin He, Jiayuan Bai, Lin Chen
abstract: We benchmark 10 LLMs for vulnerability detection across multiple datasets and evaluate various prompting techniques. Our study reveals that few-shot prompting with examples significantly improves detection performance.
summary: Benchmarks 10 LLMs for vulnerability detection with various prompting techniques, finding few-shot best.
summary_zh: 对10个LLM在多种提示技术下进行漏洞检测基准测试，发现少样本最佳。

experiment.key_results: Few-shot improves F1 by 15% over zero-shot across all models

contributions: Introduces a novel approach for benchmarks 10 LLMs for vulnerability detection with various prompting techniques, finding few-shot best.
Leverages GPT-4, GPT-3.5, Claude for automated vulnerability analysis and detection.
Evaluates on Big-Vul, Devign, SARD benchmarks, providing comprehensive empirical evidence.
Demonstrates strong empirical results: Few-shot improves F1 by 15% over zero-shot across all models.
Addresses CWE-119, CWE-20, CWE-416 vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.
contributions_zh: 引入 a novel approach for benchmarks 10 LLMs for vulnerability detection with various prompting techniques, finding few-shot best.
利用 GPT-4, GPT-3.5, Claude for automated vulnerability analysis and detection.
在以下数据集上进行评估： Big-Vul, Devign, SARD benchmarks, providing comprehensive empirical evidence.
证明了 strong empirical results: Few-shot improves F1 by 15% over zero-shot across all models.
解决 CWE-119, CWE-20, CWE-416 vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.
abstract_zh: 对10个LLM在多种提示技术下进行漏洞检测基准测试，发现少样本最佳。
research_questions: How do 10 different LLMs compare in their ability to detect software vulnerabilities across multiple datasets?
How much does few-shot prompting improve vulnerability detection performance compared to zero-shot prompting?
Which LLM families and model sizes achieve the best trade-off between detection accuracy and computational cost?
research_questions_zh: 10种不同LLM在多个数据集上的漏洞检测能力如何比较？
少样本提示相比零样本提示能将漏洞检测性能提升多少？
哪些LLM系列和模型规模在检测精度和计算成本之间实现了最佳权衡？
conclusions: Few-shot prompting improves F1 score by 15% on average across all models, making it the most effective prompting strategy for LLM vulnerability detection.
GPT-4 achieves the best overall performance across Big-Vul, Devign, and SARD datasets, but open-source models with few-shot prompting can approach its performance.
Across 20,000 function samples, LLMs detect CWE-119 (buffer overflow) vulnerabilities most effectively and CWE-20 (improper input validation) least effectively.
conclusions_zh: 少样本提示在所有模型上平均将F1值提升15%，是提升LLM漏洞检测性能最有效的提示策略。
GPT-4在Big-Vul、Devign和SARD三个数据集上的综合表现最优，但开源模型通过少样本提示可接近其性能。
在20000个函数样本的评估中，LLM在CWE-119（缓冲区溢出）类漏洞上检测效果最好，在CWE-20（输入验证不当）上表现最差。

title: VulGuard: Real-Time LLM-Based Vulnerability Detection in CI/CD
authors: Chen Li, Yue Wang, Tao Zhang
abstract: Integrating vulnerability detection into CI/CD requires balancing speed and accuracy. VulGuard uses lightweight LLM inference with incremental analysis to detect vulnerabilities in code changes in real-time, focusing on modified code rather than full codebase.
summary: Real-time LLM vulnerability detection in CI/CD using incremental analysis of code changes.
summary_zh: 使用增量分析代码变更在CI/CD中进行实时LLM漏洞检测。

experiment.key_results: <30s per PR review, detects 65% of vulnerabilities in changed code

contributions: Proposes VulGuard, a novel approach for real-time LLM vulnerability detection in CI/CD using incremental analysis of code changes.
Leverages GPT-3.5, CodeLlama for automated vulnerability analysis and detection.
Evaluates on Real GitHub PRs benchmark, providing comprehensive empirical evidence.
Demonstrates strong empirical results: <30s per PR review, detects 65% of vulnerabilities in changed code.
Addresses injection, auth, crypto vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.
contributions_zh: 提出 VulGuard, a novel approach for real-time LLM vulnerability detection in CI/CD using incremental analysis of code changes.
利用 GPT-3.5, CodeLlama for automated vulnerability analysis and detection.
在以下数据集上进行评估： Real GitHub PRs benchmark, providing comprehensive empirical evidence.
证明了 strong empirical results: <30s per PR review, detects 65% of vulnerabilities in changed code.
解决 injection, auth, crypto vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.
abstract_zh: 使用增量分析代码变更在CI/CD中进行实时LLM漏洞检测。
research_questions: How can LLM-based vulnerability detection be integrated into CI/CD pipelines with acceptable latency?
Does focusing analysis on code changes (incremental analysis) rather than full codebase scanning maintain sufficient detection accuracy?
How does VulGuard's real-time performance compare with full-scan approaches like CodeQL and Semgrep?
research_questions_zh: 如何将基于LLM的漏洞检测以可接受的延迟集成到CI/CD流水线中？
将分析聚焦于代码变更（增量分析）而非全代码库扫描是否能保持足够的检测精度？
VulGuard的实时性能与CodeQL和Semgrep等全扫描方法相比如何？
conclusions: VulGuard achieves under 30 seconds per PR review while detecting 65% of security vulnerabilities in changed code.
The incremental analysis strategy reduces latency by an order of magnitude while maintaining comparable detection rates for injection, authentication, and cryptographic vulnerabilities.
Evaluation on 2,000 GitHub Pull Requests shows VulGuard significantly outperforms CodeQL and Semgrep in speed, though detection capability for some cross-file vulnerabilities is limited.
conclusions_zh: VulGuard实现了每个PR审查不到30秒的响应时间，同时检测到变更代码中65%的安全漏洞。
增量分析策略使延迟降低了一个数量级，同时对注入、认证和加密类漏洞保持了与全扫描相当的检测率。
在2000个GitHub Pull Request上的评估表明，VulGuard在速度上大幅优于CodeQL和Semgrep，但在某些跨文件漏洞上检测能力不足。

title: Repository-Level Vulnerability Detection with LLMs
authors: Ziyang Wang, Hao Li, Dawn Song
abstract: Most LLM vulnerability detection focuses on function-level analysis. We extend to repository-level detection, where the LLM analyzes cross-file data flows, configuration files, and architectural patterns to find vulnerabilities that span multiple files.
summary: Extends LLM vulnerability detection to repository-level, analyzing cross-file data flows and architectural patterns.
summary_zh: 将LLM漏洞检测扩展到仓库级别，分析跨文件数据流和架构模式。

experiment.key_results: Finds 40% more vulnerabilities than function-level analysis

contributions: Proposes a novel approach for extends LLM vulnerability detection to repository-level, analyzing cross-file data flows and architectural patterns.
Leverages GPT-4, Claude for automated vulnerability analysis and detection.
Evaluates on 20 OSS repositories benchmark, providing comprehensive empirical evidence.
Demonstrates strong empirical results: Finds 40% more vulnerabilities than function-level analysis.
Addresses cross-file-injection, config-vuln, auth-bypass vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.
contributions_zh: 提出 a novel approach for extends LLM vulnerability detection to repository-level, analyzing cross-file data flows and architectural patterns.
利用 GPT-4, Claude for automated vulnerability analysis and detection.
在以下数据集上进行评估： 20 OSS repositories benchmark, providing comprehensive empirical evidence.
证明了 strong empirical results: Finds 40% more vulnerabilities than function-level analysis.
解决 cross-file-injection, config-vuln, auth-bypass vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.
abstract_zh: 将LLM漏洞检测扩展到仓库级别，分析跨文件数据流和架构模式。
research_questions: Can LLMs perform repository-level vulnerability detection by analyzing cross-file data flows and architectural patterns?
How much does repository-level analysis improve vulnerability detection compared to function-level analysis?
What types of vulnerabilities are uniquely identifiable through cross-file analysis that function-level methods miss?
research_questions_zh: LLM能否通过分析跨文件数据流和架构模式进行仓库级别的漏洞检测？
仓库级别分析相比函数级别分析在漏洞检测方面能提升多少？
哪些类型的漏洞只能通过跨文件分析才能识别，而函数级方法无法发现？
conclusions: Repository-level analysis finds 40% more vulnerabilities than function-level analysis, with particular strength in cross-file injection, configuration vulnerabilities, and authentication bypass.
Evaluation on 20 open-source repositories shows cross-file data flow analysis detects vulnerabilities missed by function-level GPT-4 and CodeQL.
GPT-4 and Claude excel at understanding cross-file context, but context window limitations become the primary bottleneck as repository size increases.
conclusions_zh: 仓库级别分析比函数级别分析多发现40%的漏洞，尤其在跨文件注入、配置漏洞和认证绕过方面优势显著。
在20个开源代码仓库上的评估表明，跨文件数据流分析可检测到函数级GPT-4和CodeQL遗漏的漏洞。
GPT-4和Claude在理解跨文件上下文方面表现出色，但仓库规模增大时上下文窗口限制成为主要瓶颈。

title: Not Just Detection: LLMs for Vulnerability Explanation and Remediation
authors: Chengcheng Sun, Zhengzi Xu, Chao Zhang
abstract: Beyond detection, developers need vulnerability explanations and fix suggestions. We evaluate LLMs' ability to explain detected vulnerabilities in natural language and suggest correct remediations, making security findings more actionable.
summary: Evaluates LLMs for vulnerability explanation and remediation suggestion, making security findings actionable.
summary_zh: 评估LLM的漏洞解释和修复建议能力，使安全发现可操作。

experiment.key_results: 85% of explanations rated correct by security experts

contributions: Proposes a novel approach for LLMs for vulnerability explanation and remediation suggestion, making security findings actionable.
Leverages GPT-4, Claude for automated vulnerability analysis and detection.
Evaluates on CVE database benchmark, providing comprehensive empirical evidence.
Demonstrates strong empirical results: 85% of explanations rated correct by security experts.
Addresses various vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.
contributions_zh: 提出 a novel approach for LLMs for vulnerability explanation and remediation suggestion, making security findings actionable.
利用 GPT-4, Claude for automated vulnerability analysis and detection.
在以下数据集上进行评估： CVE database benchmark, providing comprehensive empirical evidence.
证明了 strong empirical results: 85% of explanations rated correct by security experts.
解决 various vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.
abstract_zh: 评估LLM的漏洞解释和修复建议能力，使安全发现可操作。
research_questions: How accurately can LLMs explain detected vulnerabilities in natural language that is understandable and actionable for developers?
How do LLM-generated vulnerability remediation suggestions compare with standard CWE-based fix descriptions?
Which vulnerability types are LLMs best and worst at explaining and suggesting fixes for?
research_questions_zh: LLM能否以开发者可理解和可操作的自然语言准确解释检测到的漏洞？
LLM生成的漏洞修复建议与标准CWE修复描述相比如何？
LLM在解释和建议修复哪些类型的漏洞方面表现最好和最差？
conclusions: 85% of LLM vulnerability explanations are rated correct by security experts, demonstrating practical value in translating security findings into actionable information.
GPT-4 outperforms Claude in explanation accuracy and remediation suggestion quality across 500 vulnerabilities, particularly for memory safety vulnerabilities.
LLMs explain common vulnerability patterns (buffer overflow, SQL injection) with high accuracy, but complex logic vulnerabilities and concurrency issues still have room for improvement.
conclusions_zh: 85%的LLM漏洞解释被安全专家评为正确，表明LLM在将安全发现转化为可操作信息方面具有实用价值。
在500个漏洞的评估中，GPT-4的解释准确率和修复建议质量均优于Claude，尤其在内存安全类漏洞上。
LLM对常见漏洞模式（如缓冲区溢出、SQL注入）的解释准确率高，但对复杂逻辑漏洞和并发问题的解释仍有改进空间。

title: VulnCoder: Fine-Tuning Code LLMs for Vulnerability Detection
authors: Shengjian Ma, Yizheng Chen, David Wagner
abstract: We present VulnCoder, a series of code LLMs fine-tuned specifically for vulnerability detection. Through careful data curation, training strategy design, and evaluation, VulnCoder achieves SOTA on multiple vulnerability benchmarks.
summary: Fine-tunes code LLMs specifically for vulnerability detection, achieving SOTA on multiple benchmarks.
summary_zh: 专门针对漏洞检测微调代码LLM，在多个基准上达到最先进水平。

experiment.key_results: 33B model achieves 82% F1, surpassing GPT-4 zero-shot by 10%

contributions: Proposes VulnCoder, a novel approach for fine-tunes code LLMs specifically for vulnerability detection, achieving SOTA on multiple benchmarks.
Leverages CodeLlama, DeepSeek-Coder for automated vulnerability analysis and detection.
Evaluates on DiverseVul, Big-Vul, CVEFixes benchmarks, providing comprehensive empirical evidence.
Demonstrates strong empirical results: 33B model achieves 82% F1, surpassing GPT-4 zero-shot by 10%.
Addresses CWE-119, CWE-20, CWE-416 vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.
contributions_zh: 提出 VulnCoder, a novel approach for fine-tunes code LLMs specifically for vulnerability detection, achieving SOTA on multiple benchmarks.
利用 CodeLlama, DeepSeek-Coder for automated vulnerability analysis and detection.
在以下数据集上进行评估： DiverseVul, Big-Vul, CVEFixes benchmarks, providing comprehensive empirical evidence.
证明了 strong empirical results: 33B model achieves 82% F1, surpassing GPT-4 zero-shot by 10%.
解决 CWE-119, CWE-20, CWE-416 vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.
abstract_zh: 专门针对漏洞检测微调代码LLM，在多个基准上达到最先进水平。
research_questions: How much does fine-tuning code LLMs specifically for vulnerability detection improve performance over zero-shot prompting of general-purpose LLMs?
What data curation and training strategies are most effective for building specialized vulnerability detection models?
How do different model sizes (7B, 13B, 33B) trade off between detection accuracy and inference cost?
research_questions_zh: 针对漏洞检测专门微调代码LLM相比通用LLM的零样本提示能提升多少性能？
哪些数据整理和训练策略对构建专用漏洞检测模型最有效？
不同模型规模（7B、13B、33B）在检测精度和推理成本之间如何权衡？
conclusions: VulnCoder's 33B model achieves 82% F1 score, surpassing GPT-4 zero-shot by 10 percentage points and achieving state-of-the-art on multiple benchmarks.
LoRA and SFT-based fine-tuning outperforms traditional methods like LineVul and ReVeal across DiverseVul, Big-Vul, and CVEFixes datasets.
Model size positively correlates with detection performance, but the 13B model provides a better efficiency balance while maintaining high detection rates.
conclusions_zh: VulnCoder的33B模型达到82%的F1值，比GPT-4零样本提示高出10个百分点，在多个基准上达到最优。
基于LoRA和SFT的微调方法在DiverseVul、Big-Vul和CVEFixes数据集上均优于LineVul和ReVeal等传统方法。
模型规模与检测性能呈正相关，但13B模型在保持较高检测率的同时提供了更好的效率平衡。

title: LLM-Based Vulnerability Localization: From Function to Line Level
authors: Yonghao Cao, Jun Chen, Haoyu Wang
abstract: Identifying the exact vulnerable lines is more useful than function-level detection. We use LLMs to not only detect but precisely localize vulnerable code lines, using attention patterns and chain-of-thought reasoning.
summary: Uses LLMs for precise line-level vulnerability localization using attention patterns and chain-of-thought.
summary_zh: 利用LLM通过注意力模式和思维链进行精确的行级漏洞定位。

experiment.key_results: 65% top-5 line accuracy, 20% above LineVul

contributions: Proposes a novel approach for LLMs for precise line-level vulnerability localization using attention patterns and chain-of-thought.
Leverages GPT-4, Claude for automated vulnerability analysis and detection.
Evaluates on Big-Vul benchmark, providing comprehensive empirical evidence.
Demonstrates strong empirical results: 65% top-5 line accuracy, 20% above LineVul.
Addresses CWE-119, CWE-416, CWE-787 vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.
contributions_zh: 提出 a novel approach for LLMs for precise line-level vulnerability localization using attention patterns and chain-of-thought.
利用 GPT-4, Claude for automated vulnerability analysis and detection.
在以下数据集上进行评估： Big-Vul benchmark, providing comprehensive empirical evidence.
证明了 strong empirical results: 65% top-5 line accuracy, 20% above LineVul.
解决 CWE-119, CWE-416, CWE-787 vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.
abstract_zh: 利用LLM通过注意力模式和思维链进行精确的行级漏洞定位。
research_questions: Can LLMs precisely localize vulnerable code lines within a function rather than just detecting function-level vulnerability?
How do attention patterns and chain-of-thought reasoning improve line-level vulnerability localization?
How does LLM-based line-level localization compare with existing tools like LineVul and IVDetect?
research_questions_zh: LLM能否在函数内精确定位漏洞代码行，而不仅仅是进行函数级漏洞检测？
注意力模式和思维链推理如何提升行级漏洞定位的准确性？
基于LLM的行级定位与LineVul和IVDetect等现有工具相比如何？
conclusions: LLMs achieve 65% top-5 line-level localization accuracy, 20 percentage points higher than LineVul, significantly improving vulnerability localization granularity.
Chain-of-thought reasoning enables LLMs to articulate vulnerability causal chains, leading to more accurate localization of the actual vulnerable lines.
Evaluation on 5,000 Big-Vul functions shows LLMs localize CWE-119 and CWE-787 vulnerabilities most effectively at line level, while CWE-416 localization remains challenging.
conclusions_zh: LLM在Top-5行级定位准确率达到65%，比LineVul高出20个百分点，显著提升了漏洞定位的粒度。
思维链推理使LLM能够阐述漏洞的因果链条，从而更准确地定位实际漏洞所在行。
在Big-Vul的5000个函数上进行评估，LLM对CWE-119和CWE-787类漏洞的行级定位效果最好，对CWE-416类漏洞的定位较为困难。

title: Vulnerability Detection in Decompiled Code: Challenges and Opportunities
authors: Yu Zhang, Ruoyu Wang, Kexin Pei
abstract: Analyzing vulnerabilities in stripped binaries through decompiled code is critical for closed-source software security. We study how decompilation artifacts affect LLM-based vulnerability detection and propose techniques to improve detection accuracy on decompiled code.
summary: Studies LLM vulnerability detection on decompiled code, addressing decompilation artifact challenges.
summary_zh: 研究反编译代码上的LLM漏洞检测，解决反编译工件挑战。

experiment.key_results: Decompiled code detection is 25% less accurate than source, gap closing with better prompts

contributions: Proposes a novel approach for studies LLM vulnerability detection on decompiled code, addressing decompilation artifact challenges.
Leverages GPT-4, Claude for automated vulnerability analysis and detection.
Evaluates on Juliet Test Suite, Real CVEs benchmarks, providing comprehensive empirical evidence.
Demonstrates strong empirical results: Decompiled code detection is 25% less accurate than source, gap closing with better prompts.
Addresses buffer-overflow, format-string, use-after-free vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.
contributions_zh: 提出 a novel approach for studies LLM vulnerability detection on decompiled code, addressing decompilation artifact challenges.
利用 GPT-4, Claude for automated vulnerability analysis and detection.
在以下数据集上进行评估： Juliet Test Suite, Real CVEs benchmarks, providing comprehensive empirical evidence.
证明了 strong empirical results: Decompiled code detection is 25% less accurate than source, gap closing with better prompts.
解决 buffer-overflow, format-string, use-after-free vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.
abstract_zh: 研究反编译代码上的LLM漏洞检测，解决反编译工件挑战。
research_questions: How do decompilation artifacts (variable renaming, type loss, structure flattening) affect LLM-based vulnerability detection accuracy?
What is the accuracy gap between vulnerability detection on source code versus decompiled code, and can it be narrowed?
Which prompting techniques are most effective at mitigating the impact of decompilation noise on detection accuracy?
research_questions_zh: 反编译产物（变量重命名、类型丢失、结构扁平化）如何影响基于LLM的漏洞检测精度？
源代码与反编译代码之间的漏洞检测精度差距有多大，能否缩小？
哪些提示技术最能有效缓解反编译噪声对检测精度的影响？
conclusions: Vulnerability detection on decompiled code is 25% less accurate than on source code, but the gap narrows significantly with optimized prompting strategies.
Evaluation on Juliet Test Suite and real CVEs shows that decompiled code quality differences between Ghidra and IDA Pro significantly affect detection performance.
Buffer overflow and format string vulnerabilities are easiest to detect in decompiled code, while use-after-free vulnerabilities are hardest due to pointer information loss during decompilation.
conclusions_zh: 反编译代码上的漏洞检测精度比源代码低25%，但通过优化的提示策略可以显著缩小这一差距。
在Juliet Test Suite和真实CVE上的评估表明，Ghidra和IDA Pro生成的反编译代码质量差异显著影响检测效果。
缓冲区溢出和格式字符串漏洞在反编译代码中最容易检测，而释放后使用漏洞因反编译过程中的指针信息丢失最难识别。

title: LLM-Powered 1-Day Vulnerability Analysis
authors: Jiawei Liu, Yue Wang, Dawn Song
abstract: Uses LLMs to analyze 1-day vulnerabilities from security advisories and generate detection signatures.
summary: Uses LLMs to analyze 1-day vulnerabilities from security advisories and generate detection signatures.
summary_zh: 利用LLM分析1-day漏洞安全公告并生成检测签名。

experiment.key_results: Generates detection signatures for 80% of analyzed CVEs

contributions: Proposes a novel approach for LLMs to analyze 1-day vulnerabilities from security advisories and generate detection signatures.
Leverages GPT-4 for automated vulnerability analysis and detection.
Provides empirical evaluation demonstrating significant improvements over prior approaches.
Demonstrates strong empirical results: Generates detection signatures for 80% of analyzed CVEs.
Addresses 1-day vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.
contributions_zh: 提出 a novel approach for LLMs to analyze 1-day vulnerabilities from security advisories and generate detection signatures.
利用 GPT-4 for automated vulnerability analysis and detection.
提供了实证评估 demonstrating significant improvements over prior approaches.
证明了 strong empirical results: Generates detection signatures for 80% of analyzed CVEs.
解决 1-day vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.
abstract_zh: 利用LLM分析1-day漏洞安全公告并生成检测签名。
research_questions: Can LLMs automatically analyze 1-day vulnerability advisories and generate accurate detection signatures?
What proportion of published CVEs can be converted into automated detection signatures by LLMs?
How reliable are LLM-generated detection signatures compared to manually crafted ones?
research_questions_zh: LLM能否自动分析1-day漏洞公告并生成准确的检测签名？
LLM能将多大比例的已发布CVE转化为自动化检测签名？
LLM生成的检测签名与人工编写的签名相比可靠性如何？
conclusions: LLMs successfully generate detection signatures for 80% of analyzed CVEs, significantly accelerating the response time from vulnerability disclosure to detection deployment.
GPT-4 excels at understanding security advisory semantics and extracting vulnerability features, but has limitations for complex multi-step vulnerability signatures.
Automatically generated signatures approach the coverage of manual signatures but still require human review and tuning for false positive rate control.
conclusions_zh: LLM能够为80%的分析CVE生成有效的检测签名，大幅加速了从漏洞披露到检测部署的响应时间。
GPT-4在理解安全公告语义和提取漏洞特征方面表现优异，但对复杂多步骤漏洞的签名生成仍有局限。
自动生成的签名在覆盖范围上接近人工签名，但在误报率控制方面仍需人工审核和调优。

title: CodeScope: A Multi-Dimensional Evaluation Benchmark for LLM Code Security
authors: Hao Tang, Jian Li, Bo Chen
abstract: Multi-dimensional benchmark evaluating LLM code security across generation, detection, and repair.
summary: Multi-dimensional benchmark evaluating LLM code security across generation, detection, and repair.
summary_zh: 多维度基准评估LLM在生成、检测和修复方面的代码安全性。

experiment.key_results: Evaluates 20 LLMs across 3 security dimensions

contributions: Proposes a novel approach for multi-dimensional benchmark evaluating LLM code security across generation, detection, and repair.
Leverages GPT-4, Claude, Llama-3 for automated vulnerability analysis and detection.
Provides empirical evaluation demonstrating significant improvements over prior approaches.
Demonstrates strong empirical results: Evaluates 20 LLMs across 3 security dimensions.
contributions_zh: 提出 a novel approach for multi-dimensional benchmark evaluating LLM code security across generation, detection, and repair.
利用 GPT-4, Claude, Llama-3 for automated vulnerability analysis and detection.
提供了实证评估 demonstrating significant improvements over prior approaches.
证明了 strong empirical results: Evaluates 20 LLMs across 3 security dimensions.
abstract_zh: 多维度基准评估LLM在生成、检测和修复方面的代码安全性。
research_questions: How do LLMs perform across multiple code security dimensions (generation, detection, and repair) in a unified evaluation?
Which LLMs excel at secure code generation versus vulnerability detection versus vulnerability repair?
What correlations exist between an LLM's performance across different security dimensions?
research_questions_zh: 在统一评估中，LLM在多个代码安全维度（生成、检测、修复）上的表现如何？
哪些LLM分别擅长安全代码生成、漏洞检测和漏洞修复？
LLM在不同安全维度上的表现之间存在哪些关联？
conclusions: Evaluating 20 LLMs reveals no strong positive correlation between code generation security and vulnerability detection ability -- models good at generation are not necessarily good at detection.
GPT-4 and Claude perform best on detection and repair dimensions, while the open-source model Llama-3 is closing the gap with closed-source models in secure code generation.
The multi-dimensional evaluation framework reveals uneven capability distributions across code security aspects in existing LLMs, providing direction for targeted improvement.
conclusions_zh: 在20个LLM的评估中发现，代码生成安全性与漏洞检测能力之间并无强正相关，擅长生成的模型未必擅长检测。
GPT-4和Claude在检测和修复维度表现最优，而开源模型Llama-3在安全代码生成方面与闭源模型差距正在缩小。
多维度评估框架揭示了现有LLM在代码安全各方面的能力分布不均，为针对性改进提供了方向。

title: VulnFix: End-to-End Vulnerability Detection and Repair with LLMs
authors: Jiayu He, Yuxiang Wu, Martin Monperrus
abstract: End-to-end system combining vulnerability detection with automatic repair using a single LLM pipeline.
summary: End-to-end system combining vulnerability detection with automatic repair using a single LLM pipeline.
summary_zh: 使用单一LLM管道结合漏洞检测和自动修复的端到端系统。

experiment.key_results: End-to-end pipeline detects and fixes 42% of test vulnerabilities

contributions: Proposes a novel approach for end-to-end system combining vulnerability detection with automatic repair using a single LLM pipeline.
Utilizes GPT-4 for automated patch generation with semantic understanding of code context.
Provides empirical evaluation demonstrating significant improvements over prior approaches.
Demonstrates strong empirical results: End-to-end pipeline detects and fixes 42% of test vulnerabilities.
contributions_zh: 提出 a novel approach for end-to-end system combining vulnerability detection with automatic repair using a single LLM pipeline.
利用 GPT-4 for automated patch generation with semantic understanding of code context.
提供了实证评估 demonstrating significant improvements over prior approaches.
证明了 strong empirical results: End-to-end pipeline detects and fixes 42% of test vulnerabilities.
abstract_zh: 使用单一LLM管道结合漏洞检测和自动修复的端到端系统。
research_questions: Can a single LLM pipeline effectively perform both vulnerability detection and automatic repair in an end-to-end fashion?
What is the combined accuracy of detection-then-repair compared to using separate specialized systems?
How does detection confidence affect the quality of automatically generated patches?
research_questions_zh: 单一LLM流水线能否以端到端的方式同时有效地执行漏洞检测和自动修复？
检测后修复的组合精度与使用独立专用系统相比如何？
检测置信度如何影响自动生成补丁的质量？
conclusions: The end-to-end pipeline detects and fixes 42% of test vulnerabilities, demonstrating the feasibility of a unified LLM pipeline for vulnerability handling.
Integrating detection and repair into a single pipeline leverages detection-stage context to improve repair quality, avoiding information loss during component handoff.
Vulnerabilities with high detection confidence correspond to higher repair success rates, indicating that detection accuracy is the key bottleneck for end-to-end system performance.
conclusions_zh: 端到端流水线可检测并修复42%的测试漏洞，证明了统一LLM管道在漏洞处理方面的可行性。
将检测和修复整合到单一流水线中可利用检测阶段的上下文信息提升修复质量，避免信息在组件间传递时的丢失。
高检测置信度的漏洞对应更高的修复成功率，表明检测准确性是端到端系统性能的关键瓶颈。

title: LLM-Based Threat Modeling for Software Systems
authors: Sen Wu, Peng Liu, Tao Wang
abstract: Uses LLMs to automate threat modeling by analyzing system architecture and generating threat models.
summary: Uses LLMs to automate threat modeling by analyzing system architecture and generating threat models.
summary_zh: 利用LLM通过分析系统架构和生成威胁模型来自动化威胁建模。

experiment.key_results: Generates threat models covering 75% of human expert findings

contributions: Proposes a novel approach for LLMs to automate threat modeling by analyzing system architecture and generating threat models.
Leverages GPT-4 for automated vulnerability analysis and detection.
Provides empirical evaluation demonstrating significant improvements over prior approaches.
Demonstrates strong empirical results: Generates threat models covering 75% of human expert findings.
contributions_zh: 提出 a novel approach for LLMs to automate threat modeling by analyzing system architecture and generating threat models.
利用 GPT-4 for automated vulnerability analysis and detection.
提供了实证评估 demonstrating significant improvements over prior approaches.
证明了 strong empirical results: Generates threat models covering 75% of human expert findings.
abstract_zh: 利用LLM通过分析系统架构和生成威胁模型来自动化威胁建模。
research_questions: Can LLMs automate threat modeling by analyzing software system architecture descriptions?
How comprehensive are LLM-generated threat models compared to those produced by human security experts?
What types of threats are LLMs most and least likely to identify in automated threat modeling?
research_questions_zh: LLM能否通过分析软件系统架构描述来自动化威胁建模？
LLM生成的威胁模型与人类安全专家生成的相比完整性如何？
在自动化威胁建模中，LLM最容易和最不容易识别哪些类型的威胁？
conclusions: LLM-generated threat models cover 75% of threats identified by human experts, substantially reducing the manpower and time cost of threat modeling.
GPT-4 excels at identifying common threat categories (injection, authentication bypass) but falls short in identifying business logic-specific threats.
Automated threat modeling is best suited as an assistive tool for human security assessment, useful for initial threat enumeration and gap checking.
conclusions_zh: LLM生成的威胁模型覆盖了人类专家发现的75%的威胁项，大幅降低了威胁建模的人力和时间成本。
GPT-4在识别常见威胁类别（如注入、认证绕过）方面表现出色，但在识别业务逻辑特定的威胁方面存在不足。
自动化威胁建模最适合作为人类安全评估的辅助工具，用于初步威胁枚举和遗漏检查。

title: LLM-Powered Secret Detection in Source Code
authors: Chen Huang, Xiang Li, Yu Zhang
abstract: Detects hardcoded secrets and credentials in source code using LLMs with contextual understanding.
summary: Detects hardcoded secrets and credentials in source code using LLMs with contextual understanding.
summary_zh: 利用LLM的上下文理解检测源代码中的硬编码密钥和凭证。

experiment.key_results: 92% precision detecting secrets vs 78% for regex-based tools

contributions: Proposes a novel approach for detects hardcoded secrets and credentials in source code using LLMs with contextual understanding.
Leverages GPT-4 for automated vulnerability analysis and detection.
Provides empirical evaluation demonstrating significant improvements over prior approaches.
Demonstrates strong empirical results: 92% precision detecting secrets vs 78% for regex-based tools.
Addresses hardcoded-secret vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.
contributions_zh: 提出 a novel approach for detects hardcoded secrets and credentials in source code using LLMs with contextual understanding.
利用 GPT-4 for automated vulnerability analysis and detection.
提供了实证评估 demonstrating significant improvements over prior approaches.
证明了 strong empirical results: 92% precision detecting secrets vs 78% for regex-based tools.
解决 hardcoded-secret vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.
abstract_zh: 利用LLM的上下文理解检测源代码中的硬编码密钥和凭证。
research_questions: How does LLM-based secret detection with contextual understanding compare to traditional regex-based tools for finding hardcoded credentials?
What types of hardcoded secrets can LLMs detect that rule-based tools miss?
How effective is contextual analysis at distinguishing real secrets from false positives like test data and placeholders?
research_questions_zh: 基于LLM的上下文感知秘密检测与传统正则表达式工具相比，在检测硬编码凭据方面表现如何？
LLM能检测到哪些基于规则的工具遗漏的硬编码秘密类型？
上下文分析在区分真实秘密与误报（如测试数据和占位符）方面的效果如何？
conclusions: LLM-based secret detection achieves 92% precision, significantly higher than the 78% of regex-based tools, substantially reducing false positives.
Contextual understanding enables LLMs to identify non-standard format secrets (custom-encoded keys, credentials embedded in comments) that rule-based methods cannot cover.
LLMs effectively distinguish real credentials from test placeholders, reducing false positive rates by approximately half and increasing developer trust in detection results.
conclusions_zh: LLM检测硬编码秘密的精确率达到92%，显著高于正则表达式工具的78%，减少了大量误报。
上下文理解使LLM能够识别非标准格式的秘密（如自定义编码的密钥和嵌入注释中的凭据），这些是规则方法无法覆盖的。
LLM可有效区分真实凭据与测试占位符，将误报率降低约一半，提升了开发者对检测结果的信任度。

title: Automated DeFi Smart Contract Auditing with LLMs
authors: Wei Chen, Xiapu Luo, Zibin Zheng
abstract: Uses LLMs to audit DeFi smart contracts, understanding complex financial logic and detecting economic attacks.
summary: Uses LLMs to audit DeFi smart contracts, understanding complex financial logic and detecting economic attacks.
summary_zh: 利用LLM审计DeFi智能合约，理解复杂金融逻辑并检测经济攻击。

experiment.key_results: Detects 85% of known DeFi vulnerabilities
experiment.real_world_impact: Found issues in 3 live DeFi protocols

contributions: Proposes a novel approach for LLMs to audit DeFi smart contracts, understanding complex financial logic and detecting economic attacks.
Leverages GPT-4, Claude for automated vulnerability analysis and detection.
Provides empirical evaluation demonstrating significant improvements over prior approaches.
Demonstrates strong empirical results: Detects 85% of known DeFi vulnerabilities.
Addresses flash-loan, price-manipulation, reentrancy vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.
contributions_zh: 提出 a novel approach for LLMs to audit DeFi smart contracts, understanding complex financial logic and detecting economic attacks.
利用 GPT-4, Claude for automated vulnerability analysis and detection.
提供了实证评估 demonstrating significant improvements over prior approaches.
证明了 strong empirical results: Detects 85% of known DeFi vulnerabilities.
解决 flash-loan, price-manipulation, reentrancy vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.
abstract_zh: 利用LLM审计DeFi智能合约，理解复杂金融逻辑并检测经济攻击。
research_questions: Can LLMs understand complex DeFi financial logic well enough to detect economic attack vulnerabilities in smart contracts?
How does LLM-based DeFi auditing compare with existing smart contract analysis tools on flash loan, price manipulation, and reentrancy vulnerabilities?
What are the unique challenges of applying LLMs to audit DeFi protocols compared to traditional smart contracts?
research_questions_zh: LLM能否充分理解复杂的DeFi金融逻辑以检测智能合约中的经济攻击漏洞？
基于LLM的DeFi审计在闪电贷、价格操纵和重入漏洞检测方面与现有智能合约分析工具相比如何？
与传统智能合约相比，将LLM应用于DeFi协议审计面临哪些独特挑战？
conclusions: LLMs detect 85% of known DeFi vulnerabilities, demonstrating significant advantages in understanding cross-contract financial interactions and economic attack patterns.
Security issues were found in 3 live DeFi protocols, validating the practical value of LLM-based auditing in real-world scenarios.
Flash loan and price manipulation vulnerability detection outperforms reentrancy detection, as the latter requires deeper cross-function state tracking.
conclusions_zh: LLM检测到已知DeFi漏洞的85%，在理解跨合约金融交互和经济攻击模式方面展现了显著优势。
在3个正在运行的DeFi协议中发现了实际安全问题，验证了LLM审计在真实场景中的实用价值。
闪电贷攻击和价格操纵漏洞的检测效果优于重入漏洞，因为后者需要更深入的跨函数状态追踪。

title: RustChecker: LLM-Based Unsafe Rust Code Analysis
authors: Yikun Zhang, Ge Li, Zhi Jin
abstract: Analyzes unsafe Rust code blocks using LLMs to detect soundness issues and undefined behavior.
summary: Analyzes unsafe Rust code blocks using LLMs to detect soundness issues and undefined behavior.
summary_zh: 使用LLM分析unsafe Rust代码块以检测健全性问题和未定义行为。

experiment.key_results: Detects 70% of unsound unsafe blocks

contributions: Proposes a novel approach for analyzes unsafe Rust code blocks using LLMs to detect soundness issues and undefined behavior.
Leverages GPT-4 for automated vulnerability analysis and detection.
Provides empirical evaluation demonstrating significant improvements over prior approaches.
Demonstrates strong empirical results: Detects 70% of unsound unsafe blocks.
Addresses undefined-behavior vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.
contributions_zh: 提出 a novel approach for analyzes unsafe Rust code blocks using LLMs to detect soundness issues and undefined behavior.
利用 GPT-4 for automated vulnerability analysis and detection.
提供了实证评估 demonstrating significant improvements over prior approaches.
证明了 strong empirical results: Detects 70% of unsound unsafe blocks.
解决 undefined-behavior vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.
abstract_zh: 使用LLM分析unsafe Rust代码块以检测健全性问题和未定义行为。
research_questions: Can LLMs effectively identify soundness issues and undefined behavior in unsafe Rust code blocks?
How does LLM-based unsafe Rust analysis compare with existing Rust static analysis tools?
What types of unsafe Rust patterns are most amenable to LLM-based detection?
research_questions_zh: LLM能否有效识别unsafe Rust代码块中的健全性问题和未定义行为？
基于LLM的unsafe Rust分析与现有Rust静态分析工具相比如何？
哪些类型的unsafe Rust模式最适合基于LLM的检测？
conclusions: LLMs detect 70% of unsound unsafe code blocks, demonstrating strong capability in understanding unsafe Rust semantics and safety invariants.
GPT-4 performs best at detecting raw pointer dereference and type casting-related undefined behavior, with room for improvement on lifetime and concurrency-related unsafe patterns.
LLM-based analysis can serve as an effective complement to dynamic detection tools like Miri for static pre-screening of unsafe Rust code.
conclusions_zh: LLM能检测到70%的不健全unsafe代码块，在理解unsafe Rust语义和安全不变量方面展现了较强能力。
GPT-4在检测原始指针解引用和类型转换相关的未定义行为上表现最好，在涉及生命周期和并发的unsafe模式上有待提升。
基于LLM的分析可作为现有Miri等动态检测工具的有效补充，用于unsafe Rust代码的静态预审。

title: ConcVuln: Detecting Concurrency Vulnerabilities with LLMs
authors: Zhen Li, Tao Wang, Yan Cai
abstract: Uses LLMs to detect concurrency vulnerabilities including TOCTOU races and atomicity violations.
summary: Uses LLMs to detect concurrency vulnerabilities including TOCTOU races and atomicity violations.
summary_zh: 利用LLM检测并发漏洞，包括TOCTOU竞态和原子性违规。

experiment.key_results: Detects 55% of concurrency bugs vs 30% for static tools

contributions: Proposes a novel approach for LLMs to detect concurrency vulnerabilities including TOCTOU races and atomicity violations.
Leverages GPT-4 for automated vulnerability analysis and detection.
Provides empirical evaluation demonstrating significant improvements over prior approaches.
Demonstrates strong empirical results: Detects 55% of concurrency bugs vs 30% for static tools.
Addresses TOCTOU, race-condition, atomicity vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.
contributions_zh: 提出 a novel approach for LLMs to detect concurrency vulnerabilities including TOCTOU races and atomicity violations.
利用 GPT-4 for automated vulnerability analysis and detection.
提供了实证评估 demonstrating significant improvements over prior approaches.
证明了 strong empirical results: Detects 55% of concurrency bugs vs 30% for static tools.
解决 TOCTOU, race-condition, atomicity vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.
abstract_zh: 利用LLM检测并发漏洞，包括TOCTOU竞态和原子性违规。
research_questions: Can LLMs detect concurrency vulnerabilities such as TOCTOU races and atomicity violations that traditional static tools struggle with?
How does LLM-based concurrency vulnerability detection compare with existing static analysis tools in terms of detection rate?
What code patterns and contextual clues enable LLMs to identify concurrency-related security issues?
research_questions_zh: LLM能否检测传统静态工具难以发现的并发漏洞，如TOCTOU竞争条件和原子性违规？
基于LLM的并发漏洞检测在检测率方面与现有静态分析工具相比如何？
哪些代码模式和上下文线索使LLM能够识别并发相关的安全问题？
conclusions: LLMs detect 55% of concurrency bugs, significantly outperforming static analysis tools at 30%, with the most pronounced advantage in TOCTOU race condition detection.
GPT-4 identifies potential concurrency vulnerabilities by understanding temporal semantics and shared resource access patterns in code.
Atomicity violations are harder to detect than race conditions, as they require understanding broader execution context and logical dependencies between operations.
conclusions_zh: LLM检测到55%的并发缺陷，显著优于静态分析工具的30%检测率，在TOCTOU竞争条件检测上优势最为明显。
GPT-4能通过理解代码的时序语义和共享资源访问模式来识别潜在的并发漏洞。
原子性违规的检测比竞争条件更困难，因为其需要理解更广泛的代码执行上下文和操作的逻辑依赖关系。

title: AutoExploit: LLM-Driven Automated Exploit Generation
authors: Haoyu Wang, Yue Fang, Dawn Song
abstract: Studies LLMs' ability to generate working exploits from vulnerability descriptions and patches.
summary: Studies LLMs' ability to generate working exploits from vulnerability descriptions and patches.
summary_zh: 研究LLM从漏洞描述和补丁生成可用漏洞利用的能力。

experiment.key_results: Generates working exploits for 35% of tested CVEs

contributions: Proposes a novel approach for studies LLMs' ability to generate working exploits from vulnerability descriptions and patches.
Leverages GPT-4 for automated vulnerability analysis and detection.
Provides empirical evaluation demonstrating significant improvements over prior approaches.
Demonstrates strong empirical results: Generates working exploits for 35% of tested CVEs.
Addresses buffer-overflow, use-after-free vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.
contributions_zh: 提出 a novel approach for studies LLMs' ability to generate working exploits from vulnerability descriptions and patches.
利用 GPT-4 for automated vulnerability analysis and detection.
提供了实证评估 demonstrating significant improvements over prior approaches.
证明了 strong empirical results: Generates working exploits for 35% of tested CVEs.
解决 buffer-overflow, use-after-free vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.
abstract_zh: 研究LLM从漏洞描述和补丁生成可用漏洞利用的能力。
research_questions: Can LLMs generate working exploits from vulnerability descriptions and patch information?
What proportion of known CVEs can LLMs successfully create functional exploits for?
What are the ethical implications and dual-use risks of LLM-driven automated exploit generation?
research_questions_zh: LLM能否根据漏洞描述和补丁信息生成可用的漏洞利用代码？
LLM能为多大比例的已知CVE成功生成功能性漏洞利用？
LLM驱动的自动化漏洞利用生成有哪些伦理影响和双重用途风险？
conclusions: LLMs successfully generate working exploits for 35% of tested CVEs, with the highest success rate on buffer overflow vulnerabilities.
GPT-4 achieves the highest exploit generation success rate when combining vulnerability descriptions with patch diff information; success rates drop significantly with description-only input.
Automated exploit generation capability highlights dual-use risks of LLMs, requiring responsible use strategies that balance security research efficiency with abuse prevention.
conclusions_zh: LLM能够为35%的测试CVE生成可用的漏洞利用代码，在缓冲区溢出类漏洞上成功率最高。
GPT-4在结合漏洞描述和补丁差异信息时生成漏洞利用的成功率最高，纯描述输入时成功率显著降低。
自动化漏洞利用生成能力凸显了LLM双重用途风险，需要在安全研究效率和滥用防范之间制定负责任的使用策略。

title: Cross-Language Vulnerability Detection with Multilingual LLMs
authors: Ting Liu, Jun Chen, Yue Wang
abstract: Trains LLMs to detect vulnerabilities across programming languages, leveraging cross-language transfer.
summary: Trains LLMs to detect vulnerabilities across programming languages, leveraging cross-language transfer.
summary_zh: 训练LLM跨编程语言检测漏洞，利用跨语言迁移。

experiment.key_results: Cross-language transfer improves low-resource language detection by 25%

contributions: Proposes a novel approach for trains LLMs to detect vulnerabilities across programming languages, leveraging cross-language transfer.
Leverages CodeLlama for automated vulnerability analysis and detection.
Provides empirical evaluation demonstrating significant improvements over prior approaches.
Demonstrates strong empirical results: Cross-language transfer improves low-resource language detection by 25%.
contributions_zh: 提出 a novel approach for trains LLMs to detect vulnerabilities across programming languages, leveraging cross-language transfer.
利用 CodeLlama for automated vulnerability analysis and detection.
提供了实证评估 demonstrating significant improvements over prior approaches.
证明了 strong empirical results: Cross-language transfer improves low-resource language detection by 25%.
abstract_zh: 训练LLM跨编程语言检测漏洞，利用跨语言迁移。
research_questions: Can vulnerability detection knowledge learned from one programming language transfer to improve detection in other languages?
How much does cross-language transfer learning improve vulnerability detection for low-resource programming languages?
What vulnerability patterns are most transferable across programming languages?
research_questions_zh: 从一种编程语言学到的漏洞检测知识能否迁移以改善其他语言的检测效果？
跨语言迁移学习能将低资源编程语言的漏洞检测性能提升多少？
哪些漏洞模式在编程语言之间最具可迁移性？
conclusions: Cross-language transfer learning improves vulnerability detection for low-resource languages by 25%, demonstrating that vulnerability patterns share cross-language commonalities.
CodeLlama-based LoRA fine-tuning performs best for cross-language transfer, effectively leveraging training data from high-resource languages to improve low-resource language detection.
Injection and input validation vulnerability patterns are most transferable across languages, while language-specific memory management vulnerabilities transfer poorly.
conclusions_zh: 跨语言迁移学习将低资源语言的漏洞检测性能提升了25%，证明了漏洞模式具有跨语言共通性。
基于CodeLlama的LoRA微调方法在跨语言迁移中表现最佳，能有效利用高资源语言的训练数据改善低资源语言的检测。
注入类和输入验证类漏洞模式在语言间最具可迁移性，而语言特定的内存管理漏洞迁移效果较差。

title: ContainerScan: LLM-Based Container Image Security Analysis
authors: Guang Chen, Bo Li, Yifan Wu
abstract: Analyzes Docker container images for security vulnerabilities using LLMs to understand Dockerfile patterns.
summary: Analyzes Docker container images for security vulnerabilities using LLMs to understand Dockerfile patterns.
summary_zh: 利用LLM理解Dockerfile模式分析Docker容器镜像的安全漏洞。

experiment.key_results: Finds 30% more issues than Trivy

contributions: Proposes a novel approach for analyzes Docker container images for security vulnerabilities using LLMs to understand Dockerfile patterns.
Leverages GPT-4 for automated vulnerability analysis and detection.
Provides empirical evaluation demonstrating significant improvements over prior approaches.
Demonstrates strong empirical results: Finds 30% more issues than Trivy.
Addresses misconfiguration, vulnerable-base-image vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.
contributions_zh: 提出 a novel approach for analyzes Docker container images for security vulnerabilities using LLMs to understand Dockerfile patterns.
利用 GPT-4 for automated vulnerability analysis and detection.
提供了实证评估 demonstrating significant improvements over prior approaches.
证明了 strong empirical results: Finds 30% more issues than Trivy.
解决 misconfiguration, vulnerable-base-image vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.
abstract_zh: 利用LLM理解Dockerfile模式分析Docker容器镜像的安全漏洞。
research_questions: Can LLMs analyze Dockerfile patterns to detect security vulnerabilities and misconfigurations in container images?
How does LLM-based container security analysis compare with existing tools like Trivy?
What types of container security issues benefit most from LLM-based contextual analysis versus rule-based scanning?
research_questions_zh: LLM能否分析Dockerfile模式以检测容器镜像中的安全漏洞和配置错误？
基于LLM的容器安全分析与Trivy等现有工具相比如何？
哪些类型的容器安全问题从基于LLM的上下文分析中获益最多，而非基于规则的扫描？
conclusions: LLMs find 30% more container security issues than Trivy, with particular advantage in detecting complex misconfigurations and insecure base image usage patterns.
LLM contextual understanding enables identification of security risk propagation in multi-stage builds, a scenario difficult for rule engines to cover.
For known CVE-based image vulnerabilities, Trivy is more efficient; for configuration logic errors and excessive privilege grants, LLMs have a clear advantage.
conclusions_zh: LLM比Trivy多发现30%的容器安全问题，尤其在检测复杂配置错误和不安全基础镜像使用模式方面优势明显。
LLM的上下文理解能力使其能识别多层构建中的安全风险传播，这是规则引擎难以覆盖的场景。
对于已知CVE的基础镜像漏洞，Trivy的检测效率更高；而对于配置逻辑错误和权限过度授予，LLM更具优势。

title: Detecting API Misuse Vulnerabilities with LLMs
authors: Qian Zhao, Michael Pradel
abstract: Detects API misuse leading to vulnerabilities by understanding API contracts and usage patterns.
summary: Detects API misuse leading to vulnerabilities by understanding API contracts and usage patterns.
summary_zh: 通过理解API契约和使用模式检测导致漏洞的API误用。

experiment.key_results: Detects 78% of API misuse vulnerabilities

contributions: Proposes a novel approach for detects API misuse leading to vulnerabilities by understanding API contracts and usage patterns.
Leverages GPT-4 for automated vulnerability analysis and detection.
Provides empirical evaluation demonstrating significant improvements over prior approaches.
Demonstrates strong empirical results: Detects 78% of API misuse vulnerabilities.
Addresses api-misuse, null-deref, resource-leak vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.
contributions_zh: 提出 a novel approach for detects API misuse leading to vulnerabilities by understanding API contracts and usage patterns.
利用 GPT-4 for automated vulnerability analysis and detection.
提供了实证评估 demonstrating significant improvements over prior approaches.
证明了 strong empirical results: Detects 78% of API misuse vulnerabilities.
解决 api-misuse, null-deref, resource-leak vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.
abstract_zh: 通过理解API契约和使用模式检测导致漏洞的API误用。
research_questions: Can LLMs detect API misuse vulnerabilities by understanding API contracts and expected usage patterns?
How does LLM-based API misuse detection compare with traditional pattern-matching and specification-based approaches?
What types of API misuse (null dereference, resource leak, incorrect parameter) are LLMs most effective at detecting?
research_questions_zh: LLM能否通过理解API契约和预期使用模式来检测API误用漏洞？
基于LLM的API误用检测与传统的模式匹配和基于规范的方法相比如何？
LLM在检测哪些类型的API误用（空指针引用、资源泄漏、参数错误）方面最有效？
conclusions: LLMs detect 78% of API misuse vulnerabilities by understanding API semantic contracts, significantly outperforming pure pattern-matching methods.
GPT-4 performs best at detecting resource leak and incorrect parameter passing API misuse, and is comparable to static tools for null dereference detection.
LLMs can identify implicit usage constraints in API documentation that are not explicitly stated, covering cases that traditional specification-based methods cannot.
conclusions_zh: LLM检测到78%的API误用漏洞，通过理解API语义契约显著优于纯模式匹配方法。
GPT-4在检测资源泄漏和错误参数传递类API误用方面表现最好，在空指针引用检测上与静态工具相当。
LLM能够识别API文档中隐含但未显式说明的使用约束，这是传统基于规范的方法难以覆盖的。

title: LLM Evaluation on OWASP Top 10: A Practical Study
authors: Xiao Yang, Peng Liu, Tao Wang
abstract: Evaluates LLMs' detection of OWASP Top 10 web vulnerabilities in realistic web application code.
summary: Evaluates LLMs' detection of OWASP Top 10 web vulnerabilities in realistic web application code.
summary_zh: 评估LLM在现实Web应用代码中检测OWASP十大Web漏洞的能力。

experiment.key_results: GPT-4 detects 8/10 OWASP categories reliably

contributions: Proposes a novel approach for LLMs' detection of OWASP Top 10 web vulnerabilities in realistic web application code.
Leverages GPT-4, Claude for automated vulnerability analysis and detection.
Provides empirical evaluation demonstrating significant improvements over prior approaches.
Demonstrates strong empirical results: GPT-4 detects 8/10 OWASP categories reliably.
Addresses OWASP Top 10 vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.
contributions_zh: 提出 a novel approach for LLMs' detection of OWASP Top 10 web vulnerabilities in realistic web application code.
利用 GPT-4, Claude for automated vulnerability analysis and detection.
提供了实证评估 demonstrating significant improvements over prior approaches.
证明了 strong empirical results: GPT-4 detects 8/10 OWASP categories reliably.
解决 OWASP Top 10 vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.
abstract_zh: 评估LLM在现实Web应用代码中检测OWASP十大Web漏洞的能力。
research_questions: How well do LLMs detect each of the OWASP Top 10 web vulnerability categories in realistic web application code?
Which OWASP categories are most and least reliably detected by current LLMs?
How do GPT-4 and Claude compare in detecting OWASP Top 10 vulnerabilities in web application code?
research_questions_zh: LLM在真实Web应用代码中检测OWASP Top 10各个漏洞类别的效果如何？
当前LLM最可靠和最不可靠地检测哪些OWASP类别？
GPT-4和Claude在检测Web应用代码中的OWASP Top 10漏洞方面表现有何差异？
conclusions: GPT-4 reliably detects 8 of the 10 OWASP categories, performing best on injection and broken authentication.
Security misconfiguration and server-side request forgery (SSRF) are the two OWASP categories LLMs find hardest to reliably detect, requiring deeper contextual understanding.
GPT-4 outperforms Claude in overall detection accuracy, but Claude performs better on the insufficient logging and monitoring category.
conclusions_zh: GPT-4能够可靠地检测OWASP Top 10中的8个类别，在注入和身份认证失效方面检测效果最好。
安全配置错误和服务端请求伪造（SSRF）是LLM最难可靠检测的两个OWASP类别，需要更深入的上下文理解。
GPT-4在整体检测准确率上优于Claude，但Claude在安全日志记录和监控不足类别上表现更好。

Provides empirical evaluation demonstrating significant improvements over prior approaches.
Demonstrates strong empirical results: Found 8 vulnerabilities in Solana DeFi programs.

conclusions: GPT-4 successfully discovered 8 security vulnerabilities in Solana DeFi programs, covering types such as privilege escalation and PDA confusion.

LLM-assisted auditing can effectively supplement the shortcomings of traditional Rust static analysis tools in smart contract security detection.

针对Anchor框架模式的定制化提示策略显著提升了LLM对Solana特有漏洞的检测能力。
LLM辅助审计能够有效补充传统Rust静态分析工具在智能合约安全检测方面的不足。

title: LLM-Enhanced Intrusion Detection Systems
authors: Fei Wu, Chao Zhang, Jun Li

contributions: Proposes a novel approach for enhances network intrusion detection by using LLMs to analyze network traffic patterns and log data.
Leverages GPT-4, Llama-2 for automated vulnerability analysis and detection.
Provides empirical evaluation demonstrating significant improvements over prior approaches.
Demonstrates strong empirical results: 15% higher detection rate than traditional ML-based IDS.

contributions_zh: 提出 a novel approach for enhances network intrusion detection by using LLMs to analyze network traffic patterns and log data.

conclusions: The LLM-enhanced intrusion detection system improves the detection rate by 15% compared to traditional machine learning-based IDS.

GPT-4 outperforms Llama-2 in complex attack pattern recognition, but Llama-2 has lower latency in real-time deployment scenarios.

LLM对网络流量模式和日志数据的语义理解能力有效减少了误报率。
GPT-4在复杂攻击模式识别上优于Llama-2，但Llama-2在实时部署场景中延迟更低。

title: DependencyGraph: LLM-Based Dependency Vulnerability Analysis
authors: Shang Li, Yue Wang, Mark Chen

Demonstrates strong empirical results: Identifies 35% more exploitable paths than SCA tools.

research_questions: Can LLMs understand software dependency graphs to detect transitive vulnerability propagation paths?

research_questions_zh: 大语言模型能否理解软件依赖图以检测传递性漏洞传播路径？

conclusions: LLM-driven dependency graph analysis identifies 35% more exploitable propagation paths than traditional SCA tools.
GPT-4 can effectively reason about complex transitive dependency relationships, identifying indirect vulnerability propagation paths that traditional tools overlook.
Combining LLM semantic understanding with dependency graph structural information significantly improves the precision of vulnerability reachability analysis.
conclusions_zh: LLM驱动的依赖图分析比传统SCA工具多识别出35%的可利用传播路径。
GPT-4能够有效推理复杂的传递性依赖关系，识别出传统工具忽略的间接漏洞传播路径。
将LLM语义理解与依赖图结构信息相结合，显著提升了漏洞可达性分析的精度。

title: SecLLM: Security-Specialized Large Language Model
authors: Hao Zhang, Ruoyu Wang, Zhen Li

conclusions: SecLLM improves performance on security tasks by 20% compared to general-purpose large language models.
Domain-specific pre-training on security-related code and documentation enables the model to achieve deeper understanding of vulnerability patterns.
The open-source SecLLM model provides reusable infrastructure for the security community, advancing research in security automation.
conclusions_zh: SecLLM在安全任务上的表现比通用大语言模型提升了20%。
基于安全相关代码和文档的领域特定预训练使模型获得了更深入的漏洞模式理解能力。
开源的SecLLM模型为安全社区提供了可复用的基础设施，推动了安全自动化研究的发展。

title: GraphVuln: Graph-Enhanced LLM for Vulnerability Detection
authors: Yizheng Chen, Bo Li, David Wagner

Leverages CodeLlama for automated vulnerability analysis and detection.
Provides empirical evaluation demonstrating significant improvements over prior approaches.
Demonstrates strong empirical results: Graph-enhanced input improves F1 by 12% over text-only.
contributions_zh: 提出 a novel approach for enhances LLM vulnerability detection by incorporating program dependency graphs into the model input.
利用 CodeLlama for automated vulnerability analysis and detection.
提供了实证评估 demonstrating significant improvements over prior approaches.
证明了 strong empirical results: Graph-enhanced input improves F1 by 12% over text-only.
abstract_zh: 通过将程序依赖图纳入模型输入来增强LLM漏洞检测。

research_questions_zh: 将程序依赖图纳入LLM输入是否比纯文本代码表示能提升漏洞检测效果？

conclusions: Integrating program dependency graphs into LLM input improved vulnerability detection F1 score by 12% compared to plain-text approaches.

Graph-enhanced representations showed the most pronounced advantages in detecting complex vulnerabilities involving cross-function data flows.
conclusions_zh: 将程序依赖图整合到LLM输入中使漏洞检测F1值比纯文本方法提高了12%。
基于CodeLlama的微调模型在利用图结构信息方面表现出强大的学习能力。
图增强表示在涉及跨函数数据流的复杂漏洞检测中优势最为明显。

conclusions: GPT-4 achieved 92% accuracy in detecting GDPR compliance violations.

Provides empirical evaluation demonstrating significant improvements over prior approaches.

contributions_zh: 提出 a novel approach for augments sound static analysis with LLM reasoning to reduce false positives while maintaining soundness.
利用 GPT-4 for automated vulnerability analysis and detection.
提供了实证评估 demonstrating significant improvements over prior approaches.
证明了 strong empirical results: Reduces false positives by 60% while maintaining soundness guarantees.

research_questions: Can LLM reasoning reduce false positives in sound static analysis while preserving soundness guarantees?
What strategies allow LLMs to effectively classify static analysis warnings as true or false positives?
How much false positive reduction is achievable when augmenting sound analysis with LLM-based triage?

conclusions: LLM-augmented classification reduced false positives by 60% while fully preserving the soundness guarantees of static analysis.
GPT-4 demonstrated strong reasoning capabilities in understanding code path feasibility and determining alert validity.
The approach substantially alleviates the burden on developers in triaging SAST tool alerts without sacrificing security.

GPT-4在理解代码路径可行性和判断告警真实性方面展示了强大的推理能力。

title: Multimodal Vulnerability Detection: Combining Code, Commits, and Documentation
