contributions: Proposes a novel approach for multimodal LLM analysis of code, commit messages, and documentation for improved vulnerability detection.
Leverages GPT-4 for automated vulnerability analysis and detection.
Provides empirical evaluation demonstrating significant improvements over prior approaches.
Demonstrates strong empirical results: Multimodal input improves detection by 18% over code-only.
contributions_zh: 提出 a novel approach for multimodal LLM analysis of code, commit messages, and documentation for improved vulnerability detection.
利用 GPT-4 for automated vulnerability analysis and detection.
提供了实证评估 demonstrating significant improvements over prior approaches.
证明了 strong empirical results: Multimodal input improves detection by 18% over code-only.
abstract_zh: 使用多模态LLM分析代码、提交消息和文档以改进漏洞检测。
research_questions: Does combining code, commit messages, and documentation as multimodal input improve LLM-based vulnerability detection?
What is the relative contribution of each modality (code, commits, documentation) to detection accuracy?
How much improvement does multimodal analysis provide over code-only vulnerability detection?
research_questions_zh: 将代码、提交信息和文档作为多模态输入结合是否能提升基于LLM的漏洞检测？
每种模态（代码、提交、文档）对检测准确性的相对贡献是多少？
多模态分析相比仅基于代码的漏洞检测能提供多大程度的改进？
conclusions: Multimodal input (code + commit messages + documentation) improved vulnerability detection by 18% compared to code-only approaches.
Change semantic information in commit messages provides significant value for identifying security-related patches and vulnerability introductions.
Documentation helps LLMs understand intended behavior, enabling more accurate identification of vulnerabilities where code deviates from specifications.
conclusions_zh: 多模态输入（代码+提交信息+文档）使漏洞检测效果比仅用代码提升了18%。
提交信息中的变更语义信息对于识别安全相关补丁和漏洞引入具有重要价值。
文档信息帮助LLM理解预期行为，从而更准确地判断代码是否存在偏离规范的漏洞。

title: FixIt: LLM-Based Automated Fix for Static Analysis Warnings
authors: Qiushi Zhang, Jun Chen, Haoyu Wang
abstract: Automatically fixes static analysis warnings using LLMs, reducing developer burden from SAST tool noise.
summary: Automatically fixes static analysis warnings using LLMs, reducing developer burden from SAST tool noise.
summary_zh: 利用LLM自动修复静态分析警告，减少SAST工具噪音带来的开发者负担。

experiment.key_results: Fixes 55% of Semgrep warnings correctly

contributions: Proposes a novel approach for automatically fixes static analysis warnings using LLMs, reducing developer burden from SAST tool noise.
Utilizes GPT-4 for automated patch generation with semantic understanding of code context.
Provides empirical evaluation demonstrating significant improvements over prior approaches.
Demonstrates strong empirical results: Fixes 55% of Semgrep warnings correctly.
contributions_zh: 提出 a novel approach for automatically fixes static analysis warnings using LLMs, reducing developer burden from SAST tool noise.
利用 GPT-4 for automated patch generation with semantic understanding of code context.
提供了实证评估 demonstrating significant improvements over prior approaches.
证明了 strong empirical results: Fixes 55% of Semgrep warnings correctly.
abstract_zh: 利用LLM自动修复静态分析警告，减少SAST工具噪音带来的开发者负担。
research_questions: Can LLMs automatically generate correct fixes for static analysis warnings produced by SAST tools like Semgrep?
What proportion of Semgrep warnings can be correctly resolved by LLM-generated patches?
How does LLM-based auto-fixing affect developer productivity when dealing with SAST tool output?
research_questions_zh: 大语言模型能否自动为Semgrep等SAST工具产生的静态分析告警生成正确修复？
LLM生成的补丁能够正确解决多大比例的Semgrep告警？
基于LLM的自动修复如何影响开发者处理SAST工具输出时的生产效率？
conclusions: LLMs successfully and correctly fixed 55% of Semgrep alerts, significantly reducing the developer burden from SAST noise.
Incorporating contextual information from static analysis alerts into prompts effectively guides LLMs to generate targeted fix patches.
Fix success rates were highest for alert types with well-defined rules, such as unchecked return values and missing input validation.
conclusions_zh: LLM成功正确修复了55%的Semgrep告警，显著减轻了开发者处理SAST噪声的负担。
将静态分析告警的上下文信息纳入提示能够有效引导LLM生成针对性的修复补丁。
对于规则明确的告警类型（如未检查返回值、缺失输入验证），修复成功率最高。

title: CodeAgent-Fix: Multi-Step Program Repair with Tool-Augmented LLMs
authors: Yuxiang Wu, He Ye, Martin Monperrus
abstract: Multi-step repair agent that uses tools (debugger, compiler, tests) to iteratively fix complex bugs.
summary: Multi-step repair agent that uses tools (debugger, compiler, tests) to iteratively fix complex bugs.
summary_zh: 使用工具（调试器、编译器、测试）迭代修复复杂漏洞的多步修复代理。

experiment.key_results: Fixes 28% more bugs than single-step approaches

contributions: Proposes a novel approach for multi-step repair agent that uses tools (debugger, compiler, tests) to iteratively fix complex bugs.
Utilizes GPT-4 for automated patch generation with semantic understanding of code context.
Provides empirical evaluation demonstrating significant improvements over prior approaches.
Demonstrates strong empirical results: Fixes 28% more bugs than single-step approaches.
contributions_zh: 提出 a novel approach for multi-step repair agent that uses tools (debugger, compiler, tests) to iteratively fix complex bugs.
利用 GPT-4 for automated patch generation with semantic understanding of code context.
提供了实证评估 demonstrating significant improvements over prior approaches.
证明了 strong empirical results: Fixes 28% more bugs than single-step approaches.
abstract_zh: 使用工具（调试器、编译器、测试）迭代修复复杂漏洞的多步修复代理。
research_questions: Does a multi-step agent approach using tools (debugger, compiler, tests) outperform single-pass LLM patch generation?
How does iterative tool-augmented repair improve fix correctness for complex, multi-location bugs?
What is the optimal agent workflow for combining LLM reasoning with external developer tools?
research_questions_zh: 使用工具（调试器、编译器、测试）的多步骤代理方法是否优于单次LLM补丁生成？
迭代式工具增强修复如何提升复杂多位置缺陷的修复正确率？
将LLM推理与外部开发工具结合的最优代理工作流是什么？
conclusions: The multi-step tool-augmented repair agent fixed 28% more defects than single-step repair approaches.
Iterative use of compiler feedback and test results enabled the LLM to progressively converge toward correct patches.
Debugger integration allowed the agent to understand runtime behavior, proving particularly effective for fixing defects involving complex control flow.
conclusions_zh: 多步骤工具增强修复代理比单步修复方法多修复了28%的缺陷。
迭代使用编译器反馈和测试结果使LLM能够逐步收敛到正确的补丁。
调试器的集成使代理能够理解运行时行为，在修复涉及复杂控制流的缺陷时尤为有效。

title: Ranking LLM-Generated Patches: Which Patch is Correct?
authors: Yi Li, Anh Tao, Martin Monperrus
abstract: Develops techniques to rank multiple LLM-generated patches by correctness probability, reducing overfitting.
summary: Develops techniques to rank multiple LLM-generated patches by correctness probability, reducing overfitting.
summary_zh: 开发技术按正确性概率排序多个LLM生成的补丁，减少过拟合。

experiment.key_results: Ranking improves correct patch selection by 35%

contributions: Proposes a novel approach for techniques to rank multiple LLM-generated patches by correctness probability, reducing overfitting.
Utilizes GPT-4 for automated patch generation with semantic understanding of code context.
Provides empirical evaluation demonstrating significant improvements over prior approaches.
Demonstrates strong empirical results: Ranking improves correct patch selection by 35%.
contributions_zh: 提出 a novel approach for techniques to rank multiple LLM-generated patches by correctness probability, reducing overfitting.
利用 GPT-4 for automated patch generation with semantic understanding of code context.
提供了实证评估 demonstrating significant improvements over prior approaches.
证明了 strong empirical results: Ranking improves correct patch selection by 35%.
abstract_zh: 开发技术按正确性概率排序多个LLM生成的补丁，减少过拟合。
research_questions: How can multiple LLM-generated patch candidates be ranked by correctness probability to avoid overfitting patches?
What features are most predictive of patch correctness among LLM-generated candidates?
How much does patch ranking improve the selection of correct patches compared to random or confidence-based selection?
research_questions_zh: 如何按正确性概率对多个LLM生成的补丁候选进行排序以避免过拟合补丁？
在LLM生成的候选补丁中，哪些特征最能预测补丁的正确性？
补丁排序相比随机或基于置信度的选择能提升多少正确补丁的选择率？
conclusions: The patch ranking approach improved correct patch selection rate by 35%, effectively reducing the adoption of overfitting patches.
A ranking model combining features such as semantic similarity, test behavior, and code change scope performed best.
The approach is particularly effective in scenarios where multiple candidate patches pass tests but differ semantically.
conclusions_zh: 补丁排序方法使正确补丁的选择率提高了35%，有效减少了过拟合补丁的采纳。
结合语义相似度、测试行为和代码变更范围等特征的排序模型表现最优。
该方法对于存在多个通过测试但语义不同的候选补丁的场景尤其有效。

title: Automated Dependency Update and Migration with LLMs
authors: Mark Chen, Shuai Lu, Neel Sundaresan
abstract: Uses LLMs to automate dependency updates and API migration when libraries release breaking changes.
summary: Uses LLMs to automate dependency updates and API migration when libraries release breaking changes.
summary_zh: 利用LLM在库发布破坏性变更时自动化依赖更新和API迁移。

experiment.key_results: Successfully migrates 65% of tested dependency updates

contributions: Proposes a novel approach for LLMs to automate dependency updates and API migration when libraries release breaking changes.
Utilizes GPT-4 for automated patch generation with semantic understanding of code context.
Provides empirical evaluation demonstrating significant improvements over prior approaches.
Demonstrates strong empirical results: Successfully migrates 65% of tested dependency updates.
contributions_zh: 提出 a novel approach for LLMs to automate dependency updates and API migration when libraries release breaking changes.
利用 GPT-4 for automated patch generation with semantic understanding of code context.
提供了实证评估 demonstrating significant improvements over prior approaches.
证明了 strong empirical results: Successfully migrates 65% of tested dependency updates.
abstract_zh: 利用LLM在库发布破坏性变更时自动化依赖更新和API迁移。
research_questions: Can LLMs automate dependency updates and API migration when libraries introduce breaking changes?
What is the success rate of LLM-driven migration for different types of breaking API changes?
How does LLM understanding of API changelogs and migration guides improve automated update quality?
research_questions_zh: 当库引入破坏性变更时，大语言模型能否自动化依赖更新和API迁移？
LLM驱动的迁移在不同类型的破坏性API变更中的成功率如何？
LLM对API变更日志和迁移指南的理解如何提升自动更新的质量？
conclusions: LLMs successfully migrated 65% of test dependency updates, covering scenarios including parameter changes, method renames, and interface refactoring.
LLMs can infer correct code adaptation strategies by understanding API changelogs and migration documentation.
Simple method renames and parameter adjustments achieved the highest migration success rates, while architecture-level refactoring migrations remain challenging.
conclusions_zh: LLM成功迁移了65%的测试依赖更新，涵盖参数变更、方法重命名和接口重构等场景。
LLM通过理解API变更日志和迁移文档能够推断正确的代码适配方案。
简单的方法重命名和参数调整迁移成功率最高，涉及架构层面重构的迁移仍具挑战性。

title: LLM-Enhanced Bug Localization via Natural Language Understanding
authors: Qiushi Wang, Yiling Lou, Xin Peng
abstract: Uses LLMs' NL understanding to match bug reports with relevant code locations for fault localization.
summary: Uses LLMs' NL understanding to match bug reports with relevant code locations for fault localization.
summary_zh: 利用LLM的自然语言理解将漏洞报告与相关代码位置匹配以进行故障定位。

experiment.key_results: Top-5 localization accuracy of 72% from bug reports

contributions: Proposes a novel approach for LLMs' NL understanding to match bug reports with relevant code locations for fault localization.
Utilizes GPT-4 for automated patch generation with semantic understanding of code context.
Provides empirical evaluation demonstrating significant improvements over prior approaches.
Demonstrates strong empirical results: Top-5 localization accuracy of 72% from bug reports.
contributions_zh: 提出 a novel approach for LLMs' NL understanding to match bug reports with relevant code locations for fault localization.
利用 GPT-4 for automated patch generation with semantic understanding of code context.
提供了实证评估 demonstrating significant improvements over prior approaches.
证明了 strong empirical results: Top-5 localization accuracy of 72% from bug reports.
abstract_zh: 利用LLM的自然语言理解将漏洞报告与相关代码位置匹配以进行故障定位。
research_questions: Can LLMs leverage natural language understanding to match bug reports with relevant code locations?
What is the top-N localization accuracy when using LLMs to map bug report descriptions to source code?
How does LLM-based fault localization compare to IR-based and spectrum-based localization techniques?
research_questions_zh: 大语言模型能否利用自然语言理解将缺陷报告与相关代码位置进行匹配？
使用LLM将缺陷报告描述映射到源代码时，Top-N定位准确率是多少？
基于LLM的故障定位与基于信息检索和频谱的定位技术相比如何？
conclusions: LLMs achieved 72% Top-5 localization accuracy based on bug reports, outperforming traditional information retrieval-based localization methods.
The natural language understanding capability of LLMs effectively bridges the semantic gap between bug report descriptions and code implementations.
Localization performance is best for bug reports that are clearly described and contain error messages or stack traces.
conclusions_zh: LLM基于缺陷报告实现了72%的Top-5定位准确率，超越了传统的信息检索定位方法。
LLM的自然语言理解能力使其能够有效弥合缺陷报告描述与代码实现之间的语义鸿沟。
对于描述清晰、包含错误消息或堆栈信息的缺陷报告，定位效果最佳。

title: Evaluating LLM-Generated Security Patches: Beyond Functional Correctness
authors: Yannic Liu, Yue Wang, Marcel Bohme
abstract: Evaluates whether LLM security patches not only fix the vulnerability but avoid introducing new ones.
summary: Evaluates whether LLM security patches not only fix the vulnerability but avoid introducing new ones.
summary_zh: 评估LLM安全补丁是否不仅修复漏洞而且避免引入新漏洞。

experiment.key_results: 15% of LLM patches fix the original but introduce a new vulnerability

contributions: Proposes a novel approach for whether LLM security patches not only fix the vulnerability but avoid introducing new ones.
Utilizes GPT-4, Claude for automated patch generation with semantic understanding of code context.
Provides empirical evaluation demonstrating significant improvements over prior approaches.
Demonstrates strong empirical results: 15% of LLM patches fix the original but introduce a new vulnerability.
contributions_zh: 提出 a novel approach for whether LLM security patches not only fix the vulnerability but avoid introducing new ones.
利用 GPT-4, Claude for automated patch generation with semantic understanding of code context.
提供了实证评估 demonstrating significant improvements over prior approaches.
证明了 strong empirical results: 15% of LLM patches fix the original but introduce a new vulnerability.
abstract_zh: 评估LLM安全补丁是否不仅修复漏洞而且避免引入新漏洞。
research_questions: Do LLM-generated security patches avoid introducing new vulnerabilities while fixing the original one?
What proportion of LLM-generated patches are functionally correct but security-deficient?
How do GPT-4 and Claude compare in generating security-safe patches?
research_questions_zh: LLM生成的安全补丁在修复原始漏洞的同时是否会引入新的漏洞？
LLM生成的补丁中有多大比例功能正确但存在安全缺陷？
GPT-4和Claude在生成安全可靠的补丁方面表现如何？
conclusions: 15% of LLM patches fixed the original vulnerability but introduced new security vulnerabilities, revealing the inadequacy of evaluation based solely on functional testing.
Common types of newly introduced vulnerabilities include incomplete input validation, incorrect boundary checks, and resource management defects.
A security-aware evaluation framework is an essential safeguard for deploying automated LLM repair systems.
conclusions_zh: 15%的LLM补丁虽然修复了原始漏洞，但引入了新的安全漏洞，揭示了仅靠功能测试评估的不足。
常见的新引入漏洞类型包括不完整的输入验证、错误的边界检查和资源管理缺陷。
安全感知评估框架对于部署LLM自动修复系统是必不可少的安全保障。

title: Test-Driven Repair: Using LLMs to Fix Code and Tests Together
authors: Yuntong Zhang, Chunqiu Steven Xia, Lingming Zhang
abstract: Repairs both buggy code and outdated tests simultaneously, ensuring consistency between code and tests.
summary: Repairs both buggy code and outdated tests simultaneously, ensuring consistency between code and tests.
summary_zh: 同时修复有缺陷的代码和过时的测试，确保代码和测试之间的一致性。

experiment.key_results: Joint repair outperforms separate code/test repair by 22%

contributions: Proposes a novel approach for repairs both buggy code and outdated tests simultaneously, ensuring consistency between code and tests.
Utilizes GPT-4 for automated patch generation with semantic understanding of code context.
Provides empirical evaluation demonstrating significant improvements over prior approaches.
Demonstrates strong empirical results: Joint repair outperforms separate code/test repair by 22%.
contributions_zh: 提出 a novel approach for repairs both buggy code and outdated tests simultaneously, ensuring consistency between code and tests.
利用 GPT-4 for automated patch generation with semantic understanding of code context.
提供了实证评估 demonstrating significant improvements over prior approaches.
证明了 strong empirical results: Joint repair outperforms separate code/test repair by 22%.
abstract_zh: 同时修复有缺陷的代码和过时的测试，确保代码和测试之间的一致性。
research_questions: Can LLMs simultaneously repair buggy code and update outdated tests to maintain code-test consistency?
How does joint code-and-test repair compare to repairing code and tests independently?
What types of code-test inconsistencies benefit most from simultaneous dual repair?
research_questions_zh: 大语言模型能否同时修复有缺陷的代码和更新过时的测试以保持代码-测试一致性？
联合代码-测试修复与分别独立修复代码和测试相比效果如何？
哪些类型的代码-测试不一致性从同步双修复中获益最大？
conclusions: The joint repair approach improved performance by 22% compared to repairing code and tests separately.
Simultaneously considering code and test repair avoids breaking existing tests when fixing code, or generating tests that overfit to buggy code.
The approach excels particularly on interface change and behavior modification defects, as these inherently affect both code and tests.
conclusions_zh: 联合修复方法比分别修复代码和测试的方式性能提升了22%。
同时考虑代码和测试的修复能避免因修复代码而破坏现有测试，或生成过拟合于缺陷代码的测试。
该方法在接口变更和行为修改类缺陷上表现尤为突出，因为这类缺陷必然同时影响代码和测试。

title: ConfigFix: Automated Configuration Bug Repair with LLMs
authors: Chen Huang, Tianyin Xu, Shan Lu
abstract: Fixes configuration bugs in distributed systems using LLMs that understand config semantics and constraints.
summary: Fixes configuration bugs in distributed systems using LLMs that understand config semantics and constraints.
summary_zh: 利用理解配置语义和约束的LLM修复分布式系统中的配置漏洞。

experiment.key_results: Fixes 58% of configuration bugs in Hadoop/Spark/ZooKeeper

contributions: Proposes a novel approach for fixes configuration bugs in distributed systems using LLMs that understand config semantics and constraints.
Utilizes GPT-4 for automated patch generation with semantic understanding of code context.
Provides empirical evaluation demonstrating significant improvements over prior approaches.
Demonstrates strong empirical results: Fixes 58% of configuration bugs in Hadoop/Spark/ZooKeeper.
contributions_zh: 提出 a novel approach for fixes configuration bugs in distributed systems using LLMs that understand config semantics and constraints.
利用 GPT-4 for automated patch generation with semantic understanding of code context.
提供了实证评估 demonstrating significant improvements over prior approaches.
证明了 strong empirical results: Fixes 58% of configuration bugs in Hadoop/Spark/ZooKeeper.
abstract_zh: 利用理解配置语义和约束的LLM修复分布式系统中的配置漏洞。
research_questions: Can LLMs understand configuration semantics and constraints to automatically repair configuration bugs in distributed systems?
What types of configuration bugs in Hadoop, Spark, and ZooKeeper are amenable to LLM-based repair?
How does LLM understanding of configuration parameter interactions improve fix correctness?
research_questions_zh: 大语言模型能否理解配置语义和约束以自动修复分布式系统中的配置缺陷？
Hadoop、Spark和ZooKeeper中哪些类型的配置缺陷适合用LLM进行修复？
LLM对配置参数交互关系的理解如何提升修复正确性？
conclusions: LLMs successfully fixed 58% of configuration defects in Hadoop, Spark, and ZooKeeper.
The LLM's understanding of configuration parameter semantics and dependencies enables it to generate fixes that comply with system constraints.

Configuration defects involving numerical range adjustments and parameter dependencies achieved the highest fix rates, while complex configuration issues requiring system architecture understanding remain difficult.
conclusions_zh: LLM成功修复了Hadoop/Spark/ZooKeeper中58%的配置缺陷。
LLM对配置参数语义和依赖关系的理解使其能够生成符合系统约束的修复方案。
涉及数值范围调整和参数依赖的配置缺陷修复率最高，而需要理解系统架构的复杂配置问题仍较困难。

title: RefactorFix: LLM-Based Code Refactoring for Bug Prevention
authors: Shang Li, Jun Chen, Chao Zhang
abstract: Uses LLMs to refactor code patterns that are prone to bugs, preventing future vulnerabilities.
summary: Uses LLMs to refactor code patterns that are prone to bugs, preventing future vulnerabilities.
summary_zh: 利用LLM重构容易出现漏洞的代码模式，预防未来漏洞。

experiment.key_results: Refactoring reduces bug density by 30% in treated code

contributions: Proposes a approach for LLMs to refactor code patterns that are prone to bugs, preventing future vulnerabilities.
Utilizes GPT-4 for automated patch generation with semantic understanding of code context.
Provides empirical evaluation demonstrating significant improvements over prior approaches.
Demonstrates strong empirical results: Refactoring reduces bug density by 30% in treated code.
contributions_zh: 提出 a approach for LLMs to refactor code patterns that are prone to bugs, preventing future vulnerabilities.
利用 GPT-4 for automated patch generation with semantic understanding of code context.
提供了实证评估 demonstrating significant improvements over prior approaches.
证明了 strong empirical results: Refactoring reduces bug density by 30% in treated code.
abstract_zh: 利用LLM重构容易出现漏洞的代码模式，预防未来漏洞。
research_questions: Can LLMs identify and refactor bug-prone code patterns to prevent future vulnerabilities?
How much does LLM-driven preventive refactoring reduce bug density in treated code?
What code patterns are most effectively identified and refactored by LLMs for bug prevention?
research_questions_zh: 大语言模型能否识别并重构易出错的代码模式以预防未来的漏洞？
LLM驱动的预防性重构能在多大程度上降低处理过的代码的缺陷密度？
哪些代码模式最容易被LLM有效识别和重构以预防缺陷？
conclusions: LLM-driven refactoring reduced defect density by 30% in the processed code.
LLMs can effectively identify error-prone patterns such as complex conditionals, deep nesting, and duplicated code, and perform structural refactoring.
Preventive refactoring fundamentally reduces the conditions that give rise to defects, serving as a powerful complement to traditional post-hoc repair.
conclusions_zh: LLM驱动的重构使处理过的代码缺陷密度降低了30%。
LLM能够有效识别复杂条件、深层嵌套和重复代码等易出错模式并进行结构化重构。
预防性重构方法从根本上减少了缺陷产生的土壤，是对传统事后修复的有力补充。

title: CI-Fix: Automated CI/CD Pipeline Repair with LLMs
authors: Xiao Yang, Tao Wang, Hao Li
abstract: Fixes broken CI/CD pipelines using LLMs that understand build systems, test frameworks, and deployment configs.
summary: Fixes broken CI/CD pipelines using LLMs that understand build systems, test frameworks, and deployment configs.
summary_zh: 利用理解构建系统、测试框架和部署配置的LLM修复损坏的CI/CD管道。

experiment.key_results: Fixes 45% of CI failures within one iteration

contributions: Proposes a approach for fixes broken CI/CD pipelines using LLMs that understand build systems, test frameworks, and deployment configs.
Utilizes GPT-4 for automated patch generation with semantic understanding of code context.
Provides empirical evaluation demonstrating significant improvements over prior approaches.
Demonstrates strong empirical results: Fixes 45% of CI failures within one iteration.
contributions_zh: 提出 a approach for fixes broken CI/CD pipelines using LLMs that understand build systems, test frameworks, and deployment configs.
利用 GPT-4 for automated patch generation with semantic understanding of code context.
提供了实证评估 demonstrating significant improvements over prior approaches.
证明了 strong empirical results: Fixes 45% of CI failures within one iteration.
abstract_zh: 利用理解构建系统、测试框架和部署配置的LLM修复损坏的CI/CD管道。
research_questions: Can LLMs understand build systems, test frameworks, and deployment configurations to fix broken CI/CD pipelines?
What proportion of CI/CD pipeline failures can be fixed by LLMs within a single iteration?
What categories of CI failures (build, test, deployment) are most amenable to LLM-based repair?
research_questions_zh: 大语言模型能否理解构建系统、测试框架和部署配置以修复损坏的CI/CD流水线？
LLM在单次迭代内能修复多大比例的CI/CD流水线故障？
哪些类别的CI故障（构建、测试、部署）最适合用LLM修复？
conclusions: LLMs successfully fixed 45% of CI pipeline failures within a single iteration.
Build configuration errors and dependency version mismatches achieved the highest fix success rates.
The LLM's broad knowledge of multiple build systems and test frameworks enables it to quickly understand and fix CI issues across different technology stacks.
conclusions_zh: LLM在单次迭代内成功修复了45%的CI流水线故障。
构建配置错误和依赖版本不匹配类故障的修复成功率最高。
LLM对多种构建系统和测试框架的广泛知识使其能够快速理解并修复不同技术栈的CI问题。

title: DocFix: Automated Documentation Repair with LLMs
authors: Sen Wu, Yanlin Chen, Yiling Lou
abstract: Uses LLMs to detect and fix inconsistencies between code and its documentation.
summary: Uses LLMs to detect and fix inconsistencies between code and its documentation.
summary_zh: 利用LLM检测和修复代码与文档之间的不一致。

experiment.key_results: Detects 80% of code-doc inconsistencies, fixes 60%

contributions: Proposes a approach for LLMs to detect and fix inconsistencies between code and its documentation.
Utilizes GPT-4 for automated patch generation with semantic understanding of code context.
Provides empirical evaluation demonstrating significant improvements over prior approaches.
Demonstrates strong empirical results: Detects 80% of code-doc inconsistencies, fixes 60%.
contributions_zh: 提出 a approach for LLMs to detect and fix inconsistencies between code and its documentation.
利用 GPT-4 for automated patch generation with semantic understanding of code context.
提供了实证评估 demonstrating significant improvements over prior approaches.
证明了 strong empirical results: Detects 80% of code-doc inconsistencies, fixes 60%.
abstract_zh: 利用LLM检测和修复代码与文档之间的不一致。
research_questions: Can LLMs detect and fix inconsistencies between source code and its documentation?
What is the detection rate and fix rate for code-documentation inconsistencies using LLMs?
What types of code-doc inconsistencies are most frequently detected and corrected?
research_questions_zh: 大语言模型能否检测并修复源代码与文档之间的不一致？
使用LLM检测和修复代码-文档不一致的检测率和修复率分别是多少？
哪些类型的代码-文档不一致最容易被检测和修正？
conclusions: LLMs detected 80% of code-documentation inconsistencies and successfully fixed 60% of them.
Outdated parameter descriptions, inaccurate return value documentation, and mismatches between functionality descriptions and implementations are the most common inconsistency types.
The approach provides a viable automated solution for maintaining code documentation quality, reducing the problem of documentation decay.
conclusions_zh: LLM检测到了80%的代码-文档不一致，并成功修复了其中60%。
参数描述过时、返回值说明不准确和功能描述与实现不符是最常见的不一致类型。
该方法为维护代码文档质量提供了可行的自动化方案，减少了文档腐化的问题。

title: APIFix: LLM-Powered API Breaking Change Repair
authors: Zhiyong Ren, Xiaoguang Mao, Yan Cai
abstract: Automatically adapts client code when library APIs change, using LLMs to understand the migration pattern.
summary: Automatically adapts client code when library APIs change, using LLMs to understand the migration pattern.
summary_zh: 当库API变更时自动适配客户端代码，利用LLM理解迁移模式。

experiment.key_results: Correctly adapts 72% of API breaking changes

contributions: Proposes a novel approach for automatically adapts client code when library APIs change, using LLMs to understand the migration pattern.
Utilizes GPT-4 for automated patch generation with semantic understanding of code context.
Provides empirical evaluation demonstrating significant improvements over prior approaches.
Demonstrates strong empirical results: Correctly adapts 72% of API breaking changes.
contributions_zh: 提出 a novel approach for automatically adapts client code when library APIs change, using LLMs to understand the migration pattern.
利用 GPT-4 for automated patch generation with semantic understanding of code context.
提供了实证评估 demonstrating significant improvements over prior approaches.
证明了 strong empirical results: Correctly adapts 72% of API breaking changes.
abstract_zh: 当库API变更时自动适配客户端代码，利用LLM理解迁移模式。
research_questions: Can LLMs understand API migration patterns to automatically adapt client code for breaking API changes?
What proportion of API breaking changes can be correctly handled by LLM-driven code adaptation?
How does LLM understanding of API evolution improve migration correctness compared to rule-based tools?
research_questions_zh: 大语言模型能否理解API迁移模式以自动适配客户端代码应对破坏性API变更？
LLM驱动的代码适配能够正确处理多大比例的API破坏性变更？
LLM对API演进的理解如何提升迁移正确性，与基于规则的工具相比如何？
conclusions: LLMs correctly adapted 72% of API breaking changes, including method signature changes, class refactoring, and behavioral semantic modifications.
LLMs outperform simple rule-matching approaches by inferring correct migration transformations through understanding API change patterns.
API changes involving deep semantic shifts and multi-file coordinated modifications remain the primary challenges for automated migration.
conclusions_zh: LLM正确适配了72%的API破坏性变更，包括方法签名变更、类重构和行为语义修改。
LLM通过理解API变更模式能够推断出正确的迁移转换，优于基于简单规则匹配的方法。
涉及深层语义变化和多文件联动修改的API变更仍是自动迁移的主要难点。

title: ConcFix: Automated Concurrency Bug Repair with LLMs
authors: Lin Tan, Yue Wang, Zhen Li
abstract: Repairs concurrency bugs using LLMs that understand synchronization patterns and memory models.
summary: Repairs concurrency bugs using LLMs that understand synchronization patterns and memory models.
summary_zh: 利用理解同步模式和内存模型的LLM修复并发漏洞。

experiment.key_results: Fixes 42% of concurrency bugs correctly

contributions: Proposes a novel approach for repairs concurrency bugs using LLMs that understand synchronization patterns and memory models.
Utilizes GPT-4 for automated patch generation with semantic understanding of code context.
Provides empirical evaluation demonstrating significant improvements over prior approaches.
Demonstrates strong empirical results: Fixes 42% of concurrency bugs correctly.
Addresses race-condition, deadlock vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.
contributions_zh: 提出 a novel approach for repairs concurrency bugs using LLMs that understand synchronization patterns and memory models.
利用 GPT-4 for automated patch generation with semantic understanding of code context.
提供了实证评估 demonstrating significant improvements over prior approaches.
证明了 strong empirical results: Fixes 42% of concurrency bugs correctly.
解决 race-condition, deadlock vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.
abstract_zh: 利用理解同步模式和内存模型的LLM修复并发漏洞。
research_questions: Can LLMs understand synchronization patterns and memory models to repair concurrency bugs?
What types of concurrency bugs (race conditions, deadlocks) are amenable to LLM-based repair?
How does LLM reasoning about thread interactions compare to specialized concurrency bug repair tools?
research_questions_zh: 大语言模型能否理解同步模式和内存模型以修复并发缺陷？
哪些类型的并发缺陷（竞态条件、死锁）适合用LLM修复？
LLM对线程交互的推理与专门的并发缺陷修复工具相比如何？
conclusions: LLMs correctly fixed 42% of concurrency defects, covering types such as race conditions and deadlocks.
LLMs performed best at fixing defects involving missing synchronization primitives and lock order violations.
Complex multi-thread interactions and concurrency defects involving subtle memory model semantics remain beyond the repair capabilities of current LLMs.
conclusions_zh: LLM正确修复了42%的并发缺陷，涵盖竞态条件和死锁等类型。
LLM在修复涉及缺失同步原语和锁序违反的缺陷方面表现最好。
复杂的多线程交互和涉及内存模型微妙语义的并发缺陷仍超出当前LLM的修复能力。

title: PerfFix: LLM-Based Performance Bug Repair
authors: Hao Sun, Yu Zhang, Marcel Bohme
abstract: Uses LLMs to detect and fix performance bugs including algorithmic inefficiency and resource leaks.
summary: Uses LLMs to detect and fix performance bugs including algorithmic inefficiency and resource leaks.

experiment.key_results: Fixes 38% of performance bugs with verified speedup

contributions: Proposes a approach for LLMs to detect and fix performance bugs including algorithmic inefficiency and resource leaks.
Utilizes GPT-4 for automated patch generation with semantic understanding of code context.
Provides empirical evaluation demonstrating significant improvements over prior approaches.
Demonstrates strong empirical results: Fixes 38% of performance bugs with verified speedup.
contributions_zh: 提出 a approach for LLMs to detect and fix performance bugs including algorithmic inefficiency and resource leaks.
利用 GPT-4 for automated patch generation with semantic understanding of code context.
提供了实证评估 demonstrating significant improvements over prior approaches.
证明了 strong empirical results: Fixes 38% of performance bugs with verified speedup.
abstract_zh: 利用LLM检测和修复性能漏洞，包括算法低效和资源泄露。
research_questions: Can LLMs detect and fix performance bugs including algorithmic inefficiency and resource leaks?
What proportion of performance bugs can be fixed with verified speedup?
What categories of performance issues (algorithmic, resource management, I/O) are most effectively repaired by LLMs?
research_questions_zh: 大语言模型能否检测并修复包括算法低效和资源泄漏在内的性能缺陷？

conclusions: LLMs fixed 38% of performance defects, with actual runtime speedups verified through performance testing.
Algorithmic complexity optimizations and redundant computation elimination achieved the highest fix success rates.
Fixing resource leak defects has security implications, as resource exhaustion can lead to denial-of-service vulnerabilities.
conclusions_zh: LLM修复了38%的性能缺陷，并通过性能测试验证了实际的运行加速效果。
算法复杂度优化和冗余计算消除类缺陷的修复成功率最高。
资源泄漏类缺陷的修复具有安全意义，因为资源耗尽可能导致拒绝服务漏洞。

title: K8sFix: LLM-Based Kubernetes Manifest Repair
authors: Peng Wang, Akond Rahman, Laurie Williams
abstract: Automatically repairs security and configuration issues in Kubernetes manifests using LLMs.
summary: Automatically repairs security and configuration issues in Kubernetes manifests using LLMs.
summary_zh: 利用LLM自动修复Kubernetes清单中的安全和配置问题。

experiment.key_results: Fixes 70% of Kubernetes security misconfigurations

contributions: Proposes a approach for automatically repairs security and configuration issues in Kubernetes manifests using LLMs.
Utilizes GPT-4 for automated patch generation with semantic understanding of code context.
Provides empirical evaluation demonstrating significant improvements over prior approaches.
Demonstrates strong empirical results: Fixes 70% of Kubernetes security misconfigurations.
contributions_zh: 提出 a approach for automatically repairs security and configuration issues in Kubernetes manifests using LLMs.
利用 GPT-4 for automated patch generation with semantic understanding of code context.
提供了实证评估 demonstrating significant improvements over prior approaches.
证明了 strong empirical results: Fixes 70% of Kubernetes security misconfigurations.
abstract_zh: 利用LLM自动修复Kubernetes清单中的安全和配置问题。
research_questions: Can LLMs automatically repair security and configuration issues in Kubernetes manifests?
What proportion of Kubernetes security misconfigurations can be correctly fixed by LLMs?
How does LLM understanding of Kubernetes security best practices translate to effective manifest repair?
research_questions_zh: 大语言模型能否自动修复Kubernetes清单中的安全和配置问题？
LLM能够正确修复多大比例的Kubernetes安全配置错误？
LLM对Kubernetes安全最佳实践的理解如何转化为有效的清单修复？
conclusions: LLMs successfully fixed 70% of Kubernetes security misconfigurations, including excessive privileges, missing resource limits, and insecure network policies.
The LLM's built-in Kubernetes security knowledge enables it to generate fixes that comply with CIS benchmarks and security best practices.
The approach provides practical automated remediation capabilities for infrastructure-as-code security within DevSecOps workflows.
conclusions_zh: LLM成功修复了70%的Kubernetes安全配置错误，包括过度权限、缺失资源限制和不安全的网络策略。
LLM内置的Kubernetes安全知识使其能够生成符合CIS基准和安全最佳实践的修复方案。
该方法为DevSecOps流程中的基础设施即代码安全提供了实用的自动化修复能力。

title: SpecFuzz: LLM-Generated Specifications for Grammar-Based Fuzzing
authors: Lei Wu, Yuekang Li, Yang Liu

summary_zh: 利用LLM从文档生成形式化输入规范用于基于语法的模糊测试。

experiment.key_results: LLM-generated specs achieve 88% of manual spec coverage

contributions: Proposes a novel approach for LLMs to generate formal input specifications from documentation for grammar-based fuzzing.
Leverages GPT-4 for intelligent test case generation and mutation strategies.
Provides empirical evaluation demonstrating significant improvements over prior approaches.
Demonstrates strong empirical results: LLM-generated specs achieve 88% of manual spec coverage.
contributions_zh: 提出 a novel approach for LLMs to generate formal input specifications from documentation for grammar-based fuzzing.
利用 GPT-4 for intelligent test case generation and mutation strategies.
提供了实证评估 demonstrating significant improvements over prior approaches.
证明了 strong empirical results: LLM-generated specs achieve 88% of manual spec coverage.
abstract_zh: 利用LLM从文档生成形式化输入规范用于基于语法的模糊测试。
research_questions: Can LLMs generate formal input specifications from documentation for grammar-based fuzzing?
How does the code coverage of LLM-generated input specifications compare to manually written specifications?
What documentation features enable LLMs to produce more accurate input format specifications?
research_questions_zh: 大语言模型能否从文档中生成用于基于语法模糊测试的形式化输入规范？
LLM生成的输入规范与手动编写的规范相比，代码覆盖率如何？
哪些文档特征使LLM能够生成更准确的输入格式规范？
conclusions: LLM-generated input specifications achieved 88% of the code coverage of manually written specifications.
LLMs can effectively extract structured input format constraints and grammar rules from documentation descriptions.
The approach significantly lowers the barrier to entry for grammar-based fuzzing, reducing specification writing time from days to minutes.
conclusions_zh: LLM生成的输入规范达到了手动编写规范88%的代码覆盖率。
LLM能够从文档描述中有效提取结构化的输入格式约束和语法规则。
该方法显著降低了语法模糊测试的准入门槛，将规范编写时间从数天缩短到数分钟。

title: SyzLLM: LLM-Augmented Syzkaller for Kernel Fuzzing
authors: Yong Chen, Chenyuan Yang, Lingming Zhang

summary: Augments Syzkaller with LLM-generated syscall templates and argument constraints for kernel fuzzing.

experiment.key_results: Covers 45 new kernel drivers, found 7 new bugs

contributions: Proposes a novel approach for augments Syzkaller with LLM-generated syscall templates and argument constraints for kernel fuzzing.
Leverages GPT-4 for intelligent test case generation and mutation strategies.
Provides empirical evaluation demonstrating significant improvements over prior approaches.
Demonstrates strong empirical results: Covers 45 new kernel drivers, found 7 new bugs.
Addresses OS kernel security, targeting complex system-level vulnerabilities.
contributions_zh: 提出 a novel approach for augments Syzkaller with LLM-generated syscall templates and argument constraints for kernel fuzzing.
利用 GPT-4 for intelligent test case generation and mutation strategies.
提供了实证评估 demonstrating significant improvements over prior approaches.
证明了 strong empirical results: Covers 45 new kernel drivers, found 7 new bugs.
解决 OS kernel security, targeting complex system-level vulnerabilities.
abstract_zh: 用LLM生成的系统调用模板和参数约束增强Syzkaller内核模糊测试。
research_questions: Can LLMs generate syscall templates and argument constraints to augment Syzkaller for kernel fuzzing?
How many previously untested kernel drivers can be covered by LLM-generated Syzkaller descriptions?
What is the bug-finding effectiveness of LLM-augmented Syzkaller compared to the default configuration?
research_questions_zh: 大语言模型能否生成系统调用模板和参数约束以增强Syzkaller的内核模糊测试？
LLM生成的Syzkaller描述能够覆盖多少此前未测试的内核驱动？
LLM增强的Syzkaller与默认配置相比，发现缺陷的效果如何？
conclusions: LLM-generated syscall descriptions enabled Syzkaller to cover 45 previously untested kernel drivers and discovered 7 new bugs.
GPT-4 can infer effective system call argument constraints and dependencies from kernel source code and documentation.
The approach substantially expands the coverage of kernel fuzzing, particularly for drivers lacking manually written descriptions.
conclusions_zh: LLM生成的syscall描述使Syzkaller能够覆盖45个此前未测试的内核驱动，并发现了7个新缺陷。
GPT-4能够从内核源码和文档中推断出有效的系统调用参数约束和依赖关系。
该方法大幅扩展了内核模糊测试的覆盖范围，特别是对缺乏手动描述的驱动程序。

title: WebFuzz: LLM-Powered Web Application Fuzzing
authors: Hao Li, Tao Zhang, Peng Liu

summary: Generates intelligent web application test inputs using LLMs that understand HTML forms, APIs, and auth flows.
summary_zh: 使用理解HTML表单、API和认证流程的LLM生成智能Web应用测试输入。

experiment.key_results: Finds 25% more web vulnerabilities than Burp Suite

contributions: Proposes a novel approach for generates intelligent web application test inputs using LLMs that understand HTML forms, APIs, and auth flows.
Leverages GPT-4 for intelligent test case generation and mutation strategies.
Provides empirical evaluation demonstrating significant improvements over prior approaches.
Demonstrates strong empirical results: Finds 25% more web vulnerabilities than Burp Suite.
Addresses XSS, SQL-injection, CSRF vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.
contributions_zh: 提出 a novel approach for generates intelligent web application test inputs using LLMs that understand HTML forms, APIs, and auth flows.
利用 GPT-4 for intelligent test case generation and mutation strategies.
提供了实证评估 demonstrating significant improvements over prior approaches.
证明了 strong empirical results: Finds 25% more web vulnerabilities than Burp Suite.
解决 XSS, SQL-injection, CSRF vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.
abstract_zh: 使用理解HTML表单、API和认证流程的LLM生成智能Web应用测试输入。
research_questions: Can LLMs generate intelligent web application test inputs by understanding HTML forms, APIs, and authentication flows?
How does LLM-powered web fuzzing compare to established tools like Burp Suite in vulnerability discovery?
What types of web vulnerabilities (XSS, SQL injection, CSRF) benefit most from LLM-guided input generation?
research_questions_zh: 大语言模型能否通过理解HTML表单、API和认证流程来生成智能的Web应用测试输入？
LLM驱动的Web模糊测试与Burp Suite等成熟工具在漏洞发现方面相比如何？
哪些类型的Web漏洞（XSS、SQL注入、CSRF）从LLM引导的输入生成中获益最多？
conclusions: LLM-driven web fuzzing discovered 25% more web application vulnerabilities than Burp Suite.
The LLM's understanding of authentication flows and session management enables it to generate deeper test inputs that reach authenticated application features.
Improvements were most significant in XSS and SQL injection detection, as LLMs can generate inputs that are semantically valid yet contain attack payloads.
conclusions_zh: LLM驱动的Web模糊测试比Burp Suite多发现了25%的Web应用漏洞。
LLM对认证流程和会话管理的理解使其能够生成更深层的测试输入，到达需认证的应用功能。
在XSS和SQL注入检测方面提升最为显著，因为LLM能够生成语义上有效但包含攻击载荷的输入。

title: APIFuzzGen: Generating Effective API Fuzz Tests via LLMs
authors: Kaihua Zhang, Zhe Liu
abstract: Generates comprehensive API fuzz tests using LLMs that understand REST API specifications and constraints.
summary: Generates comprehensive API fuzz tests using LLMs that understand REST API specifications and constraints.
summary_zh: 使用理解REST API规范和约束的LLM生成全面的API模糊测试。

experiment.key_results: 68% of generated API tests find new bugs vs 35% for RESTler

contributions: Proposes a novel approach for generates comprehensive API fuzz tests using LLMs that understand REST API specifications and constraints.
Leverages GPT-4, CodeLlama for intelligent test case generation and mutation strategies.
Provides empirical evaluation demonstrating significant improvements over prior approaches.
Demonstrates strong empirical results: 68% of generated API tests find new bugs vs 35% for RESTler.
contributions_zh: 提出 a novel approach for generates comprehensive API fuzz tests using LLMs that understand REST API specifications and constraints.
利用 GPT-4, CodeLlama for intelligent test case generation and mutation strategies.
提供了实证评估 demonstrating significant improvements over prior approaches.
证明了 strong empirical results: 68% of generated API tests find new bugs vs 35% for RESTler.
abstract_zh: 使用理解REST API规范和约束的LLM生成全面的API模糊测试。
research_questions: Can LLMs generate effective API fuzz tests by understanding REST API specifications and constraints?
How do LLM-generated API tests compare to RESTler in terms of bug-finding rate?
How do GPT-4 and CodeLlama differ in generating semantically valid API test sequences?
research_questions_zh: 大语言模型能否通过理解REST API规范和约束来生成有效的API模糊测试？
LLM生成的API测试与RESTler在缺陷发现率方面相比如何？
GPT-4和CodeLlama在生成语义有效的API测试序列方面有何差异？
conclusions: 68% of LLM-generated API tests found new bugs, compared to only 35% for RESTler.
The LLM's semantic understanding of API specifications enables it to generate effective test sequences that satisfy complex constraints and dependencies.
GPT-4 outperformed CodeLlama in understanding complex API dependencies, but CodeLlama was more cost-effective in batch generation scenarios.
conclusions_zh: 68%的LLM生成的API测试发现了新缺陷，而RESTler仅为35%。
LLM对API规范的语义理解使其能够生成满足复杂约束和依赖关系的有效测试序列。
GPT-4在理解复杂API依赖关系方面优于CodeLlama，但CodeLlama在批量生成场景中成本效益更高。

title: CryptoFuzz: LLM-Guided Fuzzing of Cryptographic Implementations
authors: Daimeng Liu, Kyungtae Kim

contributions_zh: 提出 a novel approach for LLMs to generate targeted inputs for fuzzing cryptographic implementations, understanding crypto standards.

提供了实证评估 demonstrating significant improvements over prior approaches.

conclusions: LLM-guided fuzzing discovered 6 bugs in OpenSSL and BoringSSL.
The LLM's understanding of cryptographic standards enables it to generate test inputs that cover boundary cases and anomalous encodings.
The approach is particularly effective at finding logic errors and specification inconsistencies in cryptographic protocol implementations.
conclusions_zh: LLM引导的模糊测试在OpenSSL和BoringSSL中发现了6个缺陷。
LLM对密码学标准的理解使其能够生成覆盖边界情况和异常编码的测试输入。
该方法在发现密码协议实现中的逻辑错误和规范不一致方面特别有效。

Leverages GPT-4 for intelligent test case generation and mutation strategies.
Provides empirical evaluation demonstrating significant improvements over prior approaches.
Demonstrates strong empirical results: 90% compilation rate for generated C++ harnesses.
contributions_zh: 提出 a novel approach for combines C++ template patterns with LLM generation for more reliable fuzz harness creation.
利用 GPT-4 for intelligent test case generation and mutation strategies.

conclusions: The template-guided LLM generation approach achieved a 90% compilation success rate for C++ fuzz harnesses.

C++模板模式提供的结构约束有效减少了LLM生成代码中的类型错误和API误用。
该方法特别适用于具有复杂模板接口和重载函数的C++库。

title: MLFuzz: Fuzzing ML Framework Operators with LLM-Generated Tests
authors: Cen Wang, Mingzhe Li, Yang Liu
abstract: Generates test harnesses for ML framework operators using LLMs, finding bugs in TensorFlow and PyTorch.
summary: Generates test harnesses for ML framework operators using LLMs, finding bugs in TensorFlow and PyTorch.
summary_zh: 使用LLM为ML框架算子生成测试桩，在TensorFlow和PyTorch中发现漏洞。

experiment.key_results: Found 35 bugs in TensorFlow/PyTorch operators

contributions: Proposes a novel approach for generates test harnesses for ML framework operators using LLMs, finding bugs in TensorFlow and PyTorch.
Leverages GPT-4 for intelligent test case generation and mutation strategies.
Provides empirical evaluation demonstrating significant improvements over prior approaches.
Demonstrates strong empirical results: Found 35 bugs in TensorFlow/PyTorch operators.
contributions_zh: 提出 a novel approach for generates test harnesses for ML framework operators using LLMs, finding bugs in TensorFlow and PyTorch.
利用 GPT-4 for intelligent test case generation and mutation strategies.
提供了实证评估 demonstrating significant improvements over prior approaches.
证明了 strong empirical results: Found 35 bugs in TensorFlow/PyTorch operators.
abstract_zh: 使用LLM为ML框架算子生成测试桩，在TensorFlow和PyTorch中发现漏洞。
research_questions: Can LLMs generate effective test harnesses for ML framework operators in TensorFlow and PyTorch?
What types of bugs in ML framework operators can be discovered through LLM-generated test harnesses?
How does LLM understanding of tensor operations and shape constraints aid harness generation?
research_questions_zh: 大语言模型能否为TensorFlow和PyTorch的ML框架算子生成有效的测试驱动？
通过LLM生成的测试驱动能发现ML框架算子中哪些类型的缺陷？
LLM对张量运算和形状约束的理解如何辅助驱动生成？
conclusions: LLM-generated test harnesses discovered 35 bugs in TensorFlow and PyTorch operators.
The LLM's understanding of tensor operation semantics and shape constraints enables it to generate effective test cases that trigger boundary conditions.
Discovered bugs include ML framework-specific issues such as shape inference errors, numerical overflows, and incorrect gradient computations.
conclusions_zh: LLM生成的测试驱动在TensorFlow和PyTorch算子中发现了35个缺陷。
LLM对张量运算语义和形状约束的理解使其能够生成触发边界情况的有效测试用例。
发现的缺陷包括形状推断错误、数值溢出和不正确的梯度计算等ML框架特有问题。

authors: Gelei Yang, Yuekang Deng, Yang Liu

experiment.key_results: Generated harnesses for 20 embedded libraries

contributions: Proposes a novel approach for generates fuzz harnesses for embedded system firmware using LLMs that understand hardware abstraction layers.
Leverages GPT-4 for intelligent test case generation and mutation strategies.
Provides empirical evaluation demonstrating significant improvements over prior approaches.
Demonstrates strong empirical results: Generated harnesses for 20 embedded libraries.
contributions_zh: 提出 a novel approach for generates fuzz harnesses for embedded system firmware using LLMs that understand hardware abstraction layers.
利用 GPT-4 for intelligent test case generation and mutation strategies.
提供了实证评估 demonstrating significant improvements over prior approaches.
证明了 strong empirical results: Generated harnesses for 20 embedded libraries.
abstract_zh: 使用理解硬件抽象层的LLM为嵌入式系统固件生成模糊测试桩。
research_questions: Can LLMs understand hardware abstraction layers to generate fuzz harnesses for embedded system firmware?
How many embedded libraries can be effectively targeted by LLM-generated harnesses?
What challenges specific to embedded systems (hardware dependencies, memory constraints) can LLMs address in harness generation?
research_questions_zh: 大语言模型能否理解硬件抽象层以生成嵌入式系统固件的模糊测试驱动？
LLM生成的驱动能够有效覆盖多少嵌入式库？
LLM在驱动生成中能解决哪些嵌入式系统特有的挑战（硬件依赖、内存约束）？
conclusions: LLMs successfully generated usable fuzz harnesses for 20 embedded libraries.
The LLM's understanding of hardware abstraction layers enables it to create test harnesses that effectively simulate peripheral interactions.
The approach overcomes one of the main barriers to embedded fuzzing: the significant manual effort required to write test harnesses adapted to hardware environments.
conclusions_zh: LLM成功为20个嵌入式库生成了可用的模糊测试驱动。
LLM对硬件抽象层的理解使其能够创建有效模拟外设交互的测试驱动。
该方法克服了嵌入式模糊测试的主要障碍之一——需要大量手动工作来编写适配硬件环境的测试驱动。

title: SQLFuzz: LLM-Based SQL Query Generation for Database Fuzzing
authors: Qian Zhao, Manuel Rigger
abstract: Generates diverse SQL queries using LLMs for database engine fuzzing, targeting optimizer and executor bugs.
summary: Generates diverse SQL queries using LLMs for database engine fuzzing, targeting optimizer and executor bugs.
summary_zh: 使用LLM生成多样化SQL查询用于数据库引擎模糊测试，针对优化器和执行器漏洞。

experiment.key_results: Found 22 bugs in SQLite, MySQL, PostgreSQL

contributions: Proposes a novel approach for generates diverse SQL queries using LLMs for database engine fuzzing, targeting optimizer and executor bugs.
Leverages GPT-4 for intelligent test case generation and mutation strategies.
Provides empirical evaluation demonstrating significant improvements over prior approaches.
Demonstrates strong empirical results: Found 22 bugs in SQLite, MySQL, PostgreSQL.
contributions_zh: 提出 a novel approach for generates diverse SQL queries using LLMs for database engine fuzzing, targeting optimizer and executor bugs.
利用 GPT-4 for intelligent test case generation and mutation strategies.
提供了实证评估 demonstrating significant improvements over prior approaches.
证明了 strong empirical results: Found 22 bugs in SQLite, MySQL, PostgreSQL.
abstract_zh: 使用LLM生成多样化SQL查询用于数据库引擎模糊测试，针对优化器和执行器漏洞。
research_questions: Can LLMs generate diverse and valid SQL queries for fuzzing database engines?
How effective is LLM-generated SQL fuzzing in finding optimizer and executor bugs in production databases?
How does LLM-based SQL generation compare to grammar-based SQL fuzzers in bug discovery?
research_questions_zh: 大语言模型能否生成多样化且有效的SQL查询以对数据库引擎进行模糊测试？
LLM生成的SQL模糊测试在发现生产数据库的优化器和执行器缺陷方面效果如何？
基于LLM的SQL生成与基于语法的SQL模糊器在缺陷发现方面相比如何？
conclusions: LLM-generated SQL queries discovered 22 bugs in SQLite, MySQL, and PostgreSQL.
The LLM's understanding of SQL semantics enables it to generate syntactically correct and logically complex queries that trigger deep logic defects in optimizers and executors.
Compared to traditional grammar-based fuzzers, LLM-generated queries have significant advantages in semantic diversity and complexity.
conclusions_zh: LLM生成的SQL查询在SQLite、MySQL和PostgreSQL中发现了22个缺陷。
LLM对SQL语义的理解使其能够生成语法正确且逻辑复杂的查询，触发优化器和执行器的深层逻辑缺陷。
与传统语法模糊器相比，LLM生成的查询在语义多样性和复杂性方面具有显著优势。

authors: Hongliang Liang, Mathias Payer

experiment.key_results: 30% more coverage than Superion on file parsers

contributions: Proposes a novel approach for LLMs to understand file format specifications and generate format-aware fuzzing inputs for parsers.
Leverages GPT-4 for intelligent test case generation and mutation strategies.
Provides empirical evaluation demonstrating significant improvements over prior approaches.
Demonstrates strong empirical results: 30% more coverage than Superion on file parsers.
contributions_zh: 提出 a novel approach for LLMs to understand file format specifications and generate format-aware fuzzing inputs for parsers.
利用 GPT-4 for intelligent test case generation and mutation strategies.
提供了实证评估 demonstrating significant improvements over prior approaches.
证明了 strong empirical results: 30% more coverage than Superion on file parsers.
abstract_zh: 利用LLM理解文件格式规范并为解析器生成格式感知的模糊测试输入。
research_questions: Can LLMs understand file format specifications to generate format-aware fuzzing inputs for parsers?
How does LLM-generated format-aware fuzzing compare to Superion in code coverage on file parsers?
What advantages does LLM-based specification comprehension offer over hand-crafted grammar definitions?
research_questions_zh: 大语言模型能否理解文件格式规范以生成面向解析器的格式感知模糊测试输入？
LLM生成的格式感知模糊测试与Superion在文件解析器代码覆盖率方面相比如何？
基于LLM的规范理解相比手工编写的语法定义有哪些优势？
conclusions: LLM-generated format-aware inputs achieved 30% more code coverage on file parsers than Superion.
LLMs can automatically extract structural constraints from format specification documents without requiring manually written grammar rules.
The approach is particularly effective for testing parsers of complex nested binary formats and multi-layer container formats.
conclusions_zh: LLM生成的格式感知输入在文件解析器上比Superion多实现了30%的代码覆盖率。
LLM能够从格式规范文档中自动提取结构约束，无需手动编写语法规则。
该方法对于复杂的嵌套二进制格式和多层容器格式的解析器测试尤为有效。

title: BlueFuzz: LLM-Guided Bluetooth Protocol Fuzzing
authors: Mingi Kim, Seunggi Shin, Byoungyoung Lee
abstract: Generates Bluetooth protocol test sequences using LLMs for security testing of BT implementations.
summary: Generates Bluetooth protocol test sequences using LLMs for security testing of BT implementations.
summary_zh: 使用LLM为蓝牙实现安全测试生成蓝牙协议测试序列。

experiment.key_results: Found 8 bugs in BlueZ and Android BT stack

contributions: Proposes a novel approach for generates Bluetooth protocol test sequences using LLMs for security testing of BT implementations.
Leverages GPT-4 for intelligent test case generation and mutation strategies.
Provides empirical evaluation demonstrating significant improvements over prior approaches.
Demonstrates strong empirical results: Found 8 bugs in BlueZ and Android BT stack.
Addresses crash, spec-violation vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.
contributions_zh: 提出 a novel approach for generates Bluetooth protocol test sequences using LLMs for security testing of BT implementations.
利用 GPT-4 for intelligent test case generation and mutation strategies.
提供了实证评估 demonstrating significant improvements over prior approaches.
证明了 strong empirical results: Found 8 bugs in BlueZ and Android BT stack.
解决 crash, spec-violation vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.
abstract_zh: 使用LLM为蓝牙实现安全测试生成蓝牙协议测试序列。
research_questions: Can LLMs generate valid Bluetooth protocol test sequences for security testing of BT implementations?
What types of Bluetooth vulnerabilities (crashes, spec violations) can LLM-guided fuzzing discover?
How effective is LLM-generated protocol fuzzing against real-world Bluetooth stacks like BlueZ and Android?
research_questions_zh: 大语言模型能否生成有效的蓝牙协议测试序列以进行BT实现的安全测试？
LLM引导的模糊测试能发现哪些类型的蓝牙漏洞（崩溃、规范违反）？
